```{r}
  if (length(imp_vals) == 0) next

  obs_mean  <- mean(obs_vals);   obs_sd  <- sd(obs_vals)
  imp_mean  <- mean(imp_vals);   imp_sd  <- sd(imp_vals)
  pooled_sd <- sqrt((obs_sd^2 + imp_sd^2) / 2)
  smd       <- if (pooled_sd > 0) (imp_mean - obs_mean) / pooled_sd else 0

  quality_df <- rbind(quality_df, data.frame(
    variable  = var,
    n_missing = sum(orig_na),
    obs_mean  = round(obs_mean, 3),
    obs_sd    = round(obs_sd,   3),
    imp_mean  = round(imp_mean, 3),
    imp_sd    = round(imp_sd,   3),
    mean_diff = round(imp_mean - obs_mean, 3),
    sd_ratio  = round(imp_sd / obs_sd,     3),
    abs_smd   = round(abs(smd),            3),
    flag      = if (abs(smd) > 0.5) "⚠️ REVIEW" else "✓"
  ))
}

if (nrow(quality_df) > 0) {
  flagged <- quality_df[quality_df$flag == "⚠️ REVIEW", ]
  ok      <- quality_df[quality_df$flag == "✓", ]

  cat(sprintf("  Variables assessed: %d\n", nrow(quality_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ Acceptable (|SMD| ≤ 0.5): %d\n", nrow(ok)))
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  Review (|SMD| > 0.5):    %d\n\n", nrow(flagged)))

  if (nrow(flagged) > 0) {
    cat("  FLAGGED VARIABLES:\n")
    print(flagged[, c("variable","n_missing","obs_mean","imp_mean",
                      "obs_sd","imp_sd","abs_smd")],
          row.names = FALSE, digits = 3)
    cat("\n")
  }

  cat("  SUMMARY STATISTICS:\n")
  cat(sprintf("    Median |SMD|:    %.3f\n", median(quality_df$abs_smd)))
  cat(sprintf("    Max |SMD|:       %.3f\n", max(quality_df$abs_smd)))
  cat(sprintf("    Median SD ratio: %.3f\n", median(quality_df$sd_ratio)))
  cat(sprintf("    Max SD ratio:    %.3f\n\n", max(quality_df$sd_ratio)))

  write.csv(quality_df, "imputation_quality_assessment.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("  ✓ Saved: imputation_quality_assessment.csv\n\n")
}

# ── D4. Between-imputation variance (RIV) ─────────────────────────────────────
cat("D4. Between-imputation variance stability\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  PURPOSE: Rubin's rules decompose variance into within (W) and\n")
cat("           between (B) components. RIV = B/W. If B >> W for many\n")
cat("           variables, m may be too small or chains have not mixed.\n")
cat("  NOTE: m=24 selected for CV(SE)=5% (Von Hippel, 2020).\n\n")

riv_df <- data.frame()

for (var in vars_for_qc[vars_for_qc %in% names(df_imp)]) {
  orig_na <- is.na(df_imp[[var]])
  if (sum(orig_na) < 5) next

  imp_means <- sapply(all_imp_list,
                      function(d) mean(d[[var]][orig_na], na.rm = TRUE))
  imp_vars  <- sapply(all_imp_list,
                      function(d) var(d[[var]][orig_na],  na.rm = TRUE))

  W   <- mean(imp_vars)
  B   <- var(imp_means)
  RIV <- if (W > 0) B / W else NA_real_

  riv_df <- rbind(riv_df, data.frame(
    variable = var,
    W        = round(W,   4),
    B        = round(B,   4),
    RIV      = round(RIV, 4),
    flag     = if (!is.na(RIV) && RIV > 1.0) "⚠️ HIGH B" else "✓"
  ))
}

if (nrow(riv_df) > 0) {
  flagged_riv <- riv_df[!is.na(riv_df$flag) & riv_df$flag == "⚠️ HIGH B", ]
  cat(sprintf("  Variables assessed: %d\n", nrow(riv_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ Stable (RIV ≤ 1.0): %d\n",
              nrow(riv_df) - nrow(flagged_riv)))
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  High B/W (RIV > 1): %d\n", nrow(flagged_riv)))
  cat(sprintf("  Median RIV: %.4f\n", median(riv_df$RIV, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("  Max RIV:    %.4f\n\n", max(riv_df$RIV, na.rm = TRUE)))

  if (nrow(flagged_riv) > 0) {
    cat("  HIGH RIV VARIABLES (B > W — consider increasing m beyond 24):\n")
    print(flagged_riv %>% arrange(desc(RIV)) %>% head(10),
          row.names = FALSE)
    cat("\n")
  }

  write.csv(riv_df %>% arrange(desc(RIV)),
            "imputation_variance_decomposition.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("  ✓ Saved: imputation_variance_decomposition.csv\n\n")
}

# ── D5. FMI per variable ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("D5. Fraction of Missing Information (FMI) per variable\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  PURPOSE: FMI quantifies how much variance is attributable\n")
cat("           to missingness. High FMI (> 0.5) means estimates\n")
cat("           are heavily influenced by the imputation model.\n\n")

fmi_df <- data.frame()

for (var in vars_for_qc[vars_for_qc %in% names(df_imp)]) {
  orig_na <- is.na(df_imp[[var]])
  if (sum(orig_na) < 5) next

  imp_means <- sapply(all_imp_list,
                      function(d) mean(d[[var]][orig_na], na.rm = TRUE))
  imp_vars  <- sapply(all_imp_list,
                      function(d) var(d[[var]][orig_na],  na.rm = TRUE))

  m     <- length(imp_means)
  W     <- mean(imp_vars)
  B     <- var(imp_means)
  T_var <- W + B + B / m
  FMI   <- if (T_var > 0) (B + B / m) / T_var else NA_real_

  fmi_df <- rbind(fmi_df, data.frame(
    variable    = var,
    pct_missing = round(100 * sum(orig_na) / nrow(df_imp), 1),
    FMI         = round(FMI, 3),
    flag        = if (!is.na(FMI) && FMI > 0.5) "⚠️ HIGH" else "✓"
  ))
}

if (nrow(fmi_df) > 0) {
  high_fmi <- fmi_df[!is.na(fmi_df$flag) & fmi_df$flag == "⚠️ HIGH", ]
  cat(sprintf("  Variables assessed: %d\n", nrow(fmi_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  Median FMI: %.3f\n", median(fmi_df$FMI, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("  Max FMI:    %.3f\n", max(fmi_df$FMI, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("  High FMI (> 0.5): %d variables\n\n", nrow(high_fmi)))

  cat("  TOP 15 VARIABLES BY FMI:\n")
  print(fmi_df %>% arrange(desc(FMI)) %>% head(15) %>%
          select(variable, pct_missing, FMI, flag),
        row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\n")

  write.csv(fmi_df %>% arrange(desc(FMI)),
            "imputation_fmi_per_variable.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("  ✓ Saved: imputation_fmi_per_variable.csv\n\n")
}

# ── D6. Categorical distribution preservation ─────────────────────────────────
cat("D6. Categorical distribution preservation\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  PURPOSE: For ordered variables (polr) and the nominal polr\n")
cat("           variable (stop_wdl_exp_combined), check that imputed\n")
cat("           category proportions are plausible relative to observed.\n")
cat("  Variables assessed: education, income, employment, driving_freq,\n")
cat("    gen_health, fall2, alc_use, sleep_aids items,\n")
cat("    stop_wdl_exp_combined\n")
cat("  ACTION: If max_prop_diff > 0.20 for any variable, consider\n")
cat("    switching that variable from polr to PMM (see Van Buuren\n")
cat("    2018 §6.3 — PMM preferred when ordinal regression produces\n")
cat("    implausible distributions with sparse missingness).\n\n")

# All polr variables (ordered + polr_unordered)
ordered_imp_vars <- names(imp_params$method[
  imp_params$method == "polr" &
    names(imp_params$method) %in% c(
      imp_params$variable_types$ordered,
      imp_params$variable_types$polr_unordered
    )
])
ordered_imp_vars <- ordered_imp_vars[
  ordered_imp_vars %in% names(df_imp) &
    sapply(df_imp[ordered_imp_vars], function(x) sum(is.na(x))) > 0
]

cat_check_df <- data.frame()

for (var in ordered_imp_vars) {
  orig_na  <- is.na(df_imp[[var]])
  obs_vals <- df_imp[[var]][!orig_na]
  if (length(obs_vals) == 0) next

  imp_vals <- unlist(lapply(all_imp_list, function(d) d[[var]][orig_na]))

  obs_props <- prop.table(table(obs_vals))
  imp_props <- prop.table(table(imp_vals))
  all_levs  <- union(names(obs_props), names(imp_props))

  max_diff <- max(abs(
    sapply(all_levs, function(lv)
      (if (lv %in% names(imp_props)) imp_props[lv] else 0) -
        (if (lv %in% names(obs_props)) obs_props[lv] else 0))
  ))

  cat_check_df <- rbind(cat_check_df, data.frame(
    variable      = var,
    n_missing     = sum(orig_na),
    n_levels      = length(all_levs),
    max_prop_diff = round(max_diff, 3),
    flag          = if (max_diff > 0.2) "⚠️ REVIEW" else "✓"
  ))
}

if (nrow(cat_check_df) > 0) {
  flagged_cat <- cat_check_df[cat_check_df$flag == "⚠️ REVIEW", ]
  cat(sprintf("  Polr variables assessed: %d\n", nrow(cat_check_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ Proportions stable (max diff ≤ 0.20): %d\n",
              nrow(cat_check_df) - nrow(flagged_cat)))
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  Large proportion shift (> 0.20):      %d\n\n",
              nrow(flagged_cat)))
  if (nrow(flagged_cat) > 0) {
    cat("  FLAGGED VARIABLES (consider switching to PMM):\n")
    print(flagged_cat, row.names = FALSE)
    cat("\n")
  }
  write.csv(cat_check_df, "imputation_categorical_check.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("  ✓ Saved: imputation_categorical_check.csv\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  No polr variables with missingness to check\n\n")
}

# ── D7. Intra-scale correlation preservation ──────────────────────────────────
cat("D7. Intra-scale correlation preservation\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  PURPOSE: Items within the same scale should remain highly\n")
cat("           correlated after imputation. A large r drop (> 0.1)\n")
cat("           suggests the imputation broke the latent structure.\n")
cat("  SCALES: CISS, SURPS (with reversed items), DBAS, BFI\n\n")

if (!is.null(analysis_vars)) {
  scale_pairs <- list()
  if (!is.null(analysis_vars$ciss)  && length(analysis_vars$ciss)  > 0)
    scale_pairs[["CISS"]]  <- analysis_vars$ciss
  if (!is.null(analysis_vars$surps) && length(analysis_vars$surps) > 0)
    scale_pairs[["SURPS"]] <- analysis_vars$surps
  if (!is.null(analysis_vars$dbas)  && length(analysis_vars$dbas)  > 0)
    scale_pairs[["DBAS"]]  <- analysis_vars$dbas
  if (!is.null(analysis_vars$bfi)   && length(analysis_vars$bfi)   > 0)
    scale_pairs[["BFI"]]   <- analysis_vars$bfi
  cat("  Scale items sourced from analysis_variable_lists.rds\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  Falling back to hardcoded item names — verify these match\n")
  cat("      current variable names in df_imp before trusting D7 output.\n\n")
  scale_pairs <- list(
    CISS  = paste0("ciss", 1:21),
    SURPS = c("surps1_r","surps2","surps3","surps4_r","surps5","surps6",
              "surps7_r","surps8","surps9","surps10","surps11","surps12",
              "surps13_r","surps14","surps15","surps16","surps17","surps18",
              "surps19","surps20_r","surps21","surps22","surps23_r"),
    DBAS  = c("dbas1", paste0("dbas_", 2:16)),
    BFI   = c("reserved_r","trusting","lazy_r","relaxed_r","few_interests_r",
              "outgoing","find_fault_r","thorough","nervous","imagination")
  )
}

cor_check_df <- data.frame()

for (scale_name in names(scale_pairs)) {
  items <- scale_pairs[[scale_name]]
  items <- items[items %in% names(df_imp)]
  if (length(items) < 3) {
    cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %s: fewer than 3 items found in df_imp — skipping\n",
                scale_name))
    next
  }

  obs_cc <- df_imp[complete.cases(df_imp[, items]), items]
  if (nrow(obs_cc) < 30) next
  obs_cor_mat <- cor(obs_cc, use = "complete.obs")
  obs_cor     <- mean(abs(obs_cor_mat[upper.tri(obs_cor_mat)]))

  imp_cors <- sapply(all_imp_list, function(d) {
    c_mat <- cor(d[, items], use = "complete.obs")
    mean(abs(c_mat[upper.tri(c_mat)]))
  })
  imp_cor  <- mean(imp_cors)
  cor_drop <- obs_cor - imp_cor

  cor_check_df <- rbind(cor_check_df, data.frame(
    scale      = scale_name,
    n_items    = length(items),
    obs_mean_r = round(obs_cor,  3),
    imp_mean_r = round(imp_cor,  3),
    r_drop     = round(cor_drop, 3),
    flag       = if (cor_drop > 0.1) "⚠️ DROP" else "✓"
  ))
}

if (nrow(cor_check_df) > 0) {
  cat("  Scale-level correlation summary:\n")
  print(cor_check_df, row.names = FALSE)
  flagged_cor <- cor_check_df[cor_check_df$flag == "⚠️ DROP", ]
  if (nrow(flagged_cor) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("\n  ⚠️  %d scale(s) show r drop > 0.1 — review minpuc setting\n",
                nrow(flagged_cor)))
  } else {
    cat("\n  ✓ Within-scale correlations preserved across all scales\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
  write.csv(cor_check_df, "imputation_scale_correlation_check.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)
  cat("  ✓ Saved: imputation_scale_correlation_check.csv\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  No scales had sufficient items/observations to assess\n\n")
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART E: RECALCULATE DERIVED VARIABLES
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART E: RECALCULATING DERIVED VARIABLES\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Recalculating for all", imp_params$optimal_m, "imputed datasets...\n\n")

# ── E1. n_health_conditions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("E1. n_health_conditions\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Formula: n_physical_conditions + n_ment_conditions + n_sleep_conditions\n")
cat("  Removed from imputation in Chunk 7 Part B4 to avoid circularity;\n")
cat("  each component imputed separately and summed here.\n\n")

for (i in seq_along(all_imp_list)) {
  d <- all_imp_list[[i]]
  if (all(c("n_physical_conditions","n_ment_conditions","n_sleep_conditions")
          %in% names(d))) {
    all_imp_list[[i]]$n_health_conditions <-
      d$n_physical_conditions + d$n_ment_conditions + d$n_sleep_conditions
  }
}

d1        <- all_imp_list[[1]]
n_correct <- sum(
  d1$n_health_conditions ==
    (d1$n_physical_conditions + d1$n_ment_conditions + d1$n_sleep_conditions),
  na.rm = TRUE)

if (n_correct == nrow(d1)) {
  cat("  ✓ n_health_conditions correct in all", nrow(d1),
      "rows (imputation 1)\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  Mismatch in", nrow(d1) - n_correct, "rows — investigate\n\n")
}

# ── E2. bzra_weekly_dose_eq ───────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("E2. bzra_weekly_dose_eq\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Formula: bzra_daily_dose_eq × 7\n")
cat("  Excluded from mice() (method = '') to preserve the mathematical\n")
cat("  relationship with the daily equivalent. NAs from confirmed users\n")
cat("  with missing dose/frequency data are filled here using imputed\n")
cat("  daily values.\n\n")
cat("  IMPORTANT: This recalculation applies to all_imp_list only.\n")
cat("  mice_imputation_object.rds retains the original NAs for\n")
cat("  bzra_weekly_dose_eq. Always use imputed_datasets_final.rds\n")
cat("  for any analysis involving this variable.\n\n")

for (i in seq_along(all_imp_list)) {
  d <- all_imp_list[[i]]
  if ("bzra_daily_dose_eq" %in% names(d)) {
    all_imp_list[[i]]$bzra_weekly_dose_eq <- d$bzra_daily_dose_eq * 7
  }
}

n_na_weekly <- sum(is.na(all_imp_list[[1]]$bzra_weekly_dose_eq))
n_na_daily  <- sum(is.na(all_imp_list[[1]]$bzra_daily_dose_eq))

if (n_na_weekly == 0) {
  cat("  ✓ bzra_weekly_dose_eq: no NAs remaining\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %d NAs remaining in bzra_weekly_dose_eq\n", n_na_weekly))
}
if (n_na_daily == 0) {
  cat("  ✓ bzra_daily_dose_eq: no NAs remaining (correctly imputed)\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %d NAs remaining in bzra_daily_dose_eq\n", n_na_daily))
}
cat("\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART F: ADD OUTCOME VARIABLE BACK
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART F: ADDING OUTCOME VARIABLE BACK\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("  Outcome (freq_dich) was excluded from the imputation dataset in\n")
cat("  Chunk 7 to avoid conditioning imputed predictor values on the\n")
cat("  dependent variable. It is re-attached here from SIMOA_analysis.rds.\n\n")

if (file.exists("SIMOA_analysis.rds")) {
  SIMOA_original <- readRDS("SIMOA_analysis.rds")

  outcome_var <- imp_params$outcome
  if (is.null(outcome_var)) outcome_var <- "freq_dich"

  if (outcome_var %in% names(SIMOA_original)) {
    outcome_data <- SIMOA_original[[outcome_var]]

    for (i in seq_along(all_imp_list))
      all_imp_list[[i]][[outcome_var]] <- outcome_data

    cat("✓ Outcome variable added:", outcome_var, "\n")
    cat("  Distribution (0/1=stoppers, 2=continuers):\n")
    print(table(all_imp_list[[1]][[outcome_var]], useNA = "ifany"))
    cat("\n")
  } else {
    cat("⚠️  Outcome variable", outcome_var,
        "not found in original dataset\n\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("⚠️  SIMOA_analysis.rds not found — outcome not added\n\n")
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART G: SAVE OUTPUTS
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART G: SAVING OUTPUTS\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("TWO OBJECTS SAVED — USE GUIDANCE:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  mice_imputation_object.rds  — raw mids object from mice()\n")
cat("    • bzra_weekly_dose_eq: original NAs retained\n")
cat("    • n_health_conditions: not present (removed in Chunk 7 Part B4)\n")
cat("    • freq_dich: not present (excluded from imputation)\n")
cat("    • Only use for mice::pool() on directly imputed variables\n\n")
cat("  imputed_datasets_final.rds  — list of m=24 complete data frames\n")
cat("    • All derived vars recalculated (Part E)\n")
cat("    • Outcome (freq_dich) added back (Part F)\n")
cat("    • PRIMARY object for Chunk 9 onward\n")
cat("    • To use mice::pool() in Chunk 9: convert via mice::as.mids()\n\n")

saveRDS(imp_final,    "mice_imputation_object.rds")
saveRDS(all_imp_list, "imputed_datasets_final.rds")

cat("✓ Saved: mice_imputation_object.rds\n")
cat("✓ Saved: imputed_datasets_final.rds\n")
cat("  (List of", length(all_imp_list),
    "complete datasets — PRIMARY object for Chunk 9)\n\n")

cat("VARIABLES IN EACH IMPUTED DATASET:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat(sprintf("  Total variables: %d\n",   ncol(all_imp_list[[1]])))
cat(sprintf("  Observations:    %d\n",   nrow(all_imp_list[[1]])))
cat(sprintf("  Includes: freq_dich, all imputed vars,\n"))
cat(sprintf("            bzra_weekly_dose_eq (recalculated),\n"))
cat(sprintf("            n_health_conditions (recalculated)\n\n"))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART H: FINAL SUMMARY
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("IMPUTATION COMPLETE — SUMMARY\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("IMPUTATION SPECIFICATIONS:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Observations:         ", nrow(df_imp), "\n")
cat("  Variables imputed:    ", imp_params$n_vars_to_impute, "\n")
cat("  m (imputations):      ", imp_params$optimal_m,
    "(Von Hippel, 2020; CV(SE)=5%)\n")
cat("  maxit (iterations):   ", imp_params$optimal_maxit,
    "(Gelman-Rubin PSRF convergence experiment)\n")
cat("  Ridge penalty:        ", imp_params$ridge_penalty,
    "(van Buuren §6.4.2)\n")
cat("  Avg predictors/model:  22.3 (mincor=0.07, minpuc=0.10)\n")
cat("  Time elapsed:         ",
    round(as.numeric(elapsed_time), 2), "minutes\n\n")

cat("IMPUTATION METHODS:\n")
cat(sprintf("  PMM (continuous + Likert/COM-B + personality items): %d\n",
            sum(imp_params$method == "pmm")))
cat(sprintf("  Logreg (binary):                                      %d\n",
            sum(imp_params$method == "logreg")))
cat(sprintf("  Polr (ordered + nominal/unordered):                   %d\n",
            sum(imp_params$method == "polr")))
cat(sprintf("    Ordered:  education, income, employment, driving_freq,\n"))
cat(sprintf("              gen_health, fall2, alc_use, sleep_aids items\n"))
cat(sprintf("    Nominal (unordered storage): stop_wdl_exp_combined\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Polyreg (unordered categorical):                      %d\n",
            sum(imp_params$method == "polyreg")))
cat(sprintf("  Not imputed (derived/complete/outcome):               %d\n\n",
            sum(imp_params$method == "")))

cat("STATISTICAL VERIFICATION SUMMARY:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

if (!is.null(psrf_final)) {
  psrf_clean2 <- psrf_final[!is.na(psrf_final) & is.finite(psrf_final)]
  conv_status <- if (max(psrf_clean2) < 1.1) "✓ PASS" else "⚠️  FAIL"
  cat(sprintf("  B1. Convergence (PSRF):          %s  max=%.4f mean=%.4f\n",
              conv_status, max(psrf_clean2), mean(psrf_clean2)))
}

comp_status <- if (length(completeness_fails) == 0) "✓ PASS" else "⚠️  FAIL"
cat(sprintf("  D1. Completeness:                %s  (%d vars × %d datasets)\n",
            comp_status, length(imp_vars), imp_params$optimal_m))

range_status <- if (length(range_fails) == 0) "✓ PASS" else "⚠️  REVIEW"
cat(sprintf("  D2. PMM range preservation:      %s  (%d PMM vars)\n",
            range_status, length(pmm_vars)))

if (nrow(quality_df) > 0) {
  smd_status <- if (sum(quality_df$flag == "⚠️ REVIEW") == 0) "✓ PASS" else
    sprintf("⚠️  %d vars flagged", sum(quality_df$flag == "⚠️ REVIEW"))
  cat(sprintf("  D3. Mean/SD (|SMD| ≤ 0.5):       %s\n", smd_status))
}

if (nrow(riv_df) > 0) {
  riv_status <- if (sum(riv_df$flag == "⚠️ HIGH B", na.rm = TRUE) == 0)
    "✓ PASS" else
    sprintf("⚠️  %d vars flagged",
            sum(riv_df$flag == "⚠️ HIGH B", na.rm = TRUE))
  cat(sprintf("  D4. Between-imputation variance: %s\n", riv_status))
}

if (nrow(fmi_df) > 0)
  cat(sprintf("  D5. FMI (median / max):          %.3f / %.3f\n",
              median(fmi_df$FMI, na.rm = TRUE),
              max(fmi_df$FMI, na.rm = TRUE)))

if (nrow(cat_check_df) > 0) {
  cat_status <- if (nrow(flagged_cat) == 0) "✓ PASS" else
    sprintf("⚠️  %d vars flagged (consider polr → PMM)", nrow(flagged_cat))
  cat(sprintf("  D6. Categorical proportions:     %s\n", cat_status))
}

if (nrow(cor_check_df) > 0) {
  cor_status <- if (nrow(cor_check_df[cor_check_df$flag == "⚠️ DROP", ]) == 0)
    "✓ PASS" else "⚠️  REVIEW"
  cat(sprintf("  D7. Intra-scale correlations:    %s  (CISS, SURPS, DBAS, BFI)\n",
              cor_status))
}

cat("\n")
cat("LOGGED EVENTS:\n")
if (n_logged == 0) {
  cat("  ✓ None — imputation completed cleanly\n\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %d events — review imputation_logged_events.csv\n\n",
              n_logged))
}

cat("KEY CITATIONS:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Von Hippel (2020) Sociol Methods Res 49(3):699-718  [m=24]\n")
cat("  White et al. (2011) Stat Med 30(4):377-399          [m floor]\n")
cat("  Gelman & Rubin (1992) Stat Sci 7(4):457-472         [maxit]\n")
cat("  Brooks & Gelman (1998) J Comp Graph Stat 7(4):434   [maxit]\n")
cat("  Van Buuren (2018) Flexible Imputation (2nd ed.)      [both]\n\n")

cat("OUTPUT OBJECTS:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  PRIMARY:   imputed_datasets_final.rds\n")
cat("             → m=24 complete datasets; all derived vars correct\n")
cat("             → freq_dich included; use for all Chunk 9 analyses\n")
cat("  SECONDARY: mice_imputation_object.rds\n")
cat("             → Raw mids object; bzra_weekly NAs unfilled\n")
cat("             → Only use for directly imputed variables\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1.  mice_imputation_object.rds\n")
cat("  2.  imputed_datasets_final.rds\n")
cat("  3.  convergence_trace_plots_final.pdf\n")
cat("  4.  imputation_density_plots.pdf\n")
cat("  5.  imputation_stripplots.pdf\n")
cat("  6.  final_imputation_psrf.csv\n")
cat("  7.  imputation_quality_assessment.csv\n")
cat("  8.  imputation_variance_decomposition.csv\n")
cat("  9.  imputation_fmi_per_variable.csv\n")
cat("  10. imputation_categorical_check.csv\n")
cat("  11. imputation_scale_correlation_check.csv\n")
if (n_logged > 0)
  cat("  12. imputation_logged_events.csv\n")

cat("\n")
cat("NEXT STEPS:\n")
cat("  1. Review convergence_trace_plots_final.pdf\n")
cat("     → Confirm chains intermingle freely (no trends or drift)\n")
cat("  2. Review imputation_density_plots.pdf\n")
cat("     → Red (observed) and blue (imputed) should broadly overlap\n")
cat("  3. Check imputation_quality_assessment.csv\n")
cat("     → Flag any |SMD| > 0.5 for manuscript reporting\n")
cat("  4. Check imputation_categorical_check.csv\n")
cat("     → max_prop_diff > 0.20 for any polr variable → switch to PMM\n")
cat("  5. Check imputation_scale_correlation_check.csv\n")
cat("     → Confirm r drop < 0.1 for CISS, SURPS, DBAS, BFI\n")
cat("  6. If all checks pass → Proceed to Chunk 9\n")
cat("     (composites + logistic regression)\n\n")

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("✓ MULTIPLE IMPUTATION COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
```




## Diagnostics
```{r}
# ── INCOME DEEP DIAGNOSTIC (corrected for factor income) ─────────────────────

imp_params <- readRDS("imputation_parameters_optimized.rds")
df_imp     <- readRDS("df_imputation_ready.rds")

cat("Income class:", class(df_imp$income), "\n")
cat("Income values (first 20):\n")
print(head(df_imp$income, 20))
cat("\n")

# Convert to numeric for correlation (factor levels → integers)
obs_income_num <- as.numeric(as.character(
  df_imp$income[!is.na(df_imp$income)]))
cat("Income as numeric — unique values:", sort(unique(obs_income_num)), "\n\n")

cat("INCOME PREDICTOR MATRIX — active predictors:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
income_preds <- imp_params$predictor_matrix["income", ]
active_preds <- names(income_preds[income_preds != 0])
cat(sprintf("Total active predictors for income: %d\n\n", length(active_preds)))

cat("CORRELATIONS WITH INCOME (observed data, n=", length(obs_income_num), "):\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

cor_results <- data.frame()
for (p in active_preds) {
  if (!p %in% names(df_imp)) next
  pred_vals <- df_imp[[p]][!is.na(df_imp$income)]
  # Convert factors/ordered to numeric
  if (is.factor(pred_vals) || is.ordered(pred_vals))
    pred_vals <- as.numeric(pred_vals)
  if (!is.numeric(pred_vals)) next
  if (length(unique(pred_vals[!is.na(pred_vals)])) < 2) next
  if (var(pred_vals, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) next
  r <- cor(obs_income_num, pred_vals, use = "complete.obs")
  cor_results <- rbind(cor_results, data.frame(
    predictor = p,
    r         = round(r, 3),
    abs_r     = round(abs(r), 3)
  ))
}

cor_results <- cor_results[order(-cor_results$abs_r), ]
cat("Top 20 predictors by |r| with income:\n")
print(head(cor_results, 20), row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n\nMISSING INCOME CASES — profile vs observed:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
miss_inc <- is.na(df_imp$income)
cat(sprintf("N missing: %d\n\n", sum(miss_inc)))

check_vars <- c("age", "sex", "education", "employment",
                "phq2_score", "osss_3_score", "med_quant")
check_vars <- check_vars[check_vars %in% names(df_imp)]

for (v in check_vars) {
  vals <- df_imp[[v]]
  if (is.factor(vals) || is.ordered(vals)) vals <- as.numeric(vals)
  if (!is.numeric(vals)) next
  obs_mean  <- mean(vals[!miss_inc], na.rm = TRUE)
  miss_mean <- mean(vals[miss_inc],  na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("  %-20s: observed mean=%.2f | missing mean=%.2f | diff=%+.2f\n",
              v, obs_mean, miss_mean, miss_mean - obs_mean))
}

cat("\n\nMISSING INCOME CASES — raw values:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
show_vars <- c("age", "sex", "education", "employment", "prov_terr")
show_vars <- show_vars[show_vars %in% names(df_imp)]
print(df_imp[miss_inc, show_vars])
```




## Post-Imputation
```{r}
#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 9: POST-IMPUTATION PROCESSING
#==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   1. Create validated personality subscales using already-reversed items
#   2. Create composite variables (deferred from Chunk 5+6)
#   3. Create binary outcome variable (stopped_bzra) from freq_dich
#   4. Calculate reliability metrics
#   All operations performed on EACH imputed dataset separately
#
# NOTE ON DATA SOURCE:
#   Loads imputed_datasets_final.rds (list of m=24 complete data frames
#   from Chunk 8).
#   n_health_conditions was recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E as:
#     n_physical_conditions + n_ment_conditions + n_sleep_conditions
#   bzra_weekly_dose_eq was recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E as:
#     bzra_daily_dose_eq × 7
#   freq_dich (outcome) was added back in Chunk 8 Part F.
#   None of the above requires re-derivation here.
#
# NOTE ON REVERSED ITEMS:
#   All reversals were performed in Chunk 6 and are present in the imputed
#   data under their _r names:
#     BFI:   reserved_r, lazy_r, relaxed_r, few_interests_r, find_fault_r
#     SURPS: surps1_r, surps4_r, surps7_r, surps13_r, surps20_r, surps23_r
#   Subscale definitions below use these _r names directly.
#
# NOTE ON DBAS NAMING:
#   First item has no underscore: dbas1
#   Remaining items use underscore: dbas_2 through dbas_16
#
# NOTE ON COMPOSITE VARIABLES (deferred from Chunk 5+6):
#   benefitsC     = mean(benefit_1, benefit_4, benefit_5, benefit_6)
#   side_effectsC = mean(side_effects_1:4)
#   adlsC         = mean(adls_1, adls_2)
#   dependenceC   = mean(dependence_1:3)
#   motivationC   = mean(motivation_1:3)
#   capabilityC   = mean(capability_1, 6-capability)
#   pharmacistC   = mean(desires_2, 6-inf_hcp_3)
#   prescriberC   = mean(desires_1, 6-inf_hcp_1)
#
# NOTE ON OUTCOME:
#   freq_dich original coding:
#     0 = No longer uses BZRAs
#     1 = Uses BZRAs < 1 time per month
#     2 = Uses BZRAs > 1 time per month (regular use)
#   freq_dich was excluded from imputation in Chunk 7 and added back in
#   Chunk 8 Part F. It must not be modified here.
#
#   Binary outcome created in Part B3:
#     stopped_bzra = 1 → Stoppers/rare users (freq_dich 0 or 1)
#     stopped_bzra = 0 → Continuers          (freq_dich 2)
#   freq_dich is retained for sensitivity analyses.
#
# NOTE ON IMPUTATION METHODS (from Chunk 8):
#   PMM:     All continuous variables + all Likert/COM-B items +
#            personality scale items (DBAS, BFI, SURPS, CISS)
#   Logreg:  sex
#   Polr:    education, income, employment, driving_freq, gen_health,
#            fall2, alc_use, sleep_aids items
#   Polyreg: prov_terr
#   Polyreg: stop_wdl_exp_combined (nominal — 3-level UNORDERED factor
#            collapsed in Chunk 5+6 Part B:
#              "No withdrawal" | "Insomnia only" |
#              "Other withdrawal symptoms"
#            Dummy-coded in regression. NOT imputed with Polr because
#            categories are not ordered by severity.)
#   Not imputed: bzra_weekly_dose_eq (recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E),
#                n_health_conditions (recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E),
#                freq_dich (outcome — added in Chunk 8 Part F)
#==============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(psych)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("POST-IMPUTATION PROCESSING\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

# Load imputed datasets (list of complete data frames from Chunk 8)
if (!file.exists("imputed_datasets_final.rds")) {
  stop("ERROR: imputed_datasets_final.rds not found. Please run Chunk 8 first.")
}

all_imputed <- readRDS("imputed_datasets_final.rds")
m           <- length(all_imputed)

cat("✓ Loaded imputed_datasets_final.rds\n")
cat("  Imputations:", m, "\n")
cat("  Variables per dataset:", ncol(all_imputed[[1]]), "\n")
cat("  Observations:", nrow(all_imputed[[1]]), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION: Reversed items from Chunk 6
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("VERIFICATION: Checking reversed items from Chunk 6\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

reversed_items_expected <- c(
  "reserved_r", "lazy_r", "relaxed_r", "few_interests_r", "find_fault_r",
  "surps1_r", "surps4_r", "surps7_r", "surps13_r", "surps20_r", "surps23_r"
)

present <- reversed_items_expected[
  reversed_items_expected %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])]
absent  <- reversed_items_expected[
  !reversed_items_expected %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])]

cat(sprintf("  ✓ Reversed items present: %d / %d\n",
            length(present), length(reversed_items_expected)))
if (length(absent) > 0) {
  cat("  ✗ MISSING reversed items:\n")
  for (v in absent) cat("    •", v, "\n")
  stop("Cannot proceed — reversed items missing from imputed data.")
}
cat("\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION: Chunk 8 carry-through variables
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("VERIFICATION: Checking Chunk 8 carry-through variables\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

# n_health_conditions — recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E
if ("n_health_conditions" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  cat("  ✓ n_health_conditions present (recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E)\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  n_health_conditions NOT present — check Chunk 8 Part E output\n")
}

# bzra_weekly_dose_eq — recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E as daily × 7
if ("bzra_weekly_dose_eq" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  n_na_weekly <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]]$bzra_weekly_dose_eq))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ bzra_weekly_dose_eq present (recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E) | NAs: %d\n",
              n_na_weekly))
  if (n_na_weekly > 0)
    cat("  ⚠️  Unexpected NAs — check Chunk 8 Part E bzra recalculation\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  bzra_weekly_dose_eq NOT present — check Chunk 8 Part E output\n")
}

# freq_dich — outcome variable, added back in Chunk 8 Part F
if ("freq_dich" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  cat("  ✓ freq_dich present (outcome — added in Chunk 8 Part F)\n")
  cat("    Distribution:\n")
  cat("      0 = No longer uses:     n =",
      sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("      1 = < 1 time per month: n =",
      sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("      2 = > 1 time per month: n =",
      sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  freq_dich NOT present — check Chunk 8 Part F output\n")
}
cat("\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION: Polr-imputed categorical variables from Chunk 8
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("VERIFICATION: Checking polr/polyreg-imputed variables from Chunk 8\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Polr:    education, income, employment, driving_freq, gen_health,\n")
cat("           fall2, alc_use, sleep_aids items\n")
cat("  Polyreg: prov_terr, stop_wdl_exp_combined\n")
cat("           (stop_wdl_exp_combined is a 3-level UNORDERED factor —\n")
cat("            'No withdrawal' | 'Insomnia only' |\n")
cat("            'Other withdrawal symptoms')\n\n")

polr_vars_to_check <- c(
  "education", "income", "employment", "driving_freq", "gen_health",
  "fall2", "alc_use", "stop_wdl_exp_combined", "prov_terr"
)

for (v in polr_vars_to_check) {
  if (v %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]][[v]]))
    cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-30s | NAs in imputation 1: %d\n", v, n_na))
    if (n_na > 0)
      cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  Unexpected NAs in %s — check Chunk 8 output\n", v))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %-30s NOT present — check Chunk 8 output\n", v))
  }
}

# sleep_aids variables (dynamic — check any that survived into imputed data)
sleep_aid_vars <- grep("^sleep_aid", names(all_imputed[[1]]), value = TRUE)
if (length(sleep_aid_vars) > 0) {
  for (v in sleep_aid_vars) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]][[v]]))
    cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-30s | NAs in imputation 1: %d\n", v, n_na))
  }
} else {
  cat("  ℹ️  No sleep_aid* variables detected in imputed data\n")
}
cat("\n")

# =============================================================================
# SUBSCALE DEFINITIONS
# =============================================================================
# Each entry: list(items = character vector, reversed = which _r names)
# All items listed here are the FINAL variable names as they exist in
# the imputed data. Reversed items use their _r suffix from Chunk 6.
# =============================================================================

subscale_defs <- list(

  # ── BFI-10 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # 2 items per trait. Reversed items stored as _r (Chunk 6).
  BFI_Extraversion      = list(items    = c("reserved_r", "outgoing"),
                                reversed = "reserved_r"),
  BFI_Agreeableness     = list(items    = c("find_fault_r", "trusting"),
                                reversed = "find_fault_r"),
  BFI_Conscientiousness = list(items    = c("lazy_r", "thorough"),
                                reversed = "lazy_r"),
  BFI_Neuroticism       = list(items    = c("relaxed_r", "nervous"),
                                reversed = "relaxed_r"),
  BFI_Openness          = list(items    = c("few_interests_r", "imagination"),
                                reversed = "few_interests_r"),

  # ── SURPS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Hopelessness items 1,4,7,13,20,23 were reversed in Chunk 6 → _r names.
  # Item 17 was NOT reversed — kept as surps17.
  SURPS_Hopelessness        = list(
    items    = c("surps1_r", "surps4_r", "surps7_r", "surps13_r",
                 "surps17", "surps20_r", "surps23_r"),
    reversed = c("surps1_r", "surps4_r", "surps7_r", "surps13_r",
                 "surps20_r", "surps23_r")),
  SURPS_Impulsivity         = list(
    items    = paste0("surps", c(2, 5, 11, 15, 22)),
    reversed = character(0)),
  SURPS_Sensation_Seeking   = list(
    items    = paste0("surps", c(3, 6, 9, 12, 16, 19)),
    reversed = character(0)),
  SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity = list(
    items    = paste0("surps", c(8, 10, 14, 18, 21)),
    reversed = character(0)),

  # ── DBAS-16 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # dbas1 has no underscore; dbas_2 through dbas_16 have underscore.
  # No reverse coding for DBAS.
  #
  # Variable mapping derived from Morin et al. second-order CFA (DBAS-30 item
  # numbers in parentheses):
  #   Consequences (7):       dbas_3(5), dbas_4(8), dbas_5(10), dbas_7(12),
  #                           dbas_9(18), dbas_12(21), dbas_16(30)
  #   Worry/Helplessness (4): dbas_8(17), dbas_10(19), dbas_11(20), dbas_14(25)
  #   Expectations (2):       dbas1(1), dbas_2(2)
  #   Medication (3):         dbas_6(11), dbas_13(24), dbas_15(27)
  #
  # NOTE: DBAS_Total is created here for reliability reporting only.
  #   It is excluded from VSURF (Chunk 11B) and descriptive analysis
  #   (Chunk 10) due to collinearity with subscales.
  DBAS_Consequences       = list(
    items    = c("dbas_3", "dbas_4", "dbas_5", "dbas_7",
                 "dbas_9", "dbas_12", "dbas_16"),
    reversed = character(0)),
  DBAS_Worry_Helplessness = list(
    items    = c("dbas_8", "dbas_10", "dbas_11", "dbas_14"),
    reversed = character(0)),
  DBAS_Expectations       = list(
    items    = c("dbas1", "dbas_2"),
    reversed = character(0)),
  DBAS_Medications        = list(
    items    = c("dbas_6", "dbas_13", "dbas_15"),
    reversed = character(0)),
  DBAS_Total              = list(
    items    = c("dbas1", paste0("dbas_", 2:16)),
    reversed = character(0)),

  # ── CISS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # No reverse coding for CISS.
  CISS_Task      = list(items    = paste0("ciss", c(2, 6, 8, 11, 13, 16, 19)),
                         reversed = character(0)),
  CISS_Emotion   = list(items    = paste0("ciss", c(3, 5, 10, 12, 14, 17, 20)),
                         reversed = character(0)),
  CISS_Avoidance = list(items    = paste0("ciss", c(1, 4, 7, 9, 15, 18, 21)),
                         reversed = character(0))
)

# =============================================================================
# HELPER: Reliability assessment
# =============================================================================

assess_reliability <- function(data, items, name) {

  missing_items <- items[!items %in% names(data)]
  if (length(missing_items) > 0)
    return(list(name = name, alpha = NA, alpha_ci_lower = NA,
                alpha_ci_upper = NA, mean_r = NA,
                mean = NA, sd = NA, status = "MISSING ITEMS",
                missing = missing_items))

  item_data <- data[, items, drop = FALSE]
  item_data <- item_data[complete.cases(item_data), ]

  if (nrow(item_data) < 10)
    return(list(name = name, alpha = NA, alpha_ci_lower = NA,
                alpha_ci_upper = NA, mean_r = NA,
                mean = NA, sd = NA, status = "INSUFFICIENT DATA",
                missing = character(0)))

  item_matrix <- as.data.frame(lapply(item_data, as.numeric))

  if (any(sapply(item_matrix, sd, na.rm = TRUE) == 0))
    return(list(name = name, alpha = NA, alpha_ci_lower = NA,
                alpha_ci_upper = NA, mean_r = NA,
                mean = NA, sd = NA, status = "ZERO VARIANCE",
                missing = character(0)))

  alpha_result <- tryCatch(
    psych::alpha(item_matrix, check.keys = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL)

  if (is.null(alpha_result))
    return(list(name = name, alpha = NA, alpha_ci_lower = NA,
                alpha_ci_upper = NA, mean_r = NA,
                mean = NA, sd = NA, status = "ALPHA FAILED",
                missing = character(0)))

  scores  <- rowMeans(item_matrix)
  cor_mat <- cor(item_matrix, use = "complete.obs")

  list(
    name           = name,
    n_items        = length(items),
    alpha          = alpha_result$total$raw_alpha,
    alpha_ci_lower = alpha_result$feldt$lower.ci,
    alpha_ci_upper = alpha_result$feldt$upper.ci,
    mean_r         = mean(cor_mat[lower.tri(cor_mat)], na.rm = TRUE),
    mean           = mean(scores, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd             = sd(scores,   na.rm = TRUE),
    status         = dplyr::case_when(
      alpha_result$total$raw_alpha >= 0.70 ~ "GOOD",
      alpha_result$total$raw_alpha >= 0.60 ~ "ACCEPTABLE",
      TRUE                                 ~ "POOR"),
    missing        = character(0)
  )
}

# =============================================================================
# PART A: VALIDATE ITEM NAMES BEFORE CREATING SUBSCALES
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART A: VALIDATING ITEM NAMES\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

all_item_names <- unique(unlist(lapply(subscale_defs, `[[`, "items")))
missing_items  <- all_item_names[!all_item_names %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])]
present_items  <- all_item_names[ all_item_names %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])]

cat(sprintf("  Total unique items across all subscales: %d\n",
            length(all_item_names)))
cat(sprintf("  ✓ Present in data: %d\n", length(present_items)))

if (length(missing_items) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("  ✗ MISSING from data: %d\n", length(missing_items)))
  for (v in missing_items) cat("    •", v, "\n")
  cat("\n  ⚠️  Missing items will cause those subscales to be skipped.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  ✓ All items present — subscale creation can proceed\n\n")
}

# =============================================================================
# PART B: CREATE SUBSCALES ACROSS ALL IMPUTED DATASETS
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART B: CREATING SUBSCALES\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Scoring method: MEAN of items (interpretable on original scale)\n")
cat("Applied to all", m, "imputed datasets\n\n")

subscales_created <- character(0)
subscales_skipped <- character(0)

all_imputed <- lapply(all_imputed, function(d) {
  for (scale_name in names(subscale_defs)) {
    items <- subscale_defs[[scale_name]]$items
    if (all(items %in% names(d))) {
      item_mat        <- as.data.frame(
        lapply(d[, items, drop = FALSE], as.numeric))
      d[[scale_name]] <- rowMeans(item_mat, na.rm = FALSE)
    }
  }
  d
})

# Determine which subscales were actually created
for (scale_name in names(subscale_defs)) {
  if (scale_name %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
    subscales_created <- c(subscales_created, scale_name)
  } else {
    subscales_skipped <- c(subscales_skipped, scale_name)
  }
}

# Report by scale family
families <- list(
  "BFI-10"  = grep("^BFI_",   subscales_created, value = TRUE),
  "SURPS"   = grep("^SURPS_", subscales_created, value = TRUE),
  "DBAS-16" = grep("^DBAS_",  subscales_created, value = TRUE),
  "CISS"    = grep("^CISS_",  subscales_created, value = TRUE)
)

for (fam in names(families)) {
  cat(sprintf("%s (%d subscales):\n", fam, length(families[[fam]])))
  for (s in families[[fam]]) {
    n_rev    <- length(subscale_defs[[s]]$reversed)
    rev_note <- if (n_rev > 0)
      sprintf(" [%d item(s) reversed in Chunk 6]", n_rev) else ""
    cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-30s  items: %s%s\n",
                s,
                paste(subscale_defs[[s]]$items, collapse = ", "),
                rev_note))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

if (length(subscales_skipped) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("⚠️  SKIPPED (%d) — missing items:\n", length(subscales_skipped)))
  for (s in subscales_skipped) cat("  •", s, "\n")
  cat("\n")
}

cat(sprintf("Total subscales created: %d\n\n", length(subscales_created)))
cat("NOTE: DBAS_Total created here for reliability reporting only.\n")
cat("  It is excluded from Chunk 10 descriptives and Chunk 11B VSURF\n")
cat("  due to collinearity with DBAS subscales.\n\n")

# =============================================================================
# PART B2: COMPOSITE VARIABLES (deferred from Chunk 5+6)
# =============================================================================
# Inline reversals (scale_max + 1 - x):
#   capabilityC:  capability is reversed inline (6 - capability)
#   pharmacistC:  inf_hcp_3 is reversed inline (6 - inf_hcp_3)
#   prescriberC:  inf_hcp_1 is reversed inline (6 - inf_hcp_1)
# All other composites are simple means with no reversal.
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART B2: CREATING COMPOSITE VARIABLES\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Method: mean of items, with inline reversal where needed\n")
cat("  capabilityC:  capability reversed (6 - capability)\n")
cat("  pharmacistC:  inf_hcp_3 reversed (6 - inf_hcp_3)\n")
cat("  prescriberC:  inf_hcp_1 reversed (6 - inf_hcp_1)\n\n")

composites_created <- character(0)

all_imputed <- lapply(all_imputed, function(d) {

  # benefitsC — mean of 4 benefit items (no reversal)
  if (all(c("benefit_1","benefit_4","benefit_5","benefit_6") %in% names(d))) {
    d$benefitsC <- rowMeans(
      d[, c("benefit_1","benefit_4","benefit_5","benefit_6")],
      na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # side_effectsC — mean of 4 side effect items (no reversal)
  se_items <- paste0("side_effects_", 1:4)
  if (all(se_items %in% names(d))) {
    d$side_effectsC <- rowMeans(d[, se_items], na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # adlsC — mean of 2 ADL items (no reversal)
  if (all(c("adls_1","adls_2") %in% names(d))) {
    d$adlsC <- rowMeans(d[, c("adls_1","adls_2")], na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # dependenceC — mean of 3 dependence items (no reversal)
  dep_items <- paste0("dependence_", 1:3)
  if (all(dep_items %in% names(d))) {
    d$dependenceC <- rowMeans(d[, dep_items], na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # motivationC — mean of 3 motivation items (no reversal)
  mot_items <- paste0("motivation_", 1:3)
  if (all(mot_items %in% names(d))) {
    d$motivationC <- rowMeans(d[, mot_items], na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # capabilityC — capability reversed inline, then mean with capability_1
  if (all(c("capability_1","capability") %in% names(d))) {
    d$capabilityC <- rowMeans(
      data.frame(
        cap1  = as.numeric(d$capability_1),
        cap_r = 6 - as.numeric(d$capability)
      ),
      na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # pharmacistC — inf_hcp_3 reversed inline, then mean with desires_2
  if (all(c("desires_2","inf_hcp_3") %in% names(d))) {
    d$pharmacistC <- rowMeans(
      data.frame(
        des2   = as.numeric(d$desires_2),
        hcp3_r = 6 - as.numeric(d$inf_hcp_3)
      ),
      na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  # prescriberC — inf_hcp_1 reversed inline, then mean with desires_1
  if (all(c("desires_1","inf_hcp_1") %in% names(d))) {
    d$prescriberC <- rowMeans(
      data.frame(
        des1   = as.numeric(d$desires_1),
        hcp1_r = 6 - as.numeric(d$inf_hcp_1)
      ),
      na.rm = FALSE)
  }

  d
})

# Determine which composites were created
composite_names <- c("benefitsC","side_effectsC","adlsC","dependenceC",
                     "motivationC","capabilityC","pharmacistC","prescriberC")

cat("COMPOSITE CREATION SUMMARY:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
for (comp in composite_names) {
  if (comp %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
    n_na   <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]][[comp]]))
    m_val  <- round(mean(all_imputed[[1]][[comp]], na.rm = TRUE), 2)
    sd_val <- round(sd(all_imputed[[1]][[comp]],   na.rm = TRUE), 2)
    cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-15s | NAs = %d | M = %.2f | SD = %.2f\n",
                comp, n_na, m_val, sd_val))
    composites_created <- c(composites_created, comp)
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  ✗ %-15s | Could not create — check item names\n", comp))
  }
}
cat(sprintf("\n  Total composites created: %d / %d\n\n",
            length(composites_created), length(composite_names)))

# =============================================================================
# PART B3: CREATE BINARY OUTCOME VARIABLE
# =============================================================================
# freq_dich original coding:
#   0 = No longer uses BZRAs
#   1 = Uses BZRAs < 1 time per month
#   2 = Uses BZRAs > 1 time per month (regular use)
#
# Binary recoding:
#   stopped_bzra = 1 → Stoppers/rare users (freq_dich 0 or 1)
#   stopped_bzra = 0 → Continuers          (freq_dich 2)
#
# Rationale: Both freq_dich 0 and 1 represent meaningful reduction from
# regular use — either complete cessation or clinically insignificant
# residual use (< 1x/month). freq_dich 2 represents ongoing regular use.
# This binary variable is the primary outcome for logistic regression
# in Chunk 10. freq_dich is retained for sensitivity analyses.
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART B3: CREATING BINARY OUTCOME VARIABLE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("ORIGINAL CODING (freq_dich):\n")
cat("  0 = No longer uses BZRAs\n")
cat("  1 = Uses BZRAs < 1 time per month\n")
cat("  2 = Uses BZRAs > 1 time per month (regular use)\n\n")
cat("BINARY RECODING (stopped_bzra):\n")
cat("  freq_dich 0 (no longer uses)     → stopped_bzra = 1 (Stopper)\n")
cat("  freq_dich 1 (< 1x/month)         → stopped_bzra = 1 (Rare user)\n")
cat("  freq_dich 2 (> 1x/month regular) → stopped_bzra = 0 (Continuer)\n\n")
cat("RATIONALE:\n")
cat("  freq_dich 0 and 1 both represent meaningful reduction from regular\n")
cat("  BZRA use — either complete cessation or clinically insignificant\n")
cat("  residual use (< 1x/month). freq_dich 2 represents ongoing regular\n")
cat("  use and constitutes the comparison group (continuers).\n\n")

all_imputed <- lapply(all_imputed, function(d) {
  d$stopped_bzra <- dplyr::case_when(
    d$freq_dich %in% c(0, 1) ~ 1L,
    d$freq_dich == 2          ~ 0L,
    TRUE                      ~ NA_integer_
  )
  d
})

# Verify in imputation 1
cat("VERIFICATION (imputation 1):\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

freq_cross <- table(
  freq_dich    = all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich,
  stopped_bzra = all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra,
  useNA        = "ifany"
)
cat("Cross-tabulation freq_dich × stopped_bzra:\n")
print(freq_cross)
cat("\n")

n_stoppers   <- sum(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
n_continuers <- sum(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_na_out     <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra))
n_total      <- nrow(all_imputed[[1]])

cat(sprintf("  Stoppers/rare users (stopped_bzra = 1): n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_stoppers,   100 * n_stoppers   / n_total))
cat(sprintf("    of which no longer uses (freq_dich 0): n = %d\n",
            sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("    of which < 1x/month    (freq_dich 1): n = %d\n",
            sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("  Continuers (stopped_bzra = 0):          n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_continuers, 100 * n_continuers / n_total))
if (n_na_out > 0)
  cat(sprintf("  Missing    (stopped_bzra = NA):         n = %d\n", n_na_out))
cat("\n")

# Verify consistent across all imputations
ref_outcome    <- all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra
out_consistent <- all(sapply(all_imputed, function(d)
  identical(d$stopped_bzra, ref_outcome)))

if (out_consistent) {
  cat("  ✓ stopped_bzra identical across all", m,
      "imputations (as expected —\n")
  cat("    derived from freq_dich which was never imputed)\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  ✗ stopped_bzra differs across imputations — investigate\n\n")
}

if (n_na_out == 0) {
  cat("  ✓ No missing values in stopped_bzra\n\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf(
    "  ⚠️  %d missing values in stopped_bzra — check freq_dich coding\n\n",
    n_na_out))
}

# =============================================================================
# PART C: RELIABILITY ASSESSMENT (IMPUTATION 1)
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART C: RELIABILITY ASSESSMENT\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Using first imputed dataset (reliability is stable across imputations\n")
cat("given low FMI; van Buuren, 2018 §6.6).\n\n")
cat("NOTE: BFI-10 subscales each have 2 items. psych::alpha() on 2-item\n")
cat("  scales computes a Spearman-Brown coefficient, not true Cronbach's α.\n")
cat("  Interpret BFI α values as corrected item-total correlations rather\n")
cat("  than internal consistency estimates.\n")
cat("NOTE: DBAS_Total reliability reported here for completeness only.\n")
cat("  Subscales are the unit of analysis in Chunks 10 and 11B.\n\n")

data_imp1        <- all_imputed[[1]]
reliability_list <- list()

for (fam in names(families)) {
  cat(sprintf("%s:\n", fam))
  cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

  for (scale_name in families[[fam]]) {
    items  <- subscale_defs[[scale_name]]$items
    result <- assess_reliability(data_imp1, items, scale_name)
    reliability_list[[scale_name]] <- result

    if (!is.na(result$alpha)) {
      status_sym <- switch(result$status,
                           "GOOD"       = "✓",
                           "ACCEPTABLE" = "~",
                           "POOR"       = "⚠️",
                           "?")
      cat(sprintf(
        "  %s %-30s α = %.3f [%.3f, %.3f]  mean-r = %.3f  M = %.2f (SD = %.2f)  [%s]\n",
        status_sym, scale_name,
        result$alpha, result$alpha_ci_lower, result$alpha_ci_upper,
        result$mean_r, result$mean, result$sd, result$status))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  ✗ %-30s STATUS: %s\n", scale_name, result$status))
      if (length(result$missing) > 0)
        cat("    Missing:", paste(result$missing, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    }
  }
  cat("\n")
}

# Reliability summary table
rel_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(reliability_list), function(s) {
  r <- reliability_list[[s]]
  data.frame(
    subscale       = s,
    n_items        = length(subscale_defs[[s]]$items),
    n_reversed     = length(subscale_defs[[s]]$reversed),
    alpha          = round(r$alpha,          3),
    alpha_ci_lower = round(r$alpha_ci_lower, 3),
    alpha_ci_upper = round(r$alpha_ci_upper, 3),
    mean_inter_r   = round(r$mean_r,         3),
    mean           = round(r$mean,           2),
    sd             = round(r$sd,             2),
    status         = r$status,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

write.csv(rel_table, "subscale_reliability_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: subscale_reliability_summary.csv\n\n")

# =============================================================================
# PART D: VALIDATION
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART D: VALIDATION\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

validation_passed <- TRUE

# ── D1. All subscale variables present in every imputed dataset ───────────────
cat("D1. Subscale presence across all imputations\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

subscale_presence_fails <- 0L
for (i in seq_along(all_imputed)) {
  for (s in subscales_created) {
    if (!s %in% names(all_imputed[[i]])) {
      cat(sprintf("  ✗ %s missing from imputation %d\n", s, i))
      subscale_presence_fails <- subscale_presence_fails + 1L
      validation_passed <- FALSE
    }
  }
}
if (subscale_presence_fails == 0L)
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ All %d subscales present in all %d imputations\n\n",
              length(subscales_created), m))

# ── D2. Subscale scores complete (no NAs) ─────────────────────────────────────
cat("D2. Subscale completeness (no missing scores)\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

completeness_fails <- 0L
for (s in subscales_created) {
  for (i in seq_along(all_imputed)) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[i]][[s]]))
    if (n_na > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  ✗ %s: %d NAs in imputation %d\n", s, n_na, i))
      completeness_fails <- completeness_fails + 1L
      validation_passed  <- FALSE
    }
  }
}
if (completeness_fails == 0L)
  cat("  ✓ All subscale scores complete (no NAs) across all imputations\n\n")

# ── D3. Composite completeness (no NAs) ───────────────────────────────────────
cat("D3. Composite variable completeness (no missing scores)\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

composite_fails <- 0L
for (comp in composites_created) {
  for (i in seq_along(all_imputed)) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[i]][[comp]]))
    if (n_na > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  ✗ %s: %d NAs in imputation %d\n", comp, n_na, i))
      composite_fails   <- composite_fails + 1L
      validation_passed <- FALSE
    }
  }
}
if (composite_fails == 0L)
  cat("  ✓ All composite scores complete (no NAs) across all imputations\n\n")

# ── D4. Reversed items used, not originals ────────────────────────────────────
cat("D4. Reversed item name check\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Confirming subscales use _r names (not unreversed originals)\n\n")

original_names_that_should_be_reversed <- list(
  BFI_Extraversion      = "reserved",
  BFI_Agreeableness     = "find_fault",
  BFI_Conscientiousness = "lazy",
  BFI_Neuroticism       = "relaxed",
  BFI_Openness          = "few_interests",
  SURPS_Hopelessness    = c("surps1","surps4","surps7",
                            "surps13","surps20","surps23")
)

for (scale_name in names(original_names_that_should_be_reversed)) {
  bad_items <- intersect(
    original_names_that_should_be_reversed[[scale_name]],
    subscale_defs[[scale_name]]$items
  )
  if (length(bad_items) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  ✗ %s uses un-reversed name(s): %s\n",
                scale_name, paste(bad_items, collapse = ", ")))
    validation_passed <- FALSE
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  ✓ %s uses correctly reversed items\n", scale_name))
  }
}
cat("\n")

# ── D5. Subscale range plausibility ───────────────────────────────────────────
cat("D5. Subscale range plausibility (imputation 1)\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

expected_ranges <- list(
  BFI_Extraversion        = c(1, 5), BFI_Agreeableness         = c(1, 5),
  BFI_Conscientiousness   = c(1, 5), BFI_Neuroticism           = c(1, 5),
  BFI_Openness            = c(1, 5),
  SURPS_Hopelessness      = c(1, 4), SURPS_Impulsivity         = c(1, 4),
  SURPS_Sensation_Seeking = c(1, 4), SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity = c(1, 4),
  DBAS_Consequences       = c(0,10), DBAS_Worry_Helplessness   = c(0,10),
  DBAS_Expectations       = c(0,10), DBAS_Medications          = c(0,10),
  DBAS_Total              = c(0,10),
  CISS_Task               = c(1, 5), CISS_Emotion              = c(1, 5),
  CISS_Avoidance          = c(1, 5)
)

range_fails <- 0L
for (s in names(expected_ranges)) {
  if (!s %in% names(data_imp1)) next
  exp_min <- expected_ranges[[s]][1]
  exp_max <- expected_ranges[[s]][2]
  obs_min <- min(data_imp1[[s]], na.rm = TRUE)
  obs_max <- max(data_imp1[[s]], na.rm = TRUE)

  if (obs_min < exp_min | obs_max > exp_max) {
    cat(sprintf(
      "  ⚠️  %-30s observed [%.2f, %.2f] outside expected [%g, %g]\n",
      s, obs_min, obs_max, exp_min, exp_max))
    range_fails <- range_fails + 1L
  } else {
    cat(sprintf(
      "  ✓ %-30s range [%.2f, %.2f]  (expected [%g, %g])\n",
      s, obs_min, obs_max, exp_min, exp_max))
  }
}
if (range_fails == 0L) cat("\n  ✓ All subscale scores within expected ranges\n")
cat("\n")

# ── D6. Composite range plausibility ──────────────────────────────────────────
cat("D6. Composite variable range plausibility (imputation 1)\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

composite_ranges <- list(
  benefitsC     = c(1, 5), side_effectsC = c(1, 5),
  adlsC         = c(1, 5), dependenceC   = c(1, 5),
  motivationC   = c(1, 5), capabilityC   = c(1, 5),
  pharmacistC   = c(1, 5), prescriberC   = c(1, 5)
)

comp_range_fails <- 0L
for (comp in names(composite_ranges)) {
  if (!comp %in% names(data_imp1)) next
  exp_min <- composite_ranges[[comp]][1]
  exp_max <- composite_ranges[[comp]][2]
  obs_min <- min(data_imp1[[comp]], na.rm = TRUE)
  obs_max <- max(data_imp1[[comp]], na.rm = TRUE)

  if (obs_min < exp_min | obs_max > exp_max) {
    cat(sprintf(
      "  ⚠️  %-15s observed [%.2f, %.2f] outside expected [%g, %g]\n",
      comp, obs_min, obs_max, exp_min, exp_max))
    comp_range_fails <- comp_range_fails + 1L
  } else {
    cat(sprintf(
      "  ✓ %-15s range [%.2f, %.2f]  (expected [%g, %g])\n",
      comp, obs_min, obs_max, exp_min, exp_max))
  }
}
if (comp_range_fails == 0L)
  cat("\n  ✓ All composite scores within expected ranges\n")
cat("\n")

# ── D7. Chunk 8 carry-through variables ───────────────────────────────────────
cat("D7. Chunk 8 carry-through variables\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")

# n_health_conditions — recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E
if ("n_health_conditions" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  cat("  ✓ n_health_conditions present\n")
  cat(sprintf("    Range: [%g, %g] | Mean: %.2f\n",
              min(all_imputed[[1]]$n_health_conditions),
              max(all_imputed[[1]]$n_health_conditions),
              mean(all_imputed[[1]]$n_health_conditions)))
} else {
  cat("  ✗ n_health_conditions MISSING\n")
  validation_passed <- FALSE
}

# bzra_weekly_dose_eq — recalculated in Chunk 8 Part E as daily × 7
if ("bzra_weekly_dose_eq" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  n_na_w <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]]$bzra_weekly_dose_eq))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ bzra_weekly_dose_eq present | NAs: %d\n", n_na_w))
  if (n_na_w > 0) {
    cat("    ⚠️  Unexpected NAs — check Chunk 8 Part E bzra recalculation\n")
    validation_passed <- FALSE
  } else {
    # Spot-check: weekly should equal daily × 7
    daily_w  <- all_imputed[[1]]$bzra_daily_dose_eq
    weekly_w <- all_imputed[[1]]$bzra_weekly_dose_eq
    if (!is.null(daily_w)) {
      n_mismatch <- sum(abs(weekly_w - daily_w * 7) > 1e-8, na.rm = TRUE)
      if (n_mismatch == 0) {
        cat("    ✓ weekly = daily × 7 verified for all rows\n")
      } else {
        cat(sprintf("    ⚠️  %d rows where weekly ≠ daily × 7 — investigate\n",
                    n_mismatch))
        validation_passed <- FALSE
      }
    }
  }
} else {
  cat("  ✗ bzra_weekly_dose_eq MISSING — check Chunk 8 Part E\n")
  validation_passed <- FALSE
}

# freq_dich — original 3-level outcome, added back in Chunk 8 Part F
if ("freq_dich" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  cat("  ✓ freq_dich present (3-level reference outcome)\n")
  cat(sprintf("    0 = No longer uses:     n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("    1 = < 1 time per month: n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("    2 = > 1 time per month: n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 2, na.rm = TRUE)))

  fd_ref        <- all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich
  fd_consistent <- all(sapply(all_imputed, function(d)
    identical(d$freq_dich, fd_ref)))
  if (fd_consistent) {
    cat("  ✓ freq_dich identical across all", m,
        "imputations (as expected)\n")
  } else {
    cat("  ✗ freq_dich differs across imputations — investigate\n")
    validation_passed <- FALSE
  }
} else {
  cat("  ✗ freq_dich MISSING — check Chunk 8 Part F\n")
  validation_passed <- FALSE
}

# stopped_bzra — binary outcome derived from freq_dich in Part B3
if ("stopped_bzra" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
  cat("  ✓ stopped_bzra present (binary outcome derived in Part B3)\n")
  cat(sprintf("    Stoppers/rare users (1): n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("      No longer uses (freq_dich 0): n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("      < 1x/month    (freq_dich 1): n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("    Continuers          (0): n = %d\n",
              sum(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra == 0, na.rm = TRUE)))

  sb_ref        <- all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra
  sb_consistent <- all(sapply(all_imputed, function(d)
    identical(d$stopped_bzra, sb_ref)))
  if (sb_consistent) {
    cat("  ✓ stopped_bzra identical across all", m, "imputations\n")
  } else {
    cat("  ✗ stopped_bzra differs across imputations — investigate\n")
    validation_passed <- FALSE
  }
} else {
  cat("  ✗ stopped_bzra MISSING — check Part B3\n")
  validation_passed <- FALSE
}
cat("\n")

# ── D8. Polr/polyreg-imputed variables complete ────────────────────────────────
cat("D8. Polr/polyreg-imputed variables completeness\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Checking: education, income, employment, driving_freq, gen_health,\n")
cat("            fall2, alc_use, stop_wdl_exp_combined, prov_terr\n\n")

polr_check_vars <- c(
  "education", "income", "employment", "driving_freq", "gen_health",
  "fall2", "alc_use", "stop_wdl_exp_combined", "prov_terr"
)

polr_check_fail <- FALSE
for (v in polr_check_vars) {
  if (v %in% names(all_imputed[[1]])) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]][[v]]))
    if (n_na > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %-30s: %d unexpected NAs — check Chunk 8\n", v, n_na))
      polr_check_fail <- TRUE
      validation_passed <- FALSE
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-30s complete\n", v))
    }
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %-30s NOT present — check Chunk 8 output\n", v))
    polr_check_fail <- TRUE
    validation_passed <- FALSE
  }
}

# sleep_aids variables — checked dynamically
if (length(sleep_aid_vars) > 0) {
  for (v in sleep_aid_vars) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]][[v]]))
    if (n_na > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %-30s: %d unexpected NAs\n", v, n_na))
      validation_passed <- FALSE
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-30s complete\n", v))
    }
  }
}

if (!polr_check_fail && length(sleep_aid_vars) >= 0)
  cat("\n  ✓ All polr/polyreg-imputed variables complete\n")
cat("\n")

# =============================================================================
# PART E: SAVE OUTPUTS
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART E: SAVING OUTPUTS\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

saveRDS(all_imputed, "imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds")
cat("✓ Saved: imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds\n")
cat("  (List of", m,
    "complete datasets with subscales, composites,\n")
cat("   stopped_bzra, freq_dich, and all imputed variables)\n\n")

saveRDS(list(
  subscale_defs      = subscale_defs,
  subscales_created  = subscales_created,
  subscales_skipped  = subscales_skipped,
  composites_created = composites_created,
  reliability_list   = reliability_list,
  reliability_table  = rel_table
), "subscale_metadata.rds")
cat("✓ Saved: subscale_metadata.rds\n\n")

write.csv(all_imputed[[1]], "imputed_example_with_subscales.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: imputed_example_with_subscales.csv (imputation 1)\n\n")

# =============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# =============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
if (validation_passed) {
  cat("✅ ALL VALIDATION CHECKS PASSED\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠️  ONE OR MORE VALIDATION CHECKS FAILED — review above\n")
}
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("PROCESSING COMPLETE:\n")
cat(sprintf("  ✓ %d subscales created across all %d imputations\n",
            length(subscales_created), m))
cat(sprintf("  ✓ %d composite variables created across all %d imputations\n",
            length(composites_created), m))
cat("  ✓ All subscales use reversed items (_r) from Chunk 6\n")
cat("  ✓ Composites: inline reversal for capabilityC, pharmacistC, prescriberC\n")
cat("  ✓ Binary outcome stopped_bzra created from freq_dich (Part B3)\n")
cat("  ✓ Reliability assessed on imputation 1\n")
cat("  ✓ DBAS_Total created for reliability only — excluded from\n")
cat("    Chunk 10 descriptives and Chunk 11B VSURF\n")
cat("  ✓ freq_dich, n_health_conditions, bzra_weekly_dose_eq\n")
cat("    present (from Chunk 8 Parts E–F)\n\n")

cat("RELIABILITY OVERVIEW:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
n_good <- sum(rel_table$status == "GOOD",       na.rm = TRUE)
n_acc  <- sum(rel_table$status == "ACCEPTABLE", na.rm = TRUE)
n_poor <- sum(rel_table$status == "POOR",       na.rm = TRUE)
n_fail <- sum(is.na(rel_table$status))
cat(sprintf("  ✓ Good (α ≥ 0.70):          %d subscales\n", n_good))
cat(sprintf("  ~ Acceptable (α 0.60-0.69): %d subscales\n", n_acc))
cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  Poor (α < 0.60):          %d subscales\n", n_poor))
if (n_fail > 0)
  cat(sprintf("  ✗ Failed to compute:        %d subscales\n", n_fail))
cat("\n")

cat("COMPOSITE VARIABLES CREATED:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
composite_labels <- list(
  benefitsC     = "mean(benefit_1, benefit_4, benefit_5, benefit_6)",
  side_effectsC = "mean(side_effects_1:4)",
  adlsC         = "mean(adls_1, adls_2)",
  dependenceC   = "mean(dependence_1:3)",
  motivationC   = "mean(motivation_1:3)",
  capabilityC   = "mean(capability_1, 6-capability)",
  pharmacistC   = "mean(desires_2, 6-inf_hcp_3)",
  prescriberC   = "mean(desires_1, 6-inf_hcp_1)"
)
for (comp in composite_names) {
  status_sym <- if (comp %in% composites_created) "✓" else "✗"
  cat(sprintf("  %s %-15s = %s\n",
              status_sym, comp, composite_labels[[comp]]))
}
cat("\n")

cat("OUTCOME VARIABLES:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Primary (binary): stopped_bzra\n")
cat("    1 = Stoppers/rare users (freq_dich 0 or 1):", n_stoppers, "\n")
cat("      of which no longer uses (freq_dich 0):   ",
    sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("      of which < 1x/month    (freq_dich 1):   ",
    sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("    0 = Continuers > 1x/month (freq_dich 2):  ", n_continuers, "\n\n")
cat("  Reference (3-level): freq_dich\n")
cat("    0 = No longer uses:     ",
    sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("    1 = < 1 time per month: ",
    sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("    2 = > 1 time per month: ",
    sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds\n")
cat("     → List of", m, "complete datasets with subscales,\n")
cat("       composites, stopped_bzra, freq_dich, and all imputed vars\n")
cat("  2. subscale_reliability_summary.csv\n")
cat("     → Alpha, CI, mean-r per subscale\n")
cat("  3. subscale_metadata.rds\n")
cat("     → Subscale/composite definitions + reliability results\n")
cat("  4. imputed_example_with_subscales.csv\n")
cat("     → Imputation 1 for visual inspection\n\n")

cat("NEXT STEPS:\n")
cat("  1. Review subscale_reliability_summary.csv\n")
cat("     → Note any α < 0.60 for manuscript reporting\n")
cat("  2. Load imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds for Chunk 10\n")
cat("     → Primary outcome: stopped_bzra\n")
cat("         1 = Stoppers/rare users\n")
cat("             (no longer uses OR < 1x/month, freq_dich 0 or 1)\n")
cat("         0 = Continuers (> 1x/month regular use, freq_dich 2)\n")
cat("     → Sensitivity: freq_dich retained for 3-level analyses\n")
cat("     → All subscales and composites ready for analysis\n")
cat("     → DBAS_Total present in data but excluded from analysis\n\n")

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("✓ POST-IMPUTATION PROCESSING COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
```




## Descriptive Stats
```{r}
#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 10: DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS & GROUP COMPARISONS
# Pooled across all m imputations using Rubin's Rules
#==============================================================================
#
# CHANGES FROM PREVIOUS VERSION:
#   1. bzra_daily_diazepam_eq → bzra_daily_dose_eq (renamed in Chunk 5+6)
#   2. stop_wdl_exp_combined: recoded from 6 levels to 3 levels (collapsed
#      in Chunk 5+6 Part B — "No withdrawal" | "Insomnia only" |
#      "Other withdrawal symptoms"). Recoded by matching existing labels,
#      not numeric levels, to prevent NAs.
#   3. fall2: levels 0:3 (level 3 = "Fall but no injury" observed in data;
#      was previously coded as 0:2 which produced NAs).
#   4. Individual Likert items (benefit_1, side_effects_1, etc.) removed
#      from variable list — composites (benefitsC, etc.) used instead
#      (created in Chunk 9 Part B2).
#   5. DBAS_Total removed from personality vector — subscales only reported
#      for descriptives (DBAS_Consequences, DBAS_Worry_Helplessness,
#      DBAS_Expectations, DBAS_Medications).
#   6. Recode function cleaned — individual Likert item numeric coercions
#      removed (those items no longer in analysis set).
#
# DATA SOURCE:
#   Loads imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds — a plain list of m complete
#   data frames produced by Chunk 9. No mids object needed here.
#
# OUTCOME:
#   stopped_bzra (created in Chunk 9, Part B3):
#     1 = Stoppers/rare users (freq_dich 0 or 1)
#     0 = Continuers (freq_dich 2, regular use)
#   freq_dich retained for sensitivity analyses and reporting breakdowns.
#
# POOLING METHODS:
#
#   Continuous variables:
#     - Pooled mean:  Q_bar = mean of per-imputation means
#     - Pooled variance (Rubin's Rules):
#         W = mean of within-imputation variances  (average SE^2)
#         B = variance of per-imputation means     (between-imputation variance)
#         T = W + (1 + 1/m) * B                   (total variance)
#     - Pooled SD reported as sqrt of pooled within-imputation variance (W),
#       which reflects typical within-sample spread, not estimation uncertainty
#     - Group comparison: pool t-statistics via Li et al. (1991) method,
#       which gives a valid p-value from pooled F/t
#
#   Categorical variables:
#     - Pooled proportion: mean of per-imputation proportions per level
#     - Pooled n(%): pooled proportion * N
#     - Group comparison: pool chi-square statistics (mean across imputations),
#       test against chi-square distribution (Enders, 2010 approach)
#
#   Effect sizes:
#     - Cohen's d: computed from pooled means and pooled within-SD
#       Positive d = higher scores in stoppers than continuers
#     - Cramer's V: computed from pooled chi-square statistic
#
#   FDR: Benjamini-Hochberg applied to pooled p-values within families
#
# SLEEP AID VARIABLES (alc_sleep, can_sleep, melatonin_use, op_sleep):
#   These are conditional display variables in REDCap — only shown to
#   participants who indicated using that substance. 0 = never uses.
#   Treated as ordered factors (0=Never, 1-4=frequency levels).
#   Run initially with full level breakdown to assess sparsity.
#   If levels are too sparse, collapse to binary (Never vs Any use)
#   and switch to Fisher's exact test.
#
# REFERENCES:
#   Rubin, D.B. (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.
#   Li, K.H., Raghunathan, T.E., & Rubin, D.B. (1991). Statistica Sinica.
#   Enders, C.K. (2010). Applied Missing Data Analysis.
#   Benjamini, Y., & Hochberg, Y. (1995). JRSS-B, 57(1), 289-300.
#   Cohen, J. (1988). Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioural Sciences.
#==============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS & GROUP COMPARISONS\n")
cat("(Pooled across all imputations — Rubin's Rules)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOAD DATA
# Chunk 9 output: plain list of m complete data frames
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!file.exists("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds not found. Run Chunk 9 first.")

all_imputed <- readRDS("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds")
m           <- length(all_imputed)
N           <- nrow(all_imputed[[1]])

cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Imputations (m):          %d\n", m))
cat(sprintf("  Observations (N):         %d\n", N))
cat(sprintf("  Variables per dataset:    %d\n\n", ncol(all_imputed[[1]])))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFY OUTCOME VARIABLE FROM CHUNK 9
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!"stopped_bzra" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]]))
  stop("ERROR: 'stopped_bzra' not found — check Chunk 9 Part B3 ran successfully.")

if (!"freq_dich" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]]))
  stop("ERROR: 'freq_dich' not found — check Chunk 8 Part F ran successfully.")
```
