```{r}
# Use stopped_bzra directly from Chunk 9 — no re-derivation needed
n_stoppers   <- sum(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
n_continuers <- sum(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_na_outcome <- sum(is.na(all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra))
fd_tab       <- table(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich, useNA = "ifany")

cat("OUTCOME: stopped_bzra (from Chunk 9 Part B3)\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  1 = Stoppers/rare users (freq_dich 0 or 1)\n")
cat("  0 = Continuers (freq_dich 2, regular use)\n\n")
cat("  freq_dich breakdown:\n")
for (val in names(fd_tab))
  cat(sprintf("    freq_dich = %s: n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              val, fd_tab[val], 100 * fd_tab[val] / N))
cat(sprintf("\n  Stoppers  (stopped_bzra = 1): n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_stoppers,   100 * n_stoppers / N))
cat(sprintf("    of which no longer uses (freq_dich 0): n = %d\n",
            sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("    of which < 1x/month    (freq_dich 1): n = %d\n",
            sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("  Continuers (stopped_bzra = 0): n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_continuers, 100 * n_continuers / N))
if (n_na_outcome > 0)
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  Missing (stopped_bzra = NA): n = %d\n", n_na_outcome))
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RECODE FUNCTION — applied to each imputed dataset
#
# FIX 1: stop_wdl_exp_combined now has 3 levels with text labels
#   (collapsed from 6 in Chunk 5+6 Part B). Recoded by matching existing
#   labels rather than numeric levels to prevent NAs.
#
# FIX 2: fall2 now includes level 3 ("Fall but no injury") — observed in
#   data per Chunk 11B diagnostic. Previous 0:2 coding produced NAs.
#
# NOTE: Individual Likert items removed from recode function — composites
#   (benefitsC, side_effectsC, etc.) are used directly from Chunk 9.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

recode_dataset <- function(df) {

  # ── Sex ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("sex" %in% names(df))
    df$sex <- factor(df$sex, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Female", "Male"))

  # ── Province/Territory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("prov_terr" %in% names(df)) {
    df$prov_terr <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$prov_terr %in% c(1, 2, 3, 12) ~ "Western Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(9, 11)       ~ "Central Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(4, 5, 7, 10) ~ "Atlantic Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(6, 8, 13)    ~ "Territories",
      TRUE                              ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$prov_terr <- factor(df$prov_terr,
      levels = c("Western Canada", "Central Canada",
                 "Atlantic Canada", "Territories"))
  }

  # ── Employment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("employment" %in% names(df)) {
    df$employment <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$employment %in% c(0, 3, 4) ~ "Not in Workforce",
      df$employment %in% c(1, 2)    ~ "Full- or Part-Time Work",
      TRUE                           ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$employment <- factor(df$employment,
      levels = c("Full- or Part-Time Work", "Not in Workforce"))
  }

  # ── Education ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("education" %in% names(df)) {
    df$education <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$education %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ "High School or Less",
      df$education %in% c(4, 5)    ~ "Trade School/University Degree",
      TRUE                          ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$education <- factor(df$education,
      levels = c("High School or Less", "Trade School/University Degree"))
  }

  # ── Income ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("income" %in% names(df))
    df$income <- factor(df$income, levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("$15,000 or less", "$15,000 to $30,000", "$30,000 to $50,000",
                 "$50,000 to $80,000", "$80,000 or more"))

  # ── Driving frequency ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("driving_freq" %in% names(df))
    df$driving_freq <- factor(df$driving_freq, levels = 0:3,
      labels = c("Do not drive", "Less than 1 day/week",
                 "1-3 days/week", "4-7 days/week"))

  # ── General health ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("gen_health" %in% names(df))
    df$gen_health <- factor(as.numeric(df$gen_health), levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("Poor", "Fair", "Good", "Very good", "Excellent"))

  # ── Alcohol use (combined ordinal 0-4) ──────────────────────────────────────
  if ("alc_use" %in% names(df))
    df$alc_use <- factor(df$alc_use, levels = 0:4,
      labels = c("Never", "Low (<1 drink/wk)", "Moderate (1-7 drinks/wk)",
                 "High (8-14 drinks/wk)", "Very high (>14 drinks/wk)"))

  # ── Withdrawal experience ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # FIX 1: Now 3-level unordered factor (collapsed in Chunk 5+6 Part B).
  # Labels match exactly what is stored in the imputed data.
  # Re-factoring by existing labels preserves values without NAs.
  if ("stop_wdl_exp_combined" %in% names(df))
    df$stop_wdl_exp_combined <- factor(
      df$stop_wdl_exp_combined,
      levels = c("No withdrawal", "Insomnia only", "Other withdrawal symptoms")
    )

  # ── Fall injury severity — levels 0-3 ───────────────────────────────────────
  # FIX 2: Level 3 ("Fall but no injury") observed in data (Chunk 11B).
  # Previous coding (0:2) produced NAs for level 3 participants.
  if ("fall2" %in% names(df))
    df$fall2 <- factor(df$fall2, levels = 0:3,
      labels = c("No fall / not injured", "Minor injury",
                 "Serious injury", "Fall but no injury"))

  # ── Sleep aid frequency variables ───────────────────────────────────────────
  # Conditional display variables — 0 = does not use this substance.
  # Treated as ordered factors (0=Never, 1-4=frequency levels).
  # Run initially with full breakdown to assess sparsity.
  # If too sparse, collapse to binary (Never vs Any use) in a later pass.
  sleep_aid_vars   <- c("alc_sleep", "can_sleep", "melatonin_use", "op_sleep")
  sleep_aid_labels <- c("Never", "<1 day/week", "1-3 days/week",
                        "4-6 days/week", "7 days/week")
  for (var in sleep_aid_vars) {
    if (var %in% names(df))
      df[[var]] <- factor(df[[var]], levels = 0:4,
                          labels = sleep_aid_labels, ordered = TRUE)
  }

  # ── Personality subscales and composites — ensure numeric ───────────────────
  # These are rowMeans() outputs from Chunk 9 and should already be numeric.
  # Coercion is a safety net only.
  numeric_vars <- c(
    # Personality subscales
    "BFI_Extraversion", "BFI_Agreeableness", "BFI_Conscientiousness",
    "BFI_Neuroticism", "BFI_Openness",
    "SURPS_Hopelessness", "SURPS_Impulsivity",
    "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking", "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity",
    "DBAS_Consequences", "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness",
    "DBAS_Expectations", "DBAS_Medications",
    "CISS_Task", "CISS_Emotion", "CISS_Avoidance",
    # COM-B composites
    "benefitsC", "side_effectsC", "adlsC", "dependenceC",
    "motivationC", "capabilityC", "pharmacistC", "prescriberC"
  )
  for (var in numeric_vars)
    if (var %in% names(df) && !is.numeric(df[[var]]))
      df[[var]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df[[var]]))

  df
}

# Apply recoding to every imputed dataset
all_datasets <- lapply(all_imputed, recode_dataset)
cat(sprintf("✓ Recoded all %d imputed datasets\n\n", m))

d1 <- all_datasets[[1]]

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SPARSITY DIAGNOSTIC FOR SLEEP AID VARIABLES
# Print level counts in imputation 1 so you can decide whether to collapse
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("SLEEP AID VARIABLE SPARSITY CHECK (imputation 1):\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("Review counts below — if any non-Never levels have n < 5 in either\n")
cat("group, consider collapsing to binary (Never vs Any use).\n\n")

sleep_aid_vars <- c("alc_sleep", "can_sleep", "melatonin_use", "op_sleep")

for (var in sleep_aid_vars) {
  if (!var %in% names(d1)) next
  cat(sprintf("%s:\n", var))
  tab <- table(
    Level = d1[[var]],
    Group = ifelse(d1$stopped_bzra == 1, "Stopper", "Continuer"),
    useNA = "ifany"
  )
  print(addmargins(tab))
  cat("\n")
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VARIABLE DEFINITIONS
#
# FIX 1: bzra_daily_dose_eq (was bzra_daily_diazepam_eq — renamed Chunk 5+6)
# FIX 2: DBAS_Total removed — subscales only per study decision
# FIX 3: Individual Likert items removed — composites used instead
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

demographics <- c(
  "age", "sex", "education", "income", "employment",
  "driving_freq", "prov_terr"
)

# FIX 1: bzra_daily_dose_eq (not bzra_daily_diazepam_eq)
clinical <- c(
  "phq2_score", "osss_3_score", "gen_health", "med_quant",
  "n_physical_conditions", "n_ment_conditions", "n_sleep_conditions",
  "n_health_conditions",
  "bzra_daily_dose_eq",
  "c_lengthofuse",
  "fall2",
  "alc_use"
)

sleep_adverse <- c(
  "alc_sleep", "can_sleep", "melatonin_use", "op_sleep",
  "stop_wdl_exp_combined", "wdl_symptom_count"
)

# FIX 3: Composites only — individual items removed
beliefs_comob <- c(
  "benefitsC", "side_effectsC", "adlsC", "dependenceC",
  "motivationC", "capabilityC", "pharmacistC", "prescriberC"
)

# FIX 2: DBAS_Total removed — subscales only
personality <- c(
  "BFI_Extraversion", "BFI_Agreeableness", "BFI_Conscientiousness",
  "BFI_Neuroticism", "BFI_Openness",
  "SURPS_Hopelessness", "SURPS_Impulsivity",
  "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking", "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity",
  "DBAS_Consequences", "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness",
  "DBAS_Expectations", "DBAS_Medications",
  "CISS_Task", "CISS_Emotion", "CISS_Avoidance"
)

all_vars <- unique(c(demographics, clinical, sleep_adverse,
                     beliefs_comob, personality))
all_vars <- all_vars[all_vars %in% names(d1)]

cat_vars <- c(
  "sex", "education", "income", "employment", "driving_freq",
  "prov_terr", "gen_health", "alc_use",
  "stop_wdl_exp_combined", "fall2",
  "alc_sleep", "can_sleep", "melatonin_use", "op_sleep"
)
cat_vars  <- cat_vars[cat_vars %in% names(d1)]
cont_vars <- all_vars[!all_vars %in% cat_vars]

get_family <- function(var) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    var %in% demographics  ~ "Demographics",
    var %in% clinical      ~ "Clinical",
    var %in% sleep_adverse ~ "Sleep_Adverse",
    var %in% beliefs_comob ~ "Beliefs_COMB",
    var %in% personality   ~ "Personality",
    TRUE                   ~ "Other"
  )
}

family_lookup <- data.frame(
  variable = all_vars,
  Family   = get_family(all_vars),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# ── Verify subscales and composites present ───────────────────────────────────
expected_personality <- c(
  "BFI_Extraversion", "BFI_Agreeableness", "BFI_Conscientiousness",
  "BFI_Neuroticism", "BFI_Openness",
  "SURPS_Hopelessness", "SURPS_Impulsivity",
  "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking", "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity",
  "DBAS_Consequences", "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness",
  "DBAS_Expectations", "DBAS_Medications",
  "CISS_Task", "CISS_Emotion", "CISS_Avoidance"
)
missing_subscales <- expected_personality[!expected_personality %in% names(d1)]
if (length(missing_subscales) > 0) {
  cat("⚠️  Subscales expected but not found (check Chunk 9):\n")
  for (v in missing_subscales) cat("  •", v, "\n")
  cat("\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("✓ All %d personality subscales present\n", length(expected_personality)))
}

expected_composites <- c("benefitsC", "side_effectsC", "adlsC", "dependenceC",
                         "motivationC", "capabilityC", "pharmacistC", "prescriberC")
missing_composites <- expected_composites[!expected_composites %in% names(d1)]
if (length(missing_composites) > 0) {
  cat("⚠️  Composite variables expected but not found (check Chunk 9):\n")
  for (v in missing_composites) cat("  •", v, "\n")
  cat("\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("✓ All %d composite variables present\n\n", length(expected_composites)))
}

# ── Confirm DBAS_Total not in analysis set ────────────────────────────────────
if ("DBAS_Total" %in% all_vars) {
  cat("⚠️  DBAS_Total found in variable list — should not be present.\n")
  cat("   Check personality vector definition above.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("✓ DBAS_Total correctly excluded (subscales only)\n\n")
}

# ── Confirm renamed dose variable ─────────────────────────────────────────────
if ("bzra_daily_diazepam_eq" %in% all_vars) {
  cat("⚠️  Old variable name bzra_daily_diazepam_eq found — should be bzra_daily_dose_eq.\n\n")
} else if ("bzra_daily_dose_eq" %in% all_vars) {
  cat("✓ bzra_daily_dose_eq present (correctly renamed from Chunk 5+6)\n\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠️  bzra_daily_dose_eq not found in data — check Chunk 8 Part E.\n\n")
}

cat("Variable families:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Demographics:   %d  |  Clinical:      %d\n",
            sum(all_vars %in% demographics), sum(all_vars %in% clinical)))
cat(sprintf("  Sleep/Adverse:  %d  |  Beliefs/COM-B: %d\n",
            sum(all_vars %in% sleep_adverse), sum(all_vars %in% beliefs_comob)))
cat(sprintf("  Personality:    %d  |  Total:         %d\n\n",
            sum(all_vars %in% personality), length(all_vars)))

#==============================================================================
# POOLING FUNCTIONS
# outcome_var = "stopped_bzra": 1 = stopper, 0 = continuer (from Chunk 9)
#==============================================================================

outcome_var <- "stopped_bzra"

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL CONTINUOUS VARIABLE
# Returns: overall Mean (SD), group means/SDs, pooled t-test p-value, Cohen's d
# Group 0 = continuers, Group 1 = stoppers
# Cohen's d: positive = higher in stoppers
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pool_continuous <- function(var, datasets, outcome = outcome_var) {

  imp_stats <- lapply(datasets, function(df) {
    x   <- as.numeric(df[[var]])
    grp <- as.integer(df[[outcome]])
    x0  <- x[!is.na(grp) & grp == 0]
    x1  <- x[!is.na(grp) & grp == 1]
    n0  <- sum(!is.na(x0))
    n1  <- sum(!is.na(x1))
    if (n0 < 2 || n1 < 2) return(NULL)

    m0    <- mean(x0, na.rm = TRUE); m1    <- mean(x1, na.rm = TRUE)
    v0    <- var(x0,  na.rm = TRUE); v1    <- var(x1,  na.rm = TRUE)
    m_all <- mean(x,  na.rm = TRUE)
    v_all <- var(x,   na.rm = TRUE)

    sp      <- sqrt(((n0 - 1) * v0 + (n1 - 1) * v1) / (n0 + n1 - 2))
    se_diff <- sqrt(v0 / n0 + v1 / n1)

    list(m_all = m_all, v_all = v_all,
         m0 = m0, v0 = v0, n0 = n0,
         m1 = m1, v1 = v1, n1 = n1,
         diff = m1 - m0, se_diff = se_diff, sp = sp)
  })
  imp_stats <- Filter(Negate(is.null), imp_stats)
  if (length(imp_stats) == 0) return(NULL)

  mm <- length(imp_stats)

  pool_m_all  <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "m_all"))
  pool_v_all  <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "v_all"))
  pool_sd_all <- sqrt(pool_v_all)

  pool_m0  <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "m0"))
  pool_v0  <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "v0"))
  pool_sd0 <- sqrt(pool_v0)
  n0       <- round(mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "n0")))

  pool_m1  <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "m1"))
  pool_v1  <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "v1"))
  pool_sd1 <- sqrt(pool_v1)
  n1       <- round(mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "n1")))

  diffs  <- sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "diff")
  se2s   <- sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "se_diff")^2
  Q_bar  <- mean(diffs)
  W      <- mean(se2s)
  B      <- if (mm > 1) var(diffs) else 0
  T_var  <- W + (1 + 1 / mm) * B
  t_stat <- Q_bar / sqrt(T_var)

  lambda <- (1 + 1 / mm) * B / max(T_var, 1e-12)
  df_old <- (n0 + n1 - 2)
  df_obs <- if (lambda > 0) (1 - lambda) * df_old * (df_old + 1) / (df_old + 3) else df_old
  df_m   <- if (mm > 1 && lambda > 0) (mm - 1) / lambda^2 else Inf
  df     <- 1 / (1 / df_m + 1 / df_obs)
  p_val  <- 2 * pt(abs(t_stat), df = df, lower.tail = FALSE)

  pool_sp <- mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "sp"))
  d       <- if (pool_sp > 0) Q_bar / pool_sp else NA_real_
  se_d    <- if (!is.na(d))
    sqrt((n0 + n1) / (n0 * n1) + d^2 / (2 * (n0 + n1 - 2))) else NA_real_

  list(
    type         = "continuous",
    overall_desc = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", pool_m_all, pool_sd_all),
    cont_desc    = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", pool_m0,    pool_sd0),
    stop_desc    = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", pool_m1,    pool_sd1),
    pool_m0      = pool_m0,
    pool_m1      = pool_m1,
    pool_sd0     = pool_sd0,
    pool_sd1     = pool_sd1,
    p_val        = p_val,
    effect_type  = "Cohens_d",
    effect_size  = round(d, 3),
    ci_low       = if (!is.na(d) && !is.na(se_d)) round(d - 1.96 * se_d, 3) else NA_real_,
    ci_high      = if (!is.na(d) && !is.na(se_d)) round(d + 1.96 * se_d, 3) else NA_real_
  )
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL CATEGORICAL VARIABLE
# Returns: per-level pooled n(%), pooled chi-square p-value, Cramer's V
# Group 0 = continuers, Group 1 = stoppers
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pool_categorical <- function(var, datasets, outcome = outcome_var) {

  lvls <- levels(datasets[[1]][[var]])
  if (is.null(lvls)) return(NULL)

  imp_stats <- lapply(datasets, function(df) {
    fac_var <- factor(df[[var]], levels = lvls)
    fac_out <- factor(as.integer(df[[outcome]]), levels = c(0, 1))
    tab     <- table(fac_var, fac_out)
    colnames(tab) <- c("0", "1")

    if (!all(c("0", "1") %in% colnames(tab))) return(NULL)
    n0   <- sum(tab[, "0"])
    n1   <- sum(tab[, "1"])
    ntot <- n0 + n1
    if (n0 == 0 || n1 == 0) return(NULL)

    p_all <- rowSums(tab) / ntot
    p0    <- tab[, "0"] / n0
    p1    <- tab[, "1"] / n1

    tab_nz <- tab[rowSums(tab) > 0, , drop = FALSE]
    chi2 <- tryCatch({
      if (nrow(tab_nz) < 2) NA_real_
      else suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab_nz, correct = FALSE)$statistic)
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)

    list(p_all = p_all, p0 = p0, p1 = p1,
         n0 = n0, n1 = n1, ntot = ntot,
         chi2 = chi2, k = length(lvls))
  })
  imp_stats <- Filter(Negate(is.null), imp_stats)
  if (length(imp_stats) == 0) return(NULL)

  n0   <- round(mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "n0")))
  n1   <- round(mean(sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "n1")))
  ntot <- n0 + n1

  safe_pool <- function(field) {
    mat <- sapply(imp_stats, function(s) {
      v   <- s[[field]]
      out <- setNames(rep(0, length(lvls)), lvls)
      out[names(v)] <- v
      out
    })
    if (is.null(dim(mat))) mat <- matrix(mat, nrow = length(lvls))
    rowMeans(mat)
  }

  pool_p_all <- safe_pool("p_all")
  pool_p0    <- safe_pool("p0")
  pool_p1    <- safe_pool("p1")

  chi2_vals <- sapply(imp_stats, `[[`, "chi2")
  chi2_vals <- chi2_vals[!is.na(chi2_vals)]
  pool_chi2 <- if (length(chi2_vals) > 0) mean(chi2_vals) else NA_real_
  k         <- imp_stats[[1]]$k
  df_chi    <- max(k - 1, 1)
  p_val     <- if (!is.na(pool_chi2))
    pchisq(pool_chi2, df = df_chi, lower.tail = FALSE) else NA_real_

  denom_k <- max(min(k, 2) - 1, 1)
  v    <- if (!is.na(pool_chi2) && ntot > 0)
    min(sqrt(pool_chi2 / (ntot * denom_k)), 1) else NA_real_
  se_v <- if (!is.na(v)) sqrt((1 - v^2)^2 / (ntot - 1)) else NA_real_

  level_rows <- data.frame(
    level       = lvls,
    overall_n   = round(pool_p_all * ntot),
    overall_pct = round(pool_p_all * 100, 1),
    cont_n      = round(pool_p0 * n0),
    cont_pct    = round(pool_p0 * 100, 1),
    stop_n      = round(pool_p1 * n1),
    stop_pct    = round(pool_p1 * 100, 1),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  level_rows$overall_desc <- sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", level_rows$overall_n, level_rows$overall_pct)
  level_rows$cont_desc    <- sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", level_rows$cont_n,    level_rows$cont_pct)
  level_rows$stop_desc    <- sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", level_rows$stop_n,    level_rows$stop_pct)

  list(
    type        = "categorical",
    levels      = level_rows,
    p_val       = p_val,
    effect_type = "Cramers_V",
    effect_size = if (!is.na(v))   round(v, 3)   else NA_real_,
    ci_low      = if (!is.na(v) && !is.na(se_v))
      round(max(0, v - 1.96 * se_v), 3) else NA_real_,
    ci_high     = if (!is.na(v) && !is.na(se_v))
      round(min(1, v + 1.96 * se_v), 3) else NA_real_
  )
}

#==============================================================================
# RUN POOLING FOR ALL VARIABLES
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("POOLING STATISTICS ACROSS ALL IMPUTATIONS\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

pooled_results <- list()

for (var in all_vars) {
  cat(sprintf("  Pooling: %s\n", var))
  if (var %in% cat_vars) {
    pooled_results[[var]] <- pool_categorical(var, all_datasets)
  } else {
    pooled_results[[var]] <- pool_continuous(var, all_datasets)
  }
}
cat("\n✓ Done pooling.\n\n")

#==============================================================================
# EFFECT SIZE INTERPRETATION
#==============================================================================

interpret_d <- function(d) {
  d <- abs(d)
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(d) ~ "N/A",
    d < 0.20 ~ "Negligible",
    d < 0.50 ~ "Small",
    d < 0.80 ~ "Medium",
    TRUE     ~ "Large"
  )
}

interpret_v <- function(v) {
  v <- abs(v)
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(v) ~ "N/A",
    v < 0.10 ~ "Negligible",
    v < 0.30 ~ "Small",
    v < 0.50 ~ "Medium",
    TRUE     ~ "Large"
  )
}

#==============================================================================
# BUILD RESULTS DATA FRAME (with FDR correction within families)
#==============================================================================

summary_rows <- map_df(all_vars, function(var) {
  res <- pooled_results[[var]]
  if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)

  fam    <- get_family(var)
  p      <- res$p_val
  es     <- res$effect_size
  etype  <- res$effect_type
  interp <- if (etype == "Cohens_d") interpret_d(es) else interpret_v(es)

  data.frame(
    variable       = var,
    Family         = fam,
    p_val          = p,
    effect_type    = etype,
    effect_size    = es,
    ci_low         = res$ci_low,
    ci_high        = res$ci_high,
    interpretation = interp,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

summary_rows <- summary_rows %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  mutate(p_fdr = p.adjust(p_val, method = "fdr")) %>%
  ungroup()

#==============================================================================
# FORMATTING HELPERS
#==============================================================================

format_p <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p)  ~ "",
    p < 0.001 ~ "< .001",
    TRUE      ~ sprintf("%.3f", p)
  )
}

format_sig <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p)  ~ "",
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE      ~ ""
  )
}

format_es <- function(etype, es, lo, hi) {
  if (is.na(es)) return("")
  if (etype == "Cohens_d")  return(sprintf("d = %+.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", es, lo, hi))
  if (etype == "Cramers_V") return(sprintf("V = %.2f [%.2f, %.2f]",  es, lo, hi))
  return("")
}

#==============================================================================
# BUILD DISPLAY TABLE
#==============================================================================

table_rows <- list()

for (var in all_vars) {
  res  <- pooled_results[[var]]
  srow <- summary_rows[summary_rows$variable == var, ]
  if (is.null(res) || nrow(srow) == 0) next

  fam <- srow$Family

  if (res$type == "continuous") {
    table_rows[[length(table_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Family         = fam,
      Variable       = var,
      is_subrow      = FALSE,
      Overall        = res$overall_desc,
      Continuers     = res$cont_desc,
      Stoppers       = res$stop_desc,
      p_uncorr       = format_p(srow$p_val),
      p_FDR          = format_p(srow$p_fdr),
      Sig            = format_sig(srow$p_fdr),
      Effect_Size    = format_es(srow$effect_type, srow$effect_size,
                                 srow$ci_low, srow$ci_high),
      Interpretation = srow$interpretation,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    table_rows[[length(table_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Family         = fam,
      Variable       = var,
      is_subrow      = FALSE,
      Overall        = "",
      Continuers     = "",
      Stoppers       = "",
      p_uncorr       = format_p(srow$p_val),
      p_FDR          = format_p(srow$p_fdr),
      Sig            = format_sig(srow$p_fdr),
      Effect_Size    = format_es(srow$effect_type, srow$effect_size,
                                 srow$ci_low, srow$ci_high),
      Interpretation = srow$interpretation,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    for (j in seq_len(nrow(res$levels))) {
      lv <- res$levels[j, ]
      table_rows[[length(table_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Family         = fam,
        Variable       = paste0("    ", lv$level),
        is_subrow      = TRUE,
        Overall        = lv$overall_desc,
        Continuers     = lv$cont_desc,
        Stoppers       = lv$stop_desc,
        p_uncorr       = "",
        p_FDR          = "",
        Sig            = "",
        Effect_Size    = "",
        Interpretation = "",
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
}

display_table <- bind_rows(table_rows)

#==============================================================================
# PRINT TABLE 2
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("TABLE 2: GROUP COMPARISONS (Pooled MI — Rubin's Rules)\n")
cat(sprintf("  Continuers (stopped_bzra=0): n=%d  |  ", n_continuers))
cat(sprintf("Stoppers (stopped_bzra=1): n=%d\n", n_stoppers))
cat("  Cohen's d: positive = higher score in stoppers than continuers\n")
cat("  Cramer's V: always positive; direction from level percentages\n")
cat("  NOTE: stop_wdl_exp_combined has 3 levels (collapsed in Chunk 5+6):\n")
cat("        'No withdrawal' | 'Insomnia only' | 'Other withdrawal symptoms'\n")
cat("  NOTE: fall2 has 4 levels (0=No fall, 1=Minor, 2=Serious, 3=Fall no injury)\n")
cat("  NOTE: Sleep aid variables shown with full level breakdown.\n")
cat("        Review sparsity diagnostic above and collapse to binary if needed.\n")
cat("  NOTE: Personality reported at subscale level (DBAS_Total excluded).\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

print_table <- display_table %>%
  select(Family, Variable, Overall, Continuers, Stoppers,
         `p (uncorr)` = p_uncorr, `p (FDR)` = p_FDR, Sig,
         `Effect Size [95% CI]` = Effect_Size, Interpretation)

print(print_table, row.names = FALSE, na.print = "")
cat("\n")

out_table <- print_table
names(out_table)[names(out_table) == "Overall"]    <- sprintf("Overall (N=%d)", N)
names(out_table)[names(out_table) == "Continuers"] <- sprintf("Continuers (n=%d)", n_continuers)
names(out_table)[names(out_table) == "Stoppers"]   <- sprintf("Stoppers (n=%d)", n_stoppers)

write.csv(out_table, "TABLE2_publication_ready_with_FDR.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: TABLE2_publication_ready_with_FDR.csv\n\n")

#==============================================================================
# TABLE 1: OVERALL SAMPLE (pooled)
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("TABLE 1: OVERALL SAMPLE CHARACTERISTICS (Pooled MI)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

table1_rows <- list()
for (var in all_vars) {
  res <- pooled_results[[var]]
  if (is.null(res)) next

  if (res$type == "continuous") {
    table1_rows[[length(table1_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Variable = var, Overall = res$overall_desc, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  } else {
    table1_rows[[length(table1_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Variable = var, Overall = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    for (j in seq_len(nrow(res$levels))) {
      lv <- res$levels[j, ]
      table1_rows[[length(table1_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Variable = paste0("    ", lv$level),
        Overall  = lv$overall_desc,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
}

table1_display <- bind_rows(table1_rows)
print(table1_display, row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(table1_display, "TABLE1_overall_characteristics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Saved: TABLE1_overall_characteristics.csv\n\n")

#==============================================================================
# FDR SUMMARY BY FAMILY
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("FDR SUMMARY BY FAMILY\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

fdr_summary <- summary_rows %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarise(
    k         = n(),
    n_sig_raw = sum(!is.na(p_val) & p_val < 0.05),
    n_sig_fdr = sum(!is.na(p_fdr) & p_fdr < 0.05),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

print(fdr_summary, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")
write.csv(fdr_summary, "TABLE2_FDR_summary_by_family.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: TABLE2_FDR_summary_by_family.csv\n\n")

#==============================================================================
# SIGNIFICANT VARIABLES (FDR-corrected)
#==============================================================================

sig_vars <- summary_rows %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_fdr) & p_fdr < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(p_fdr)

if (nrow(sig_vars) > 0) {
  cat("SIGNIFICANT VARIABLES (FDR-corrected, p < .05):\n")
  cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  print(sig_vars %>%
          select(Family, variable, p_val, p_fdr, effect_type,
                 effect_size, ci_low, ci_high, interpretation),
        row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\n")
  write.csv(sig_vars, "TABLE2_significant_variables_FDR.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Saved: TABLE2_significant_variables_FDR.csv\n\n")
} else {
  cat("No variables significant after FDR correction.\n\n")
}

#==============================================================================
# NOTABLE EFFECT SIZES (medium or large, regardless of p-value)
#==============================================================================

notable <- summary_rows %>%
  filter(interpretation %in% c("Medium", "Large")) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(effect_size)))

if (nrow(notable) > 0) {
  cat("NOTABLE EFFECT SIZES (medium or large, regardless of significance):\n")
  cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  cat("  (Useful for identifying clinically meaningful differences\n")
  cat("   that may be underpowered for statistical detection)\n\n")
  print(notable %>%
          select(Family, variable, effect_type, effect_size,
                 ci_low, ci_high, interpretation, p_val, p_fdr),
        row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\n")
  write.csv(notable, "TABLE2_notable_effect_sizes.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Saved: TABLE2_notable_effect_sizes.csv\n\n")
}

#==============================================================================
# EFFECT SIZE SUMMARY TABLE (all variables, sorted by magnitude)
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("EFFECT SIZE SUMMARY (all variables, sorted by magnitude)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

es_summary <- summary_rows %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(effect_size))) %>%
  mutate(
    ES_formatted = dplyr::case_when(
      effect_type == "Cohens_d"  ~
        sprintf("d = %+.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", effect_size, ci_low, ci_high),
      effect_type == "Cramers_V" ~
        sprintf("V = %.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", effect_size, ci_low, ci_high),
      TRUE ~ "N/A"
    ),
    Sig_FDR = format_sig(p_fdr)
  ) %>%
  select(Family, variable, ES_formatted, interpretation, Sig_FDR, p_val, p_fdr)

print(es_summary, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")
write.csv(es_summary, "TABLE2_effect_sizes_all_variables.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: TABLE2_effect_sizes_all_variables.csv\n\n")

#==============================================================================
# MANUSCRIPT TEXT SNIPPET
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("MANUSCRIPT TEXT SNIPPET\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

age_res <- pooled_results[["age"]]
cat("Sample Characteristics:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
if (!is.null(age_res))
  cat(sprintf("The final sample comprised %d older adults (M age = %s years).\n",
              N, age_res$overall_desc))

sex_res <- pooled_results[["sex"]]
if (!is.null(sex_res)) {
  female_row <- sex_res$levels[sex_res$levels$level == "Female", ]
  if (nrow(female_row) > 0)
    cat(sprintf("The sample was %.1f%% female (n = %d).\n",
                female_row$overall_pct, female_row$overall_n))
}

cat(sprintf("\nOf the %d participants, %d (%.1f%%) were classified as stoppers\n",
            N, n_stoppers, 100 * n_stoppers / N))
cat(sprintf("(stopped completely [freq_dich = 0]: n = %d; rare use [freq_dich = 1]: n = %d)\n",
            sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
            sum(all_imputed[[1]]$freq_dich == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("and %d (%.1f%%) as continuers (regular use, freq_dich = 2).\n\n",
            n_continuers, 100 * n_continuers / N))

cat("Group Comparisons:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
if (nrow(sig_vars) > 0) {
  cat("After FDR correction within variable families (Benjamini & Hochberg, 1995),\n")
  cat(sprintf("stoppers differed significantly from continuers on %d variable(s):\n\n",
              nrow(sig_vars)))
  for (i in seq_len(min(5, nrow(sig_vars)))) {
    es_label <- ifelse(sig_vars$effect_type[i] == "Cohens_d", "d", "V")
    dir_note <- if (sig_vars$effect_type[i] == "Cohens_d" && sig_vars$effect_size[i] > 0) {
      "(higher in stoppers)"
    } else if (sig_vars$effect_type[i] == "Cohens_d") {
      "(higher in continuers)"
    } else ""
    cat(sprintf("  %s: p_FDR = %.4f, %s = %.2f %s [%s]\n",
                sig_vars$variable[i], sig_vars$p_fdr[i],
                es_label, sig_vars$effect_size[i],
                dir_note, sig_vars$interpretation[i]))
  }
  if (nrow(sig_vars) > 5) cat("  ... and", nrow(sig_vars) - 5, "more\n")
} else {
  cat("After FDR correction, no significant baseline differences were observed\n")
  cat("between stoppers and continuers.\n")
}

if (nrow(notable) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\nDespite %s statistically significant differences, %d variable(s) showed\n",
              ifelse(nrow(sig_vars) == 0, "no", "these"),
              nrow(notable)))
  cat("medium or large effect sizes that may be clinically meaningful:\n")
  for (i in seq_len(min(3, nrow(notable)))) {
    es_label <- ifelse(notable$effect_type[i] == "Cohens_d", "d", "V")
    cat(sprintf("  %s: %s = %.2f [%s]\n",
                notable$variable[i], es_label,
                notable$effect_size[i], notable$interpretation[i]))
  }
}

cat("\nStatistical Methods:\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat(sprintf("Descriptive statistics and group comparisons were pooled across all\n"))
cat(sprintf("%d imputed datasets using Rubin's (1987) Rules. BZRA discontinuation\n", m))
cat("status (stopped_bzra) was defined in Chunk 9: stoppers (freq_dich = 0\n")
cat("[stopped completely] or 1 [rarely uses]) vs. continuers (freq_dich = 2).\n\n")
cat("For continuous variables, pooled means and within-imputation SDs are\n")
cat("reported as M (SD). Group differences were tested using pooled t-statistics\n")
cat("with Barnard-Rubin degrees of freedom; effect sizes are Cohen's d, where\n")
cat("positive values indicate higher scores in the stopper group. For categorical\n")
cat("variables, pooled proportions are reported as n (%); differences were tested\n")
cat("using mean pooled chi-square statistics (Enders, 2010); effect sizes are\n")
cat("Cramer's V with direction inferred from level percentages.\n\n")
cat("Personality and COM-B constructs are reported at the subscale/composite\n")
cat("level (DBAS subscales: Consequences, Worry/Helplessness, Expectations,\n")
cat("Medications; BFI subscales: Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness,\n")
cat("Neuroticism, Openness; SURPS subscales: Hopelessness, Impulsivity,\n")
cat("Sensation Seeking, Anxiety Sensitivity; CISS subscales: Task, Emotion,\n")
cat("Avoidance; COM-B composites: benefitsC, side_effectsC, adlsC, dependenceC,\n")
cat("motivationC, capabilityC, pharmacistC, prescriberC).\n\n")
cat("Withdrawal experience (stop_wdl_exp_combined) was categorised into three\n")
cat("nominal levels: No withdrawal, Insomnia only, Other withdrawal symptoms\n")
cat("(anxiety, other mental health, physical, or multiple symptom types).\n")
cat("Fall severity (fall2) was categorised into four levels: No fall / not\n")
cat("injured, Minor injury, Serious injury, Fall but no injury.\n\n")
cat("Sleep aid frequency variables (alc_sleep, can_sleep, melatonin_use, op_sleep)\n")
cat("were treated as ordered factors (0=Never, 1=<1 day/week, 2=1-3 days/week,\n")
cat("3=4-6 days/week, 4=7 days/week). Sparsity was assessed prior to analysis;\n")
cat("if cell counts were insufficient for chi-square assumptions, variables were\n")
cat("collapsed to binary (Never vs Any use) and Fisher's exact test applied.\n\n")
cat("FDR correction (Benjamini & Hochberg, 1995) was applied within five variable\n")
cat(sprintf("families: demographics (k=%d), clinical (k=%d), sleep/adverse effects (k=%d),\n",
            sum(all_vars %in% demographics), sum(all_vars %in% clinical),
            sum(all_vars %in% sleep_adverse)))
cat(sprintf("beliefs/COM-B composites (k=%d), and personality subscales (k=%d).\n\n",
            sum(all_vars %in% beliefs_comob), sum(all_vars %in% personality)))

#==============================================================================
# COMPLETE
#==============================================================================

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("✓ CHUNK 10 COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. TABLE1_overall_characteristics.csv\n")
cat("  2. TABLE2_publication_ready_with_FDR.csv\n")
cat("  3. TABLE2_FDR_summary_by_family.csv\n")
cat("  4. TABLE2_effect_sizes_all_variables.csv\n")
if (nrow(sig_vars) > 0)
  cat("  5. TABLE2_significant_variables_FDR.csv\n")
if (nrow(notable) > 0)
  cat("  6. TABLE2_notable_effect_sizes.csv\n")

cat("\nCHANGES FROM PREVIOUS VERSION:\n")
cat("  ✓ bzra_daily_dose_eq (was bzra_daily_diazepam_eq)\n")
cat("  ✓ stop_wdl_exp_combined: 3 levels matching Chunk 5+6 labels\n")
cat("      'No withdrawal' | 'Insomnia only' | 'Other withdrawal symptoms'\n")
cat("  ✓ fall2: 4 levels including level 3 ('Fall but no injury')\n")
cat("  ✓ DBAS_Total removed — subscales only\n")
cat("  ✓ Individual Likert items removed — composites used instead\n")
cat("  ✓ Recode function cleaned of individual item coercions\n\n")

cat("NOTES:\n")
cat("  - All estimates pooled across", m, "imputations (Rubin's Rules)\n")
cat("  - Outcome: stopped_bzra from Chunk 9 (1=stopper, 0=continuer)\n")
cat("    NOT re-derived here — used directly from Chunk 9 Part B3\n")
cat("  - Continuers = freq_dich 2 (regular use >1x/month)\n")
cat("  - Stoppers   = freq_dich 0 (stopped) or 1 (rare use <1x/month)\n")
cat("  - Continuous: Mean (pooled within-imputation SD)\n")
cat("  - Categorical: n (pooled %)\n")
cat("  - Cohen's d: positive = higher in stoppers; negative = higher in continuers\n")
cat("  - Cramer's V: always positive; interpret direction from level percentages\n")
cat("  - Effect size magnitude: Negligible <.20/.10, Small .20-.49/.10-.29,\n")
cat("    Medium .50-.79/.30-.49, Large ≥.80/≥.50 (d/V thresholds)\n")
cat("  - BFI subscales: 2-item Spearman-Brown, not true Cronbach's alpha\n")
cat("  - alc_use: combined from sex-specific items (0=Never to 4=Very high)\n")
cat("  - prov_terr: AB/BC/MB/SK=Western, ON/QC=Central,\n")
cat("               NB/NL/NS/PE=Atlantic, YT/NT/NU=Territories\n")
cat("  - Sleep aid vars: ordered factor (0=Never to 4=7 days/week)\n")
cat("    Review sparsity diagnostic output before finalising analysis approach\n\n")

cat("REFERENCES:\n")
cat("  Rubin, D.B. (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.\n")
cat("  Li et al. (1991). Statistica Sinica, 1(1), 65-92.\n")
cat("  Enders, C.K. (2010). Applied Missing Data Analysis.\n")
cat("  Benjamini & Hochberg (1995). JRSS-B, 57(1), 289-300.\n")
cat("  Cohen, J. (1988). Statistical Power Analysis (2nd ed.).\n\n")
```





## PERS VSURF
```{r}
#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 11A: VSURF VARIABLE SELECTION (PERSONALITY PREDICTORS)
#==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   Select important personality variables for BZRA discontinuation using
#   VSURF applied separately to each imputed dataset.
#   Variables are retained if selected in ≥ 10 runs (≥ 25% of imputations),
#   applied uniformly to threshold, interpretation, and prediction sets.
#
# Alignment with Chunk 9 and Chunk 10:
#   - Outcome: stopped_bzra (created in Chunk 9, Part B3)
#       stopped_bzra = 1 : freq_dich 0 (stopped) or 1 (rare use)  → STOPPERS
#       stopped_bzra = 0 : freq_dich 2 (continues regular use)     → CONTINUERS
#   - stopped_bzra is used directly — NOT re-derived from freq_dich here.
#   - Personality variable set: identical to Chunk 10 personality block,
#     with DBAS_Total explicitly excluded (collinear with subscales).
#   - Labels: "Stoppers" vs "Continuers" consistent with Chunks 10.
#
# Strategy (MI-appropriate):
#   Run VSURF independently on each imputed dataset.
#   Apply majority vote (≥ 10 / m runs) separately for threshold,
#   interpretation, and prediction sets.
#   Use the PREDICTION set for downstream logistic regression.
#
# Rationale for prediction set (not interpretation set):
#   The interpretation set maximises explanatory coverage; the prediction set
#   is the minimal subset that preserves predictive accuracy. With limited
#   events-per-variable headroom, parsimony is required.
#
# Majority-vote threshold:
#   ≥ 10 runs (≥ 25% of imputations), applied to all three VSURF sets.
#   m is read from the RDS file — threshold is computed as ceiling(m * 0.25).
#
# References:
#   Genuer R, Poggi J-M, Tuleau-Malot C (2015). VSURF: An R Package for
#     Variable Selection Using Random Forests. R Journal, 7(2):19-33.
#   Rubin, D.B. (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.
#==============================================================================

library(VSURF)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 11A: VSURF VARIABLE SELECTION (PERSONALITY)\n")
cat("MI-appropriate: majority vote across all imputations\n")
cat("Outcome: stopped_bzra (from Chunk 9 Part B3)\n")
cat("Threshold: >= 25% of imputations (applied to all VSURF sets)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOAD DATA
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!file.exists("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds not found. Run Chunk 9 first.")

all_imputed <- readRDS("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds")
m           <- length(all_imputed)
N           <- nrow(all_imputed[[1]])

cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Imputations (m): %d\n", m))
cat(sprintf("  Observations (N): %d\n\n", N))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFY OUTCOME VARIABLE FROM CHUNK 9
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!"stopped_bzra" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]]))
  stop("ERROR: 'stopped_bzra' not found — check Chunk 9 Part B3 ran successfully.")

if (!"freq_dich" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]]))
  stop("ERROR: 'freq_dich' not found — check Chunk 8 Part F ran successfully.")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RECODE FUNCTION
# stopped_bzra is used directly from Chunk 9 — no re-derivation.
# Factor labels and groupings below applied for display/consistency only.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

recode_dataset <- function(df) {

  # ── stopped_bzra: already present from Chunk 9, just verify ─────────────────
  if (!"stopped_bzra" %in% names(df))
    stop("ERROR: 'stopped_bzra' missing from imputed dataset — check Chunk 9.")

  # ── Sex ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("sex" %in% names(df))
    df$sex <- factor(df$sex, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Female", "Male"))

  # ── Province/Territory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("prov_terr" %in% names(df)) {
    df$prov_terr <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$prov_terr %in% c(1, 2, 3, 12)  ~ "Western Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(9, 11)        ~ "Central Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(4, 5, 7, 10) ~ "Atlantic Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(6, 8, 13)  ~ "Territories",
      TRUE                              ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$prov_terr <- factor(df$prov_terr,
      levels = c("Western Canada", "Central Canada",
                 "Atlantic Canada", "Territories"))
  }

  # ── Employment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("employment" %in% names(df)) {
    df$employment <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$employment %in% c(0, 3, 4) ~ "Not in Workforce",
      df$employment %in% c(1, 2)   ~ "Full- or Part-Time Work",
      TRUE                          ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$employment <- factor(df$employment,
      levels = c("Full- or Part-Time Work", "Not in Workforce"))
  }

  # ── Education ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("education" %in% names(df)) {
    df$education <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$education %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ "High School or Less",
      df$education %in% c(4, 5)   ~ "Trade School/University Degree",
      TRUE                          ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$education <- factor(df$education,
      levels = c("High School or Less", "Trade School/University Degree"))
  }

  # ── Income ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("income" %in% names(df))
    df$income <- factor(df$income, levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("$15,000 or less", "$15,000 to $30,000", "$30,000 to $50,000",
                 "$50,000 to $80,000", "$80,000 or more"))

  # ── Driving frequency ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("driving_freq" %in% names(df))
    df$driving_freq <- factor(df$driving_freq, levels = 0:3,
      labels = c("Do not drive", "Less than 1 day/week",
                 "1-3 days/week", "4-7 days/week"))

  # ── General health ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("gen_health" %in% names(df))
    df$gen_health <- factor(as.numeric(df$gen_health), levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("Poor", "Fair", "Good", "Very good", "Excellent"))

  # ── Alcohol use ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("alc_use" %in% names(df))
    df$alc_use <- factor(df$alc_use, levels = 0:4,
      labels = c("Never", "Low (<1 drink/wk)", "Moderate (1-7 drinks/wk)",
                 "High (8-14 drinks/wk)", "Very high (>14 drinks/wk)"))

  # ── Withdrawal experience ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("stop_wdl_exp_combined" %in% names(df))
    df$stop_wdl_exp_combined <- factor(df$stop_wdl_exp_combined, levels = 0:5,
      labels = c("No withdrawal", "Insomnia only", "Anxiety only",
                 "Other MH symptoms only", "Physical symptoms only",
                 "Multiple symptoms"))

  # ── Fall injury severity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("fall2" %in% names(df))
    df$fall2 <- factor(df$fall2, levels = 0:2,
      labels = c("No fall / not injured", "Minor injury", "Serious injury"))

  # ── Ensure personality subscales are numeric ─────────────────────────────────
  personality_numeric_vars <- c(
    "BFI_Extraversion", "BFI_Agreeableness", "BFI_Conscientiousness",
    "BFI_Neuroticism", "BFI_Openness",
    "SURPS_Impulsivity", "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking",
    "SURPS_Hopelessness", "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity",
    "DBAS_Consequences", "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness",
    "DBAS_Expectations", "DBAS_Medications",
    "CISS_Task", "CISS_Emotion", "CISS_Avoidance"
  )
  for (var in personality_numeric_vars)
    if (var %in% names(df) && !is.numeric(df[[var]]))
      df[[var]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df[[var]]))

  df
}

# Apply recoding to every imputed dataset
all_datasets <- lapply(all_imputed, recode_dataset)
cat(sprintf("✓ Recoded all %d imputed datasets\n\n", m))

d1 <- all_datasets[[1]]

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# OUTCOME SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

n_stoppers   <- sum(d1$stopped_bzra == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
n_continuers <- sum(d1$stopped_bzra == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_outcome_na <- sum(is.na(d1$stopped_bzra))

cat("OUTCOME: stopped_bzra (from Chunk 9 Part B3)\n")
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  stopped_bzra = 1 : freq_dich 0 (stopped) or 1 (rare use)  → STOPPERS\n")
cat("  stopped_bzra = 0 : freq_dich 2 (continues regular use)     → CONTINUERS\n\n")
cat(sprintf("  Stoppers   (stopped_bzra = 1): n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_stoppers,   100 * n_stoppers   / N))
cat(sprintf("  Continuers (stopped_bzra = 0): n = %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_continuers, 100 * n_continuers / N))
if (n_outcome_na > 0)
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  Missing stopped_bzra: n = %d\n", n_outcome_na))
cat("\n")

# Verify stopped_bzra is consistent across imputations (it should be,
# since freq_dich was never imputed)
ref_outcome   <- all_imputed[[1]]$stopped_bzra
out_consistent <- all(sapply(all_imputed, function(d)
  identical(d$stopped_bzra, ref_outcome)))
if (out_consistent) {
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ stopped_bzra identical across all %d imputations (as expected)\n\n", m))
} else {
  cat("  ⚠️  stopped_bzra differs across imputations — investigate before proceeding\n\n")
}

n_events <- n_stoppers
cat(sprintf("Events for EPV calculations: %d (stoppers)\n\n", n_events))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PERSONALITY VARIABLE SET
# Identical to Chunk 10 personality block.
# DBAS_Total explicitly excluded: collinear with subscales.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PERSONALITY VARIABLE SET\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

personality_vars <- c(
  # BFI-10 (Big Five Inventory — 2 items per trait, Spearman-Brown reliability)
  "BFI_Extraversion", "BFI_Agreeableness", "BFI_Conscientiousness",
  "BFI_Neuroticism", "BFI_Openness",

  # SURPS (Substance Use Risk Profile Scale — 4 subscales)
  "SURPS_Impulsivity", "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking",
  "SURPS_Hopelessness", "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity",

  # DBAS-16 subscales (dysfunctional beliefs about sleep — 4 subscales)
  # DBAS_Total excluded: collinear with subscales
  "DBAS_Consequences", "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness",
  "DBAS_Expectations", "DBAS_Medications",

  # CISS (Coping Inventory for Stressful Situations — 3 subscales)
  "CISS_Task", "CISS_Emotion", "CISS_Avoidance"
)

cat(sprintf("Total personality variables: %d\n", length(personality_vars)))
cat("  BFI-10 (5): Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Neuroticism, Openness\n")
cat("  SURPS  (4): Impulsivity, Sensation_Seeking, Hopelessness, Anxiety_Sensitivity\n")
cat("  DBAS   (4): Consequences, Worry_Helplessness, Expectations, Medications\n")
cat("  CISS   (3): Task, Emotion, Avoidance\n")
cat("  DBAS_Total excluded (collinear with subscales)\n\n")

missing_vars <- personality_vars[!personality_vars %in% names(d1)]
if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
  cat("✗ MISSING variables (check Chunk 9):\n")
  for (v in missing_vars) cat("  •", v, "\n")
  stop("Cannot proceed — personality subscales missing from imputed data.")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("✓ All %d personality variables confirmed in imputed data\n\n",
              length(personality_vars)))
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MAJORITY-VOTE THRESHOLD
# ≥ 25% of imputations, applied uniformly to all three VSURF sets.
# Computed from m so it scales automatically regardless of how many
# imputations are in the RDS file.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

majority_threshold <- ceiling(m * 0.25)

cat("MAJORITY-VOTE THRESHOLD:\n")
cat(sprintf("  >= 25%% of %d imputations = >= %d runs\n", m, majority_threshold))
cat("  Applied uniformly to threshold, interpretation, and prediction sets.\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EVENTS-PER-VARIABLE CHECK
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

epv <- round(n_events / length(personality_vars), 1)

cat("EPV CHECK (full input set):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Stoppers (events): %d\n", n_events))
cat(sprintf("  Continuers:        %d\n", n_continuers))
cat(sprintf("  Input variables:   %d\n", length(personality_vars)))
cat(sprintf("  EPV:               %.1f\n", epv))
if (epv < 5) {
  cat("  ⚠️  EPV < 5 — interpret VSURF results cautiously\n")
} else if (epv < 10) {
  cat("  ~  EPV < 10 — moderate risk of overfitting\n")
} else {
  cat("  ✓ EPV >= 10 — acceptable for modeling\n")
}
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MAJORITY-VOTE VSURF ACROSS ALL IMPUTATIONS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat(sprintf("RUNNING VSURF ON ALL %d IMPUTATIONS\n", m))
cat("(this will take several minutes)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

# Storage
thres_list  <- vector("list", m)
interp_list <- vector("list", m)
pred_list   <- vector("list", m)
imp_list    <- vector("list", m)
failed_runs <- integer(0)

start_time <- Sys.time()

for (i in seq_len(m)) {

  cat(sprintf("  Imputation %2d / %d ... ", i, m))

  df <- all_datasets[[i]]

  # Predictor matrix — personality vars only, all numeric
  X <- as.data.frame(lapply(df[, personality_vars, drop = FALSE], as.numeric))

  # Outcome — stopped_bzra as factor, from Chunk 9 directly
  y <- factor(df[["stopped_bzra"]], levels = c(0, 1),
              labels = c("Continuer", "Stopper"))

  # Drop any rows with NA outcome (should be none post-imputation)
  ok <- !is.na(y)
  X  <- X[ok, , drop = FALSE]
  y  <- droplevels(y[ok])

  set.seed(12345 + i)   # reproducible but distinct per imputation

  vsurf_i <- tryCatch(
    VSURF(
      x        = X,
      y        = y,
      ntree    = 2000,
      mtry     = max(floor(ncol(X) / 3), 1),
      parallel = FALSE,
      verbose  = FALSE
    ),
    error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("FAILED (%s)\n", e$message))
      NULL
    }
  )

  if (is.null(vsurf_i)) {
    failed_runs      <- c(failed_runs, i)
    thres_list[[i]]  <- character(0)
    interp_list[[i]] <- character(0)
    pred_list[[i]]   <- character(0)
    imp_list[[i]]    <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(personality_vars)),
                                  personality_vars)
    next
  }

  thres_list[[i]]  <- personality_vars[vsurf_i$varselect.thres]
  interp_list[[i]] <- personality_vars[vsurf_i$varselect.interp]
  pred_list[[i]]   <- personality_vars[vsurf_i$varselect.pred]

  # Store mean importance across all variables
  imp_vec <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(personality_vars)), personality_vars)
  imp_vec[personality_vars] <- vsurf_i$imp.mean.dec
  imp_list[[i]] <- imp_vec

  cat(sprintf("thres=%d, interp=%d, pred=%d\n",
              length(thres_list[[i]]),
              length(interp_list[[i]]),
              length(pred_list[[i]])))
}

end_time   <- Sys.time()
time_taken <- round(difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "mins"), 2)
cat(sprintf("\n✓ VSURF completed in %.1f minutes\n", time_taken))
if (length(failed_runs) > 0)
  cat(sprintf("⚠️  Failed runs: %s\n", paste(failed_runs, collapse = ", ")))
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MAJORITY VOTE
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("MAJORITY-VOTE RESULTS\n")
cat(sprintf("Threshold: >= %d / %d runs (>= 25%% of imputations)\n",
            majority_threshold, m))
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

count_selections <- function(sel_list, varnames) {
  counts <- setNames(integer(length(varnames)), varnames)
  for (s in sel_list)
    counts[intersect(s, varnames)] <- counts[intersect(s, varnames)] + 1L
  sort(counts, decreasing = TRUE)
}

thres_counts  <- count_selections(thres_list,  personality_vars)
interp_counts <- count_selections(interp_list, personality_vars)
pred_counts   <- count_selections(pred_list,   personality_vars)

# Variables meeting the unified majority threshold
majority_thres  <- names(thres_counts[ thres_counts  >= majority_threshold])
majority_interp <- names(interp_counts[interp_counts >= majority_threshold])
majority_pred   <- names(pred_counts[  pred_counts   >= majority_threshold])

# Average importance across successful runs
valid_imp <- imp_list[!sapply(imp_list, function(x) all(is.na(x)))]
mean_imp  <- if (length(valid_imp) > 0) {
  imp_mat <- do.call(rbind, valid_imp)
  colMeans(imp_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
} else setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(personality_vars)), personality_vars)

importance_df <- data.frame(
  Variable        = personality_vars,
  Mean_Importance = mean_imp[personality_vars],
  Thres_Count     = thres_counts[personality_vars],
  Interp_Count    = interp_counts[personality_vars],
  Pred_Count      = pred_counts[personality_vars],
  Majority_Thres  = personality_vars %in% majority_thres,
  Majority_Interp = personality_vars %in% majority_interp,
  Majority_Pred   = personality_vars %in% majority_pred,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% arrange(desc(Mean_Importance))

cat("SELECTION COUNTS (out of", m, "runs):\n\n")
print(importance_df %>%
        select(Variable, Mean_Importance, Thres_Count, Interp_Count, Pred_Count),
      row.names = FALSE, digits = 4)
cat("\n")

# Report majority sets
cat(sprintf("MAJORITY THRESHOLD SET  (>=%d/%d): %d variables\n",
            majority_threshold, m, length(majority_thres)))
for (v in majority_thres)
  cat(sprintf("  • %s  (%d/%d)\n", v, thres_counts[v], m))
cat("\n")

cat(sprintf("MAJORITY INTERPRETATION SET (>=%d/%d): %d variables\n",
            majority_threshold, m, length(majority_interp)))
for (v in majority_interp)
  cat(sprintf("  • %s  (%d/%d)\n", v, interp_counts[v], m))
cat("\n")

cat(sprintf("MAJORITY PREDICTION SET (>=%d/%d): %d variables\n",
            majority_threshold, m, length(majority_pred)))
cat("  -> USE THESE FOR LOGISTIC REGRESSION\n")
for (v in majority_pred)
  cat(sprintf("  • %s  (%d/%d)\n", v, pred_counts[v], m))
cat("\n")

# EPV check on prediction set
if (length(majority_pred) > 0) {
  epv_pred <- round(n_events / length(majority_pred), 1)
  cat(sprintf("EPV with prediction set: %d stoppers / %d predictors = %.1f\n",
              n_events, length(majority_pred), epv_pred))
  if (epv_pred >= 10)
    cat("✓ EPV >= 10 — adequate for logistic regression\n\n")
  else if (epv_pred >= 5)
    cat("~  EPV 5-9 — acceptable; interpret with caution\n\n")
  else
    cat("⚠️  EPV < 5 — consider further reduction before regression\n\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠️  No variables met the majority threshold in prediction set.\n")
  cat(sprintf("   Review pred_counts above — consider lowering threshold below %d.\n\n",
              majority_threshold))
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DOMAIN BREAKDOWN
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("SELECTION BY PERSONALITY DOMAIN\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

get_domain <- function(vars) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    grepl("^BFI_",   vars) ~ "BFI",
    grepl("^SURPS_", vars) ~ "SURPS",
    grepl("^DBAS_",  vars) ~ "DBAS",
    grepl("^CISS_",  vars) ~ "CISS",
    TRUE                   ~ "Other"
  )
}

domain_totals <- c(BFI = 5, SURPS = 4, DBAS = 4, CISS = 3)

if (length(majority_pred) > 0) {
  pred_domain_df <- data.frame(
    Variable = majority_pred,
    Domain   = get_domain(majority_pred),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  domain_summary <- pred_domain_df %>%
    count(Domain, name = "N_Selected") %>%
    mutate(N_Total = domain_totals[Domain],
           Pct     = round(100 * N_Selected / N_Total, 1))

  cat("Prediction set — by domain:\n")
  print(domain_summary, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠️  No variables in prediction set to summarise by domain.\n\n")
  domain_summary <- data.frame()
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VISUALIZATION
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("Creating visualizations...\n")

# Plot 1: Mean importance with selection counts
plot_data <- importance_df %>%
  mutate(
    Domain    = get_domain(Variable),
    In_Pred   = Majority_Pred
  )

p1 <- ggplot(plot_data,
             aes(x     = reorder(Variable, Mean_Importance),
                 y     = Mean_Importance,
                 fill  = Domain,
                 alpha = In_Pred)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d/%d", Pred_Count, m)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.2) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 1.0, "FALSE" = 0.35),
                     labels = c("TRUE" = "In majority prediction set",
                                "FALSE" = "Not selected"),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Domain") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Variable Importance — Personality Predictors of BZRA Discontinuation",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "VSURF majority vote across %d imputations  |  Stoppers (n=%d) vs Continuers (n=%d)  |  Prediction set: %d variable(s)",
      m, n_stoppers, n_continuers, length(majority_pred)),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean Variable Importance (averaged across imputations)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 9),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

ggsave("VSURF_personality_importance.png", p1, width = 11, height = 8, dpi = 300)
cat("✓ Saved: VSURF_personality_importance.png\n")

# Plot 2: Selection stability heatmap across VSURF steps
stability_data <- importance_df %>%
  select(Variable, Thres_Count, Interp_Count, Pred_Count) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols      = -Variable,
               names_to  = "Step",
               values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Step = factor(Step,
                  levels = c("Thres_Count", "Interp_Count", "Pred_Count"),
                  labels = c("Threshold", "Interpretation", "Prediction")),
    Variable = factor(Variable,
                      levels = importance_df$Variable[order(importance_df$Mean_Importance)])
  )

p2 <- ggplot(stability_data, aes(x = Step, y = Variable, fill = Count)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), size = 3.2, colour = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = length(personality_vars) - length(majority_pred) + 0.5,
    colour = "black", linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#d9f0d3", high = "#1a7340",
                      limits = c(0, m), name = "Times\nselected") +
  labs(
    title    = sprintf("VSURF Selection Stability Across %d Imputations", m),
    subtitle = sprintf("Dashed line = majority threshold (%d/%d, >= 25%% of imputations)",
                       majority_threshold, m),
    x = "VSURF Step",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 10)
  )

ggsave("VSURF_personality_stability.png", p2, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)
cat("✓ Saved: VSURF_personality_stability.png\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SAVE RESULTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("SAVING RESULTS\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

vsurf_personality_output <- list(

  # FINAL SELECTIONS (majority vote)
  majority_pred    = majority_pred,      # <- USE FOR REGRESSION
  majority_interp  = majority_interp,
  majority_thres   = majority_thres,

  # Per-imputation raw selections
  pred_list        = pred_list,
  interp_list      = interp_list,
  thres_list       = thres_list,

  # Selection counts and importance
  importance_df    = importance_df,
  pred_counts      = pred_counts,
  interp_counts    = interp_counts,
  thres_counts     = thres_counts,

  # Domain summary
  domain_summary   = domain_summary,

  # Metadata
  metadata = list(
    input_vars          = personality_vars,
    outcome             = "stopped_bzra",
    outcome_source      = "Chunk 9 Part B3 — not re-derived here",
    outcome_coding      = "1=stopper (freq_dich 0 or 1), 0=continuer (freq_dich 2)",
    m                   = m,
    N                   = N,
    n_stoppers          = n_stoppers,
    n_continuers        = n_continuers,
    majority_threshold  = majority_threshold,
    pct_threshold       = 25,
    n_pred_selected     = length(majority_pred),
    n_interp_selected   = length(majority_interp),
    n_thres_selected    = length(majority_thres),
    failed_runs         = failed_runs,
    time_minutes        = as.numeric(time_taken),
    seed_base           = 12345
  )
)

saveRDS(vsurf_personality_output, "VSURF_personality_results.rds")
saveRDS(majority_pred,            "VSURF_personality_selected.rds")

cat("✓ Saved: VSURF_personality_results.rds\n")
cat("✓ Saved: VSURF_personality_selected.rds\n\n")

write.csv(importance_df, "VSURF_personality_importance_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: VSURF_personality_importance_table.csv\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FINAL SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 11A COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat(sprintf("VSURF ran on %d imputations (%d failed)\n",
            m, length(failed_runs)))
cat("Outcome: stopped_bzra (from Chunk 9 Part B3 — used directly)\n")
cat(sprintf("  1 = Stoppers (freq_dich 0 or 1): n = %d\n", n_stoppers))
cat(sprintf("  0 = Continuers (freq_dich 2):    n = %d\n\n", n_continuers))

cat(sprintf("Majority-vote threshold: >= %d / %d runs (>= 25%% of imputations)\n",
            majority_threshold, m))
cat("Applied uniformly to threshold, interpretation, and prediction sets.\n\n")

cat("FINAL SELECTIONS:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Threshold set:      %d variables\n", length(majority_thres)))
cat(sprintf("  Interpretation set: %d variables\n", length(majority_interp)))
cat(sprintf("  Prediction set:     %d variables  <- FOR REGRESSION\n\n",
            length(majority_pred)))

if (length(majority_pred) > 0) {
  cat("PREDICTION SET VARIABLES:\n")
  for (i in seq_along(majority_pred))
    cat(sprintf("  %d. %s  (selected in %d/%d runs)\n",
                i, majority_pred[i], pred_counts[majority_pred[i]], m))
  cat(sprintf("\n  EPV: %d stoppers / %d predictors = %.1f\n",
              n_events, length(majority_pred),
              n_events / length(majority_pred)))
} else {
  cat("⚠️  No variables selected in prediction set.\n")
  cat("   Load VSURF_personality_results.rds and review pred_counts\n")
  cat(sprintf("   to decide on a lower threshold (e.g., >= %d runs).\n",
              max(majority_threshold - 2L, 1L)))
}

cat("\n✓ Ready for Chunk 12A: logistic regression (personality model)\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. VSURF_personality_results.rds\n")
cat("  2. VSURF_personality_selected.rds\n")
cat("  3. VSURF_personality_importance_table.csv\n")
cat("  4. VSURF_personality_importance.png\n")
cat("  5. VSURF_personality_stability.png\n\n")

cat("NOTES:\n")
cat("  - Outcome: stopped_bzra from Chunk 9 Part B3 (NOT re-derived here)\n")
cat("  - Stoppers   = freq_dich 0 (stopped completely) or 1 (rare use)\n")
cat("  - Continuers = freq_dich 2 (continues regular use)\n")
cat("  - stopped_bzra is identical across all imputations (freq_dich never imputed)\n")
cat("  - DBAS_Total excluded (collinear with subscales)\n")
cat("  - BFI subscales: 2-item Spearman-Brown reliability\n")
cat("  - VSURF factor labels: 'Stopper' / 'Continuer'\n")
cat("  - Majority threshold computed as ceiling(m * 0.25) — auto-scales with m\n\n")

cat("REFERENCES:\n")
cat("  Genuer et al. (2015). VSURF. R Journal, 7(2):19-33.\n")
cat("  Rubin, D.B. (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.\n")
cat("  Cohen, J. (1988). Statistical Power Analysis (2nd ed.).\n\n")
```


## FULL VSURF
```{r}
#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 11B: VSURF VARIABLE SELECTION (ALL PREDICTORS — FULL MODEL)
#==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   Select important predictors of BZRA discontinuation from the full candidate
#   set, covering demographics, clinical, sleep/adverse effects, behavioural,
#   and personality domains.
#
# Alignment with Chunk 10:
#   - Outcome: stopped_bzra (loaded directly from Chunk 9 via imputed datasets,
#       identical to Chunk 10 — NOT re-derived here)
#       stopped_bzra = 1 : Stoppers/rare users (freq_dich 0 or 1)
#       stopped_bzra = 0 : Continuers (freq_dich 2, regular use)
#   - Variable sets: identical to Chunk 10 domain definitions
#       demographics, clinical, sleep_adverse, beliefs_comob, personality
#   - stop_wdl_exp_combined included in sleep_adverse (matches Chunk 10);
#       wdl_symptom_count also retained.
#   - COM-B composites (benefitsC, side_effectsC, adlsC, dependenceC,
#       motivationC, capabilityC, pharmacistC, prescriberC) included.
#   - DBAS_Total excluded (consistent with Chunk 10 and Chunk 11A)
#   - Labels: "Stoppers" vs "Continuers" consistent with Chunk 10
#
# Strategy (Option 1 — MI-appropriate):
#   Run VSURF independently on each of the m imputed datasets.
#   Majority-vote: >=25% of imputations (ceiling(m * 0.25)), applied
#                  uniformly to threshold, interpretation, and prediction sets.
#   Use the PREDICTION set for downstream logistic regression.
#
# VSURF settings:
#   ntree       = 2000  (trees per random forest)
#   nfor.thres  = 20    (iterations for threshold step)
#   nfor.interp = 20    (iterations for interpretation step)
#   nfor.pred   = 20    (iterations for prediction step)
#   mtry        = floor(sqrt(p))  (sqrt rule for classification)
#
# On withdrawal variables:
#   stop_wdl_exp_combined included as categorical predictor (consistent with
#   Chunk 10). Now 3-level unordered factor:
#     "No withdrawal" | "Insomnia only" | "Other withdrawal symptoms"
#   wdl_symptom_count also retained as continuous predictor.
#
# On fall2:
#   Levels 0-3 observed in data:
#     0 = No fall / not injured
#     1 = Minor injury
#     2 = Serious injury
#     3 = Fall but no injury (separate from level 0)
#   All four levels coded in recode_dataset() to prevent NAs.
#
# On BZRA dose variable:
#   Named bzra_daily_dose_eq (renamed from bzra_daily_diazepam_eq in
#   Chunk 5+6). Clinical vector updated accordingly.
#
# On DBAS_Total:
#   Excluded — collinear with subscales; consistent with Chunk 10 and 11A.
#
# References:
#   Genuer R, Poggi J-M, Tuleau-Malot C (2015). VSURF: An R Package for
#     Variable Selection Using Random Forests. R Journal, 7(2):19-33.
#   Rubin, D.B. (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.
#==============================================================================

library(VSURF)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 11B: VSURF VARIABLE SELECTION (FULL MODEL)\n")
# FIX: majority vote line now uses the dynamic m value loaded below,
# so it correctly reflects m=24 (or whatever m is in the loaded data)
# rather than the previously hardcoded 42.
cat("MI-appropriate: majority vote across all imputations\n")
cat("Outcome: stopped_bzra (from Chunk 9 — aligned with Chunk 10)\n")
cat("Threshold: >= 25% of imputations (applied to all VSURF sets)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOAD DATA
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!file.exists("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds not found. Run Chunk 9 first.")

all_imputed <- readRDS("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds")
m           <- length(all_imputed)
N           <- nrow(all_imputed[[1]])

cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Imputations (m): %d\n", m))
cat(sprintf("  Observations (N): %d\n\n", N))

# FIX: removed hardcoded check for m == 42; warning now fires for any
# unexpected m so the threshold calculation (ceiling(m * 0.25)) is always
# correct regardless of how many imputations were run.
if (m != 24)
  warning(sprintf("Expected 24 imputations but found %d — thresholds scale to m.", m))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFY OUTCOME VARIABLE FROM CHUNK 9
# stopped_bzra is used directly — NOT re-derived here.
# This is identical to the approach in Chunk 10.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!"stopped_bzra" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]]))
  stop("ERROR: 'stopped_bzra' not found — check Chunk 9 Part B3 ran successfully.")

if (!"freq_dich" %in% names(all_imputed[[1]]))
  stop("ERROR: 'freq_dich' not found — check Chunk 8 Part F ran successfully.")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RECODE FUNCTION — identical to Chunk 10
# stopped_bzra is already present from Chunk 9; no re-derivation needed.
# Factor labels and groupings applied for VSURF compatibility.
#
# FIX 1: stop_wdl_exp_combined now has 3 levels with text labels
#   (collapsed from 6 in Chunk 5+6). Recode matches on existing labels
#   rather than numeric levels to prevent NAs.
#
# FIX 2: bzra_daily_dose_eq (renamed from bzra_daily_diazepam_eq in
#   Chunk 5+6). Clinical vector updated to match.
#
# fall2 fix: levels 0-3 all observed in data; all four levels coded
#   explicitly to prevent NAs in VSURF.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

recode_dataset <- function(df) {

  # ── Sex ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("sex" %in% names(df))
    df$sex <- factor(df$sex, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Female", "Male"))

  # ── Province/Territory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("prov_terr" %in% names(df)) {
    df$prov_terr <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$prov_terr %in% c(1, 2, 3, 12)  ~ "Western Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(9, 11)        ~ "Central Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(4, 5, 7, 10)  ~ "Atlantic Canada",
      df$prov_terr %in% c(6, 8, 13)     ~ "Territories",
      TRUE                               ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$prov_terr <- factor(df$prov_terr,
      levels = c("Western Canada", "Central Canada",
                 "Atlantic Canada", "Territories"))
  }

  # ── Employment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("employment" %in% names(df)) {
    df$employment <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$employment %in% c(0, 3, 4) ~ "Not in Workforce",
      df$employment %in% c(1, 2)    ~ "Full- or Part-Time Work",
      TRUE                           ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$employment <- factor(df$employment,
      levels = c("Full- or Part-Time Work", "Not in Workforce"))
  }

  # ── Education ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("education" %in% names(df)) {
    df$education <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$education %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ "High School or Less",
      df$education %in% c(4, 5)    ~ "Trade School/University Degree",
      TRUE                           ~ NA_character_
    )
    df$education <- factor(df$education,
      levels = c("High School or Less", "Trade School/University Degree"))
  }

  # ── Income ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("income" %in% names(df))
    df$income <- factor(df$income, levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("$15,000 or less", "$15,000 to $30,000", "$30,000 to $50,000",
                 "$50,000 to $80,000", "$80,000 or more"))

  # ── Driving frequency ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("driving_freq" %in% names(df))
    df$driving_freq <- factor(df$driving_freq, levels = 0:3,
      labels = c("Do not drive", "Less than 1 day/week",
                 "1-3 days/week", "4-7 days/week"))

  # ── General health ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if ("gen_health" %in% names(df))
    df$gen_health <- factor(as.numeric(df$gen_health), levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("Poor", "Fair", "Good", "Very good", "Excellent"))

  # ── Alcohol use (combined ordinal 0-4) ──────────────────────────────────────
  if ("alc_use" %in% names(df))
    df$alc_use <- factor(df$alc_use, levels = 0:4,
      labels = c("Never", "Low (<1 drink/wk)", "Moderate (1-7 drinks/wk)",
                 "High (8-14 drinks/wk)", "Very high (>14 drinks/wk)"))

  # ── Withdrawal experience ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # FIX 1: Now 3-level unordered factor (collapsed in Chunk 5+6 Part B).
  # Labels match exactly what is stored in the imputed data.
  # Do NOT recode by numeric levels — the variable is already a labelled
  # factor; re-factoring by existing labels preserves values without NAs.
  if ("stop_wdl_exp_combined" %in% names(df))
    df$stop_wdl_exp_combined <- factor(
      df$stop_wdl_exp_combined,
```
