```{r}
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

output <- list(

  pooled_A         = pooled_A,
  pooled_B         = pooled_B,
  cv_results_A     = cv_results_A,
  cv_results_B     = cv_results_B,
  cv_by_imputation = cv_by_imputation,
  fold_A           = fold_A,
  fold_B           = fold_B,
  summary_table    = summary_table,

  metadata = list(
    outcome             = outcome_var,
    outcome_coding      = chunk12$metadata$outcome_coding,
    vsurf_set_used      = "interpretation (Step 2) for both models",
    scores_used         = "raw (original scale — consistent with Chunk 12 raw models)",
    vars_A              = vars_A,
    vars_B              = vars_B,
    n_folds             = n_folds,
    boot.n              = boot.n,
    m                   = m,
    N                   = N,
    seed_base           = 123,
    seed_boot_offset    = 10000,
    apparent_auc_source = "regression_comparison_results.rds (Chunk 12 raw models)"
  )
)

saveRDS(output,             "cv_results.rds")
write_csv(summary_table,    "cv_summary_table.csv")
write_csv(cv_by_imputation, "cv_by_imputation.csv")
write_csv(fold_A,           "cv_fold_stability_modelA.csv")
write_csv(fold_B,           "cv_fold_stability_modelB.csv")

cat("✓ Saved: cv_results.rds\n")
cat("✓ Saved: cv_summary_table.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: cv_by_imputation.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: cv_fold_stability_modelA.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: cv_fold_stability_modelB.csv\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 13 COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat(sprintf("Outcome: %s  |  Stoppers: %d  |  Continuers: %d\n\n",
            outcome_var,
            chunk12$metadata$n_stoppers,
            chunk12$metadata$n_continuers))

cat("VSURF sets used: Interpretation (Step 2) — both models\n")
cat("Scores used:     Raw (original scale)\n\n")

cat(sprintf("Model A (Personality): Apparent=%.3f  CV=%.3f [%.3f, %.3f]  Optimism=%.3f\n",
            apparent_auc_A,
            pooled_A$cv_mean, pooled_A$cv_lower, pooled_A$cv_upper,
            pooled_A$optimism))
cat(sprintf("Model B (Full):        Apparent=%.3f  CV=%.3f [%.3f, %.3f]  Optimism=%.3f\n\n",
            apparent_auc_B,
            pooled_B$cv_mean, pooled_B$cv_lower, pooled_B$cv_upper,
            pooled_B$optimism))

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. cv_results.rds\n")
cat("  2. cv_summary_table.csv\n")
cat("  3. cv_by_imputation.csv\n")
cat("  4. cv_fold_stability_modelA.csv\n")
cat("  5. cv_fold_stability_modelB.csv\n\n")

cat("NOTES:\n")
cat("  - Apparent AUC loaded from Chunk 12 raw models (no model refitting)\n")
cat("  - Per-imputation optimism uses per-imputation apparent AUC from\n")
cat("    Chunk 12 DeLong results — ensures correct alignment\n")
cat("  - CV fold seed = 123 + i per imputation; bootstrap seed = fold seed + 10000\n")
cat("  - Both models use VSURF interpretation set (Step 2)\n")
cat("  - Raw scores used throughout — predictors on original scale\n")
cat("  - Unseen factor levels in test folds (e.g. stop_wdl_exp_combined):\n")
cat("    affected rows assigned training-set base rate as prediction.\n")
cat("    Messages printed during CV report how many rows were affected.\n\n")

cat("REFERENCES:\n")
cat("  Steyerberg EW (2009). Clinical Prediction Models. Springer.\n")
cat("  Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.\n\n")

cat("✓ Ready for Chunk 14: reporting / visualisation\n\n")
```



## Influential Case
```{r}
sp_cols <- grep("^c_sp___", names(SIMOA), value = TRUE)
case_350 <- SIMOA[SIMOA$record_id == 350, ]
case_350[, sp_cols]

# Pull all raw clonazepam variables for Case 350
case_350[, grep("clon", names(SIMOA), value = TRUE)]

#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 14: INFLUENTIAL CASES ANALYSIS — MODEL A & MODEL B
#==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   Characterise and assess the robustness of influential cases separately
#   for Model A (Personality, interpretation set) and Model B (Full,
#   interpretation set).
#
# HOW COOK'S D IS CALCULATED:
#   Cook's distance (Cook, 1977) measures how much all fitted values change
#   when a single observation is deleted from the model. For logistic
#   regression, it is approximated as:
#
#     D_i = (h_ii / (p * (1 - h_ii)^2)) * r_i^2
#
#   where:
#     h_ii   = leverage of observation i (diagonal of the hat matrix),
#              reflecting how far i's predictor values are from the centroid
#     r_i    = Pearson residual for observation i — how far the observed
#              outcome is from the model's predicted probability
#     p      = number of parameters (including intercept)
#
#   Intuitively: Cook's D is large when an observation has BOTH unusual
#   predictor values (high leverage) AND a poor fit (large residual).
#   Either alone is not sufficient — a high-leverage point that is well
#   predicted has small D; a poorly-predicted point at the centroid of X
#   also has small D.
#
#   Across multiple imputations, Cook's D is computed separately on each
#   imputed dataset (because the imputed values of missing predictors differ
#   across datasets, so leverage and residuals will vary slightly). The MEAN
#   Cook's D across all m imputations is used to flag cases, which is more
#   stable than a single-imputation estimate. The min-max range across
#   imputations indicates how consistently a case is influential: a small
#   range means the case is influential regardless of which imputed values
#   it received, strengthening the conclusion that its influence is real
#   rather than a product of a single imputation's values.
#
#   Threshold: 4/N (Cook & Weisberg, 1982). Cases whose MEAN Cook's D
#   exceeds this threshold are flagged as influential.
#
# Influential case identification:
#   Cook's D computed per imputation; mean Cook's D averaged across all
#   imputations. Cases with mean Cook's D > 4/N flagged as influential.
#   The same case IDs are removed consistently from all datasets for
#   the removal sensitivity analysis.
#
# Descriptive profiling (Parts C-F):
#   Continuous variables: statistics pooled across all imputations.
#   Factor variables (e.g. stop_wdl_exp_combined): frequency counts and
#   percentages per level, pooled across imputations. Pooled t-tests
#   replaced by Fisher's exact test for factors.
#
# Scenario classification (Part F):
#   Data-driven thresholds (pooled across imputations):
#   - Continuous: predictor flagged if inf/non-inf top OR bottom quartile
#     rate ratio >= 1.5 (directional).
#   - Factor: predictor flagged if ANY level has inf/non-inf prevalence
#     ratio >= 1.5. Flagged level(s) reported.
#   Scenario assigned by proportion of flagged predictors:
#     >= 75% flagged -> Scenario 1 (coherent clinical subgroup)
#     25-74% flagged -> Scenario 2 (partial / heterogeneous)
#     <  25% flagged -> Scenario 3 (statistical outliers)
#
# Removal sensitivity (Part G):
#   Model refit on all imputed datasets with influential cases removed.
#   Coefficients pooled using manual Rubin's rules.
#   Pooled ORs compared to Chunk 12 RAW pooled ORs (model_A_raw /
#   model_B_raw from regression_comparison_results.rds).
#   NOTE: term column cast to character before joining to avoid Factor
#   mismatch with pool_glm_manual output (which returns character terms).
#
# Overlap analysis (Parts H-I):
#   Three groups of influential cases are distinguished:
#     - Overlap: cases influential in BOTH Model A and Model B
#     - A-only:  cases influential in Model A but not Model B
#     - B-only:  cases influential in Model B but not Model A
#
#   Overlap cases are the most analytically important: they exert undue
#   influence regardless of predictor domain, suggesting their influence
#   is not domain-specific but reflects something fundamental about those
#   participants (e.g. extreme outcome values, sparse covariate profiles).
#
#   Part H: Standard four-group overlap analysis (Cook's D, outcome rates,
#           predictor profiles across all four groups).
#
#   Part I (new): Overlap-specific deep-dive.
#     I1. Why do overlap cases appear in both models?
#         Compares Cook's D between overlap and single-model cases per model.
#         High D in both models indicates domain-general influence.
#     I2. What distinguishes A-only from B-only influential cases?
#         Profiles predictors unique to each model for that model's
#         single-model influential cases. Tests whether A-only cases are
#         unusual on personality predictors and B-only cases unusual on
#         non-personality predictors.
#     I3. Do overlap cases disproportionately drive non-robust findings?
#         Refits each model after removing overlap cases only and checks
#         whether non-robust predictors from Part G lose significance.
#         If yes, the non-robust finding is anchored to the overlap group
#         rather than to the full influential set.
#
# Robustness classification:
#   When separation_flag = TRUE AND m_converged = 0, classified as non-robust.
#   When separation_flag = TRUE AND m_converged >= 1, classified as
#   "Separation in reduced sample — interpret cautiously".
#
# References:
#   Cook RD (1977). Detection of influential observations. Technometrics.
#   Cook RD, Weisberg S (1982). Residuals and Influence in Regression.
#   Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.
#==============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(pROC)
library(mice)
library(broom)
library(car)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 14: INFLUENTIAL CASES ANALYSIS\n")
cat("Model A (Personality) and Model B (Full) — interpretation set\n")
cat("Raw-score models used (consistent with Chunk 12)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Cook's D measures how much all fitted values change when a single\n")
cat("observation is deleted. It combines leverage (how unusual the\n")
cat("predictor values are) with residual size (how poorly the model\n")
cat("predicts that observation): D_i = (h_ii / (p*(1-h_ii)^2)) * r_i^2.\n")
cat("Computed per imputation; mean D across all imputations used for\n")
cat("flagging. Min-max range across imputations indicates stability.\n")
cat("Threshold: 4/N (Cook & Weisberg, 1982).\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PRINT HELPER — formats data frames as plain text via cat() only.
# Never calls print() or capture.output(), both of which RStudio intercepts
# and routes to the viewer pane in notebook/script contexts.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
print_df <- function(df) {
  df  <- as.data.frame(df)
  nms <- names(df)

  char_df <- as.data.frame(
    lapply(df, function(x) {
      if (is.numeric(x)) {
        formatted <- formatC(x, format = "g", digits = 5)
        ifelse(is.na(x), "NA", formatted)
      } else {
        ifelse(is.na(x), "NA", as.character(x))
      }
    }),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  names(char_df) <- nms

  widths <- mapply(function(col_name, col_vals) {
    max(nchar(col_name), max(nchar(col_vals), na.rm = TRUE))
  }, nms, char_df, SIMPLIFY = TRUE)

  header <- paste(mapply(formatC, nms,      width = widths, flag = "-"), collapse = "  ")
  sep    <- paste(mapply(function(w) paste(rep("-", w), collapse = ""),
                         widths), collapse = "  ")

  cat(header, "\n", sep = "")
  cat("\n")

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(char_df))) {
    row_str <- paste(mapply(formatC, as.character(char_df[i, ]),
                            width = widths, flag = "-"), collapse = "  ")
    cat(row_str, "\n", sep = "")
  }
  cat("\n")
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART A: LOAD DATA AND RESULTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART A: Loading data and Chunk 12 results\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

if (!file.exists("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds not found. Run Chunk 9 first.")

imputed_list <- readRDS("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds")
m            <- length(imputed_list)
N            <- nrow(imputed_list[[1]])

if (m != 24)
  warning(sprintf("Expected 24 imputations but found %d.", m))

if (!file.exists("regression_comparison_results.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: regression_comparison_results.rds not found. Run Chunk 12 first.")

chunk12     <- readRDS("regression_comparison_results.rds")
vars_A      <- chunk12$variables$model_A
vars_B      <- chunk12$variables$model_B
outcome_var <- chunk12$metadata$outcome

# FIX: Chunk 12 (updated) stores raw-score summaries under model_A_raw and
# model_B_raw. Cast term to character to avoid Factor/character join issues
# in pool_glm_manual downstream.
chunk12$summaries$model_A_raw <- chunk12$summaries$model_A_raw %>%
  mutate(term = as.character(term))
chunk12$summaries$model_B_raw <- chunk12$summaries$model_B_raw %>%
  mutate(term = as.character(term))

formula_A <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(vars_A, collapse = " + ")))
formula_B <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(vars_B, collapse = " + ")))

threshold <- 4 / N

is_factor_var <- function(vars, data) {
  sapply(vars, function(v) is.factor(data[[v]]))
}
factor_flags_A <- is_factor_var(vars_A, imputed_list[[1]])
factor_flags_B <- is_factor_var(vars_B, imputed_list[[1]])

cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds  (m=%d, N=%d)\n", m, N))
cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded regression_comparison_results.rds\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Using raw-score summaries: model_A_raw / model_B_raw\n\n"))
cat(sprintf("Outcome: %s\n", outcome_var))
cat(sprintf("Cook's D threshold: %.5f  (4 / %d)\n\n", threshold, N))
cat(sprintf("Model A predictors (%d): %s\n", length(vars_A),
            paste(vars_A, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("  Factor variables: %s\n\n",
            if (any(factor_flags_A)) paste(vars_A[factor_flags_A], collapse = ", ")
            else "none"))
cat(sprintf("Model B predictors (%d): %s\n", length(vars_B),
            paste(vars_B, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("  Factor variables: %s\n\n",
            if (any(factor_flags_B)) paste(vars_B[factor_flags_B], collapse = ", ")
            else "none"))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

get_cooks_stats <- function(imputed_list, formula_obj) {
  mm        <- length(imputed_list)
  NN        <- nrow(imputed_list[[1]])
  cooks_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = NN, ncol = mm)
  for (i in seq_len(mm)) {
    fit_i          <- glm(formula_obj, data = imputed_list[[i]], family = binomial())
    cooks_mat[, i] <- cooks.distance(fit_i)
  }
  list(
    mean = rowMeans(cooks_mat, na.rm = TRUE),
    min  = apply(cooks_mat, 1, min,  na.rm = TRUE),
    max  = apply(cooks_mat, 1, max,  na.rm = TRUE),
    mat  = cooks_mat
  )
}

pool_glm_manual <- function(fit_list) {
  fit_list <- fit_list[!sapply(fit_list, is.null)]
  m_valid  <- length(fit_list)

  coef_list <- lapply(fit_list, function(f) {
    cf <- summary(f)$coefficients
    data.frame(term     = rownames(cf),
               estimate = cf[, "Estimate"],
               se       = cf[, "Std. Error"],
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  })

  all_terms <- unique(unlist(lapply(coef_list, `[[`, "term")))

  bind_rows(lapply(all_terms, function(trm) {

    ests <- sapply(coef_list, function(d) {
      idx <- match(trm, d$term); if (is.na(idx)) NA_real_ else d$estimate[idx]
    })
    ses <- sapply(coef_list, function(d) {
      idx <- match(trm, d$term); if (is.na(idx)) NA_real_ else d$se[idx]
    })

    separation <- any(abs(ests) > 10 | ses > 5, na.rm = TRUE)
    valid      <- !is.na(ests) & abs(ests) <= 10 & ses <= 5
    m_use      <- sum(valid)

    if (m_use < 2)
      return(data.frame(term = trm, estimate = NA_real_, se_rubin = NA_real_,
                        OR = NA_real_, OR_lower = NA_real_, OR_upper = NA_real_,
                        OR_CI = "NA", p.value = NA_real_, sig = "?",
                        separation_flag = separation, m_converged = m_use,
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

    Q_bar <- mean(ests[valid]); U_bar <- mean(ses[valid]^2)
    B     <- var(ests[valid]);  T_var <- U_bar + (1 + 1 / m_use) * B
    se_r  <- sqrt(T_var);      z     <- Q_bar / se_r
    p_val <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(z))
    or <- exp(Q_bar); lo <- exp(Q_bar - 1.96 * se_r); hi <- exp(Q_bar + 1.96 * se_r)

    data.frame(
      term            = trm,
      estimate        = Q_bar, se_rubin = se_r,
      OR = or, OR_lower = lo, OR_upper = hi,
      OR_CI           = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", or, lo, hi),
      p.value         = p_val,
      sig             = case_when(p_val < 0.001 ~ "***", p_val < 0.01 ~ "**",
                                  p_val < 0.05  ~ "*",   p_val < 0.10 ~ ".", TRUE ~ ""),
      separation_flag = separation, m_converged = m_use,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }))
}

pooled_continuous_summary <- function(imputed_list, indices, var, label) {
  means <- sapply(imputed_list, function(d) mean(d[[var]][indices], na.rm = TRUE))
  sds   <- sapply(imputed_list, function(d) sd(d[[var]][indices],   na.rm = TRUE))
  data.frame(Variable = var, Group = label, Type = "continuous",
             Level = NA_character_, Mean = mean(means, na.rm = TRUE),
             SD = mean(sds, na.rm = TRUE), N_level = NA_real_, Pct = NA_real_,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

pooled_factor_summary <- function(imputed_list, indices, var, label) {
  levs  <- levels(imputed_list[[1]][[var]])
  n_sub <- length(indices)
  mm    <- length(imputed_list)

  counts_mat <- sapply(levs, function(lv)
    sapply(imputed_list, function(d)
      sum(d[[var]][indices] == lv, na.rm = TRUE)))

  mean_counts <- colMeans(counts_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_pct    <- 100 * mean_counts / n_sub

  data.frame(Variable = var, Group = label, Type = "factor", Level = levs,
             Mean = NA_real_, SD = NA_real_,
             N_level = round(mean_counts, 1), Pct = round(mean_pct, 1),
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

pooled_var_summary <- function(imputed_list, indices, var, label) {
  if (is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[var]]))
    pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, indices, var, label)
  else
    pooled_continuous_summary(imputed_list, indices, var, label)
}

pooled_predictor_summary <- function(imputed_list, indices, vars, label) {
  bind_rows(lapply(vars, function(v)
    pooled_var_summary(imputed_list, indices, v, label)))
}

pooled_comparison <- function(imputed_list, var, idx1, idx2) {
  if (is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[var]])) {
    d   <- imputed_list[[1]]
    tbl <- table(c(rep("Influential", length(idx1)),
                   rep("Non-influential", length(idx2))),
                 c(as.character(d[[var]][idx1]),
                   as.character(d[[var]][idx2])))
    ft  <- fisher.test(tbl, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 2000)
    list(type = "fisher", p_pool = ft$p.value, statistic = NA_real_)
  } else {
    t_stats <- sapply(imputed_list, function(d)
      t.test(d[[var]][idx1], d[[var]][idx2])$statistic)
    p_pool  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(mean(t_stats) /
                                sqrt(var(t_stats) * (1 + 1 / length(imputed_list)))))
    list(type = "t-test", p_pool = p_pool, statistic = mean(t_stats))
  }
}

pooled_percentile_dist <- function(imputed_list, indices, var) {
  if (is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[var]])) return(NULL)
  mm     <- length(imputed_list)
  breaks <- c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100)
  labs   <- c("0-25th", "25-50th", "50-75th", "75-100th")

  counts_mat <- matrix(0L, nrow = 4, ncol = mm, dimnames = list(labs, NULL))
  for (i in seq_len(mm)) {
    pctile <- ecdf(imputed_list[[i]][[var]])(imputed_list[[i]][[var]]) * 100
    tbl    <- table(cut(pctile[indices], breaks = breaks,
                        labels = labs, include.lowest = TRUE))
    counts_mat[, i] <- as.integer(tbl)
  }
  mc  <- rowMeans(counts_mat)
  data.frame(Quartile = labs, Mean_N = round(mc, 1),
             Mean_Pct = round(100 * mc / sum(mc), 1),
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

classify_scenario <- function(imputed_list, inf_idx, noninf_idx, model_vars) {
  mm <- length(imputed_list)

  ratio_rows <- lapply(model_vars, function(v) {

    if (is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]])) {
      levs <- levels(imputed_list[[1]][[v]])
      level_ratios <- sapply(levs, function(lv) {
        inf_p    <- mean(sapply(imputed_list, function(d)
          mean(d[[v]][inf_idx]    == lv, na.rm = TRUE)), na.rm = TRUE)
        noninf_p <- mean(sapply(imputed_list, function(d)
          mean(d[[v]][noninf_idx] == lv, na.rm = TRUE)), na.rm = TRUE)
        if (noninf_p > 0) inf_p / noninf_p else NA_real_
      })
      names(level_ratios) <- levs
      flagged_levs <- names(level_ratios)[!is.na(level_ratios) & level_ratios >= 1.5]
      flagged      <- length(flagged_levs) > 0
      direction    <- if (flagged)
        paste0("Level(s): ", paste(flagged_levs, collapse = ", ")) else "Not flagged"

      data.frame(Variable = v, Type = "factor",
                 Hi_Inf_Pct = NA_real_, Hi_NonInf_Pct = NA_real_, Hi_Ratio = NA_real_,
                 Lo_Inf_Pct = NA_real_, Lo_NonInf_Pct = NA_real_, Lo_Ratio = NA_real_,
                 Flagged = flagged, Direction = direction, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

    } else {
      hi_inf <- hi_ni <- lo_inf <- lo_ni <- numeric(mm)
      for (i in seq_len(mm)) {
        df  <- imputed_list[[i]]
        q75 <- quantile(df[[v]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
        q25 <- quantile(df[[v]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
        hi_inf[i] <- mean(df[[v]][inf_idx]    > q75, na.rm = TRUE)
        hi_ni[i]  <- mean(df[[v]][noninf_idx] > q75, na.rm = TRUE)
        lo_inf[i] <- mean(df[[v]][inf_idx]    < q25, na.rm = TRUE)
        lo_ni[i]  <- mean(df[[v]][noninf_idx] < q25, na.rm = TRUE)
      }
      him <- mean(hi_inf); hin <- mean(hi_ni)
      lom <- mean(lo_inf); lon <- mean(lo_ni)
      hi_r <- if (hin > 0) him / hin else NA_real_
      lo_r <- if (lon > 0) lom / lon else NA_real_
      flagged   <- (!is.na(hi_r) && hi_r >= 1.5) || (!is.na(lo_r) && lo_r >= 1.5)
      direction <- case_when(
        !is.na(hi_r) && hi_r >= 1.5 && (is.na(lo_r) || hi_r >= lo_r) ~ "High extreme",
        !is.na(lo_r) && lo_r >= 1.5 ~ "Low extreme",
        TRUE ~ "Not flagged")

      data.frame(Variable = v, Type = "continuous",
                 Hi_Inf_Pct = round(100*him,1), Hi_NonInf_Pct = round(100*hin,1),
                 Hi_Ratio = round(hi_r,2),
                 Lo_Inf_Pct = round(100*lom,1), Lo_NonInf_Pct = round(100*lon,1),
                 Lo_Ratio = round(lo_r,2),
                 Flagged = flagged, Direction = direction, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }
  })

  ratio_df    <- bind_rows(ratio_rows)
  n_flagged   <- sum(ratio_df$Flagged)
  pct_flagged <- 100 * n_flagged / length(model_vars)
  scenario    <- case_when(
    pct_flagged >= 75 ~ "SCENARIO 1: Coherent clinical subgroup (>= 75% predictors flagged)",
    pct_flagged >= 25 ~ "SCENARIO 2: Heterogeneous / partial profile (25-74% predictors flagged)",
    TRUE              ~ "SCENARIO 3: Statistical outliers — no coherent profile (< 25% flagged)")

  list(ratio_df = ratio_df, n_flagged = n_flagged,
       pct_flagged = pct_flagged, scenario = scenario)
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART B: IDENTIFY INFLUENTIAL CASES
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART B: Identifying influential cases (mean Cook's D)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Cook's D computation:\n")
cat("  D_i = (h_ii / (p * (1-h_ii)^2)) * r_i^2\n")
cat("  h_ii = leverage (diagonal of hat matrix)\n")
cat("  r_i  = Pearson residual\n")
cat("  p    = number of model parameters\n")
cat("  Fit separately per imputation; mean D used for flagging.\n")
cat("  Small min-max range = influence is stable across imputed values.\n\n")

cat(sprintf("Computing Cook's D across %d imputations for Model A...\n", m))
cooks_A   <- get_cooks_stats(imputed_list, formula_A)
inf_idx_A <- which(cooks_A$mean > threshold)

cat(sprintf("Computing Cook's D across %d imputations for Model B...\n", m))
cooks_B   <- get_cooks_stats(imputed_list, formula_B)
inf_idx_B <- which(cooks_B$mean > threshold)

noninf_idx_A <- setdiff(seq_len(N), inf_idx_A)
noninf_idx_B <- setdiff(seq_len(N), inf_idx_B)

overlap     <- intersect(inf_idx_A, inf_idx_B)
only_A      <- setdiff(inf_idx_A, inf_idx_B)
only_B      <- setdiff(inf_idx_B, inf_idx_A)
noninf_both <- intersect(noninf_idx_A, noninf_idx_B)

cat(sprintf("\nModel A — Influential cases: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            length(inf_idx_A), N, 100 * length(inf_idx_A) / N))
cat(sprintf("Model B — Influential cases: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            length(inf_idx_B), N, 100 * length(inf_idx_B) / N))
cat(sprintf("\nOverlap (influential in both): %d\n",  length(overlap)))
cat(sprintf("Influential in Model A only:   %d\n",    length(only_A)))
cat(sprintf("Influential in Model B only:   %d\n",    length(only_B)))
cat(sprintf("Non-influential in both:       %d\n\n",  length(noninf_both)))

if (length(inf_idx_A) > 0 || length(inf_idx_B) > 0) {
  all_inf  <- unique(c(inf_idx_A, inf_idx_B))
  range_A  <- mean(cooks_A$max[all_inf] - cooks_A$min[all_inf], na.rm = TRUE)
  range_B  <- mean(cooks_B$max[all_inf] - cooks_B$min[all_inf], na.rm = TRUE)
  cat("Cook's D stability (mean min-max range across imputations):\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Model A: %.5f  |  Model B: %.5f\n", range_A, range_B))
  cat("  Small range = influence stable regardless of imputed values.\n\n")
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DEFINE PER-MODEL ANALYSIS FUNCTION
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

run_influential_analysis <- function(model_label, formula_obj, model_vars,
                                     inf_idx, cooks_stats,
                                     imputed_list, outcome_var,
                                     chunk12_summary) {

  NN         <- nrow(imputed_list[[1]])
  mm         <- length(imputed_list)
  noninf_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(NN), inf_idx)
  fv         <- is_factor_var(model_vars, imputed_list[[1]])
  cont_vars  <- model_vars[!fv]
  fac_vars   <- model_vars[fv]

  cat(sprintf("\n%s\n", strrep("═", 63)))
  cat(sprintf("ANALYSIS: %s\n", model_label))
  cat(sprintf("%s\n\n", strrep("═", 63)))

  # ── PART C: Outcome comparison ───────────────────────────────────────────
  cat("PART C: Outcome comparison\n")
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

  outcome1       <- imputed_list[[1]][[outcome_var]]
  inf_outcome    <- outcome1[inf_idx]
  noninf_outcome <- outcome1[noninf_idx]

  outcome_summary <- data.frame(
    Group            = c("Influential", "Non-influential"),
    N                = c(length(inf_idx), length(noninf_idx)),
    N_Discontinued   = c(sum(inf_outcome),  sum(noninf_outcome)),
    Pct_Discontinued = round(c(100 * mean(inf_outcome), 100 * mean(noninf_outcome)), 1)
  )
  print_df(outcome_summary)

  cont_table <- table(
    c(rep("Influential", length(inf_idx)), rep("Non-influential", length(noninf_idx))),
    c(inf_outcome, noninf_outcome))
  fisher_out <- fisher.test(cont_table)
  cat(sprintf("Fisher's exact test: OR = %.2f [%.2f, %.2f], p = %.4f\n\n",
              fisher_out$estimate, fisher_out$conf.int[1],
              fisher_out$conf.int[2], fisher_out$p.value))

  # ── PART D: Pooled predictor distributions ───────────────────────────────
  cat(sprintf("PART D: Predictor distributions (pooled across %d imputations)\n", mm))
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

  if (length(cont_vars) > 0) {
    cat("Continuous variables — mean (SD):\n\n")
    cont_wide <- bind_rows(
      pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, inf_idx,    cont_vars, "Influential"),
      pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, noninf_idx, cont_vars, "Non-influential")
    ) %>%
      mutate(Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", Mean, SD)) %>%
      select(Variable, Group, Mean_SD) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = Mean_SD)
    print_df(cont_wide)

    cat("Pooled t-tests:\n")
    for (v in cont_vars) {
      tt <- pooled_comparison(imputed_list, v, inf_idx, noninf_idx)
      cat(sprintf("  %-35s pooled p = %.4f\n", v, tt$p_pool))
    }
    cat("\n")
  }

  if (length(fac_vars) > 0) {
    cat("Factor variables — mean N (%) per level:\n\n")
    for (v in fac_vars) {
      cat(sprintf("  %s:\n", v))
      fac_wide <- bind_rows(
        pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, inf_idx,    v, "Influential"),
        pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, noninf_idx, v, "Non-influential")
      ) %>%
        mutate(N_Pct = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f%%)", N_level, Pct)) %>%
        select(Level, Group, N_Pct) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = N_Pct)
      print_df(fac_wide)
      ft <- pooled_comparison(imputed_list, v, inf_idx, noninf_idx)
      cat(sprintf("  Fisher's exact (simulated p): p = %.4f\n\n", ft$p_pool))
    }
  }

  # ── PART E: Percentile distributions ────────────────────────────────────
  cat(sprintf("PART E: Percentile distributions — continuous variables\n"))
  cat(sprintf("(pooled across %d imputations; factor variables excluded)\n", mm))
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

  for (v in model_vars) {
    if (is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]])) {
      cat(sprintf("%s: factor — see Part D\n\n", v)); next
    }
    cat(sprintf("%s:\n", v))
    pct_df <- pooled_percentile_dist(imputed_list, inf_idx, v)
    for (j in seq_len(nrow(pct_df)))
      cat(sprintf("  %-15s mean N=%.1f (%.1f%%)\n",
                  pct_df$Quartile[j], pct_df$Mean_N[j], pct_df$Mean_Pct[j]))
    cat("\n")
  }

  # ── PART F: Data-driven scenario classification ───────────────────────────
  cat("PART F: Data-driven scenario classification\n")
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")
  cat("Continuous: flagged if quartile rate ratio >= 1.5 (directional, pooled)\n")
  cat("Factor:     flagged if any level prevalence ratio >= 1.5 (pooled)\n\n")

  scen <- classify_scenario(imputed_list, inf_idx, noninf_idx, model_vars)

  cont_rows <- scen$ratio_df %>% filter(Type == "continuous")
  fac_rows  <- scen$ratio_df %>% filter(Type == "factor")

  if (nrow(cont_rows) > 0) {
    cat("Continuous predictors:\n")
    print_df(cont_rows %>% select(Variable, Hi_Inf_Pct, Hi_NonInf_Pct, Hi_Ratio,
                                   Lo_Inf_Pct, Lo_NonInf_Pct, Lo_Ratio,
                                   Flagged, Direction))
  }
  if (nrow(fac_rows) > 0) {
    cat("Factor predictors:\n")
    print_df(fac_rows %>% select(Variable, Flagged, Direction))
  }

  cat(sprintf("Flagged predictors: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              scen$n_flagged, length(model_vars), scen$pct_flagged))
  cat(sprintf("Scenario: %s\n\n", scen$scenario))

  # ── PART G: Removal sensitivity ──────────────────────────────────────────
  cat(sprintf("PART G: Removal sensitivity (pooled across %d imputations)\n", mm))
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")
  cat(sprintf("Removing %d influential cases from all %d imputed datasets...\n\n",
              length(inf_idx), mm))
  cat("Comparing against Chunk 12 RAW-score pooled coefficients.\n\n")

  fits_clean <- lapply(seq_len(mm), function(i) {
    tryCatch(
      glm(formula_obj, data = imputed_list[[i]][noninf_idx, , drop = FALSE],
          family = binomial()),
      error = function(e) { cat(sprintf("  Imputation %d failed: %s\n", i, e$message)); NULL }
    )
  })

  pooled_clean  <- pool_glm_manual(fits_clean)

  # FIX: reference Chunk 12 raw-score summaries (model_A_raw / model_B_raw)
  pooled_full   <- chunk12_summary %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)")
  pooled_clean2 <- pooled_clean    %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)")

  sep_all  <- pooled_clean2$term[pooled_clean2$separation_flag &
                                   pooled_clean2$m_converged == 0]
  sep_some <- pooled_clean2$term[pooled_clean2$separation_flag &
                                   pooled_clean2$m_converged >= 1]

  if (length(sep_all) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("⚠️  Complete collapse (m_converged=0) for: %s\n",
                paste(sep_all, collapse = ", ")))
    cat("   Full-sample effect driven by influential cases — NON-ROBUST.\n\n")

    for (ct in sep_all) {
      cat(sprintf("Diagnostic: raw estimates for '%s':\n", ct))
      diag_df <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(fits_clean), function(i) {
        f <- fits_clean[[i]]
        if (is.null(f)) return(data.frame(imp=i, estimate=NA_real_, se=NA_real_))
        cf  <- summary(f)$coefficients
        idx <- match(ct, rownames(cf))
        if (is.na(idx)) return(data.frame(imp=i, estimate=NA_real_, se=NA_real_))
        data.frame(imp=i, estimate=cf[idx,"Estimate"], se=cf[idx,"Std. Error"])
      }))
      print_df(diag_df)
      med_e <- median(diag_df$estimate, na.rm=TRUE)
      if (!is.na(med_e))
        cat(sprintf("\nMedian estimate: %.3f  (OR=%.3f)  Median SE: %.3f\n\n",
                    med_e, exp(med_e), median(diag_df$se, na.rm=TRUE)))
      else
        cat("\nMedian estimate: NA — term dropped in all imputations.\n\n")
    }
  }

  if (length(sep_some) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("⚠️  Partial separation after removal for: %s\n",
                paste(sep_some, collapse = ", ")))
    cat("   Pooled estimate available but interpret cautiously.\n\n")
  }

  removal_comparison <- full_join(
    pooled_full   %>% select(term, OR_full=OR, OR_CI_full=OR_CI, p_full=p.value),
    pooled_clean2 %>% select(term, OR_clean=OR, OR_CI_clean=OR_CI,
                              p_clean=p.value, separation_flag, m_converged),
    by = "term"
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      OR_change_pct = round(100 * (OR_clean - OR_full) / OR_full, 1),
      Sig_full  = p_full  < 0.05,
      Sig_clean = p_clean < 0.05,
      Robustness = case_when(
        m_converged == 0                                       ~ "Non-robust (effect collapses after removal)",
        separation_flag & m_converged >= 1                     ~ "Separation in reduced sample — interpret cautiously",
        Sig_full  & Sig_clean & abs(OR_change_pct) >= 25      ~ "Robust but substantial magnitude shift (>=25%)",
        Sig_full  & Sig_clean                                  ~ "Robust (remains significant)",
        Sig_full  & !Sig_clean                                 ~ "Non-robust (loses significance)",
        !Sig_full & Sig_clean                                  ~ "Gains significance after removal",
        TRUE                                                   ~ "Both non-significant"
      )
    )

  cat("Coefficient comparison (full vs influential removed):\n")
  print_df(removal_comparison %>%
             select(term, OR_CI_full, OR_CI_clean, OR_change_pct,
                    separation_flag, m_converged, Robustness))

  robust_labels    <- c("Robust (remains significant)",
                        "Robust but substantial magnitude shift (>=25%)",
                        "Separation in reduced sample — interpret cautiously")
  nonrobust_labels <- c("Non-robust (loses significance)",
                        "Non-robust (effect collapses after removal)")

  n_robust    <- sum(removal_comparison$Robustness %in% robust_labels)
  n_nonrobust <- sum(removal_comparison$Robustness %in% nonrobust_labels)
  n_gains     <- sum(removal_comparison$Robustness == "Gains significance after removal")
  n_both_ns   <- sum(removal_comparison$Robustness == "Both non-significant")

  cat(sprintf("\nRobust predictors:                     %d / %d\n",
              n_robust,    nrow(removal_comparison)))
  cat(sprintf("Non-robust predictors:                 %d / %d\n",
              n_nonrobust, nrow(removal_comparison)))
  cat(sprintf("Gains significance after removal:      %d / %d\n",
              n_gains,     nrow(removal_comparison)))
  cat(sprintf("Both non-significant (full & clean):   %d / %d\n\n",
              n_both_ns,   nrow(removal_comparison)))

  if (n_nonrobust > 0) {
    cat("Non-robust predictors:\n")
    removal_comparison %>%
      filter(Robustness %in% nonrobust_labels) %>%
      select(term, Robustness) %>%
      pwalk(~ cat(sprintf("  • %s  [%s]\n", ..1, ..2)))
    cat("\n")
  }

  if (n_gains > 0) {
    cat("Predictors gaining significance after removal:\n")
    cat("  (These were suppressed by influential cases in the full model.)\n")
    removal_comparison %>%
      filter(Robustness == "Gains significance after removal") %>%
      select(term, OR_CI_full, OR_CI_clean, OR_change_pct) %>%
      pwalk(~ cat(sprintf("  • %s: full=%s -> clean=%s (change: %+.1f%%)\n",
                          ..1, ..2, ..3, ..4)))
    cat("\n")
  }

  if (any(removal_comparison$Robustness == "Robust but substantial magnitude shift (>=25%)")) {
    cat("Robust but substantial magnitude shift (>=25%%):\n")
    cat("  (Significance preserved but OR changed materially — interpret with caution.)\n")
    removal_comparison %>%
      filter(Robustness == "Robust but substantial magnitude shift (>=25%)") %>%
      select(term, OR_CI_full, OR_CI_clean, OR_change_pct) %>%
      pwalk(~ cat(sprintf("  • %s: full=%s -> clean=%s (change: %+.1f%%)\n",
                          ..1, ..2, ..3, ..4)))
    cat("\n")
  }

  list(model_label = model_label, n_influential = length(inf_idx),
       inf_idx = inf_idx, threshold = threshold,
       outcome_summary = outcome_summary, fisher_outcome = fisher_out,
       scenario = scen, removal_comparison = removal_comparison,
       fits_clean = fits_clean,
       n_robust = n_robust, n_nonrobust = n_nonrobust,
       n_gains = n_gains)
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PARTS C-G: RUN FOR BOTH MODELS
# FIX: pass model_A_raw / model_B_raw as chunk12_summary
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

results_A <- run_influential_analysis(
  model_label     = "MODEL A — Personality (interpretation set, raw scores)",
  formula_obj     = formula_A, model_vars = vars_A,
  inf_idx         = inf_idx_A, cooks_stats = cooks_A,
  imputed_list    = imputed_list, outcome_var = outcome_var,
  chunk12_summary = chunk12$summaries$model_A_raw)

results_B <- run_influential_analysis(
  model_label     = "MODEL B — Full (interpretation set, raw scores)",
  formula_obj     = formula_B, model_vars = vars_B,
  inf_idx         = inf_idx_B, cooks_stats = cooks_B,
  imputed_list    = imputed_list, outcome_var = outcome_var,
  chunk12_summary = chunk12$summaries$model_B_raw)

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART H: OVERLAP ANALYSIS — FOUR-GROUP COMPARISON
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART H: Overlap analysis — cases influential in both models\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Overlap cases are flagged as influential in BOTH Model A and Model B.\n")
cat("Their influence is not domain-specific — it persists across personality-\n")
cat("only and full predictor sets — suggesting something fundamental about\n")
cat("these participants (extreme outcome values, unusual predictor profiles\n")
cat("spanning both domains).\n\n")

cat(sprintf("Group membership (N=%d):\n", N))
cat(sprintf("  Influential in both:    %d (%.1f%%)\n", length(overlap),     100*length(overlap)/N))
cat(sprintf("  Influential A only:     %d (%.1f%%)\n", length(only_A),      100*length(only_A)/N))
cat(sprintf("  Influential B only:     %d (%.1f%%)\n", length(only_B),      100*length(only_B)/N))
cat(sprintf("  Non-influential (both): %d (%.1f%%)\n\n", length(noninf_both), 100*length(noninf_both)/N))

# ── H1: Cook's D for overlap cases ───────────────────────────────────────────
cat("H1: Cook's D for overlap cases (mean and range across imputations)\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

if (length(overlap) > 0) {
  overlap_cooks <- data.frame(
    Case_ID       = overlap,
    CooksD_A_mean = round(cooks_A$mean[overlap], 5),
    CooksD_A_min  = round(cooks_A$min[overlap],  5),
    CooksD_A_max  = round(cooks_A$max[overlap],  5),
    CooksD_B_mean = round(cooks_B$mean[overlap], 5),
    CooksD_B_min  = round(cooks_B$min[overlap],  5),
    CooksD_B_max  = round(cooks_B$max[overlap],  5),
    Combined_mean = round((cooks_A$mean[overlap] + cooks_B$mean[overlap]) / 2, 5)
  ) %>% arrange(desc(Combined_mean))

  print_df(overlap_cooks)
  cat(sprintf("Summary across %d overlap case(s):\n", nrow(overlap_cooks)))
  cat(sprintf("  Model A: mean=%.5f  (range: %.5f - %.5f)\n",
              mean(overlap_cooks$CooksD_A_mean),
              min(overlap_cooks$CooksD_A_min), max(overlap_cooks$CooksD_A_max)))
  cat(sprintf("  Model B: mean=%.5f  (range: %.5f - %.5f)\n",
              mean(overlap_cooks$CooksD_B_mean),
              min(overlap_cooks$CooksD_B_min), max(overlap_cooks$CooksD_B_max)))
  cat(sprintf("  Combined: %.5f\n\n", mean(overlap_cooks$Combined_mean)))
} else {
  cat("No overlap cases.\n\n")
  overlap_cooks <- data.frame()
}

# ── H2: Outcome rates across four groups ─────────────────────────────────────
cat("H2: Outcome rates across influence groups\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

outcome1     <- imputed_list[[1]][[outcome_var]]
group_labels <- rep("Non-influential (both)", N)
group_labels[only_A]  <- "Influential (A only)"
group_labels[only_B]  <- "Influential (B only)"
group_labels[overlap] <- "Influential (both)"

outcome_by_group <- data.frame(Group = group_labels, Outcome = outcome1) %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), N_Discontinued = sum(Outcome),
            Pct_Discontinued = round(100 * mean(Outcome), 1), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Pct_Discontinued))

print_df(outcome_by_group)

if (length(unique(group_labels)) >= 2) {
  chi_4 <- chisq.test(table(group_labels, outcome1),
                      simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 2000)
  cat(sprintf("Chi-square (%d groups, simulated p): X2=%.3f, p=%.4f\n\n",
              length(unique(group_labels)), chi_4$statistic, chi_4$p.value))
}

# ── H3: Predictor profiles across groups ─────────────────────────────────────
cat(sprintf("H3: Predictor profiles across influence groups\n"))
cat(sprintf("(pooled across %d imputations)\n", m))
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

all_pred_vars <- union(vars_A, vars_B)
all_cont      <- all_pred_vars[!sapply(all_pred_vars, function(v) is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]]))]
all_fac       <- all_pred_vars[sapply(all_pred_vars,  function(v) is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]]))]

groups_list <- Filter(function(x) length(x) > 0, list(
  "Influential (both)"     = overlap,
  "Influential (A only)"   = only_A,
  "Influential (B only)"   = only_B,
  "Non-influential (both)" = noninf_both))

if (length(all_cont) > 0) {
  cat("Continuous variables — mean (SD):\n\n")
  profile_cont <- bind_rows(lapply(names(groups_list), function(grp)
    pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, groups_list[[grp]], all_cont, grp))) %>%
    mutate(Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", Mean, SD)) %>%
    select(Variable, Group, Mean_SD) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = Mean_SD)
  print_df(profile_cont)
}

if (length(all_fac) > 0) {
  cat("Factor variables — mean N (%) per level:\n\n")
  for (v in all_fac) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s:\n", v))
    fac_profile <- bind_rows(lapply(names(groups_list), function(grp)
      pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, groups_list[[grp]], v, grp))) %>%
      mutate(N_Pct = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f%%)", N_level, Pct)) %>%
      select(Level, Group, N_Pct) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = N_Pct)
    print_df(fac_profile)
  }
}

# ── H4: Cook's D — overlap vs single-model influential ───────────────────────
cat("H4: Cook's D — overlap vs single-model influential\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

if (length(overlap) > 0 || length(only_A) > 0 || length(only_B) > 0) {
  cooks_compare <- data.frame(
    Group         = c(rep("Overlap", length(overlap)),
                      rep("A only",  length(only_A)),
                      rep("B only",  length(only_B))),
    CooksD_A_mean = c(cooks_A$mean[overlap], cooks_A$mean[only_A], cooks_A$mean[only_B]),
    CooksD_B_mean = c(cooks_B$mean[overlap], cooks_B$mean[only_A], cooks_B$mean[only_B])
  )
  cooks_h4 <- cooks_compare %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(N = n(),
              Mean_CooksD_A = round(mean(CooksD_A_mean, na.rm=TRUE), 5),
              Mean_CooksD_B = round(mean(CooksD_B_mean, na.rm=TRUE), 5),
              Max_CooksD_A  = round(max(CooksD_A_mean,  na.rm=TRUE), 5),
              Max_CooksD_B  = round(max(CooksD_B_mean,  na.rm=TRUE), 5),
              .groups = "drop")
  print_df(cooks_h4)
} else {
  cat("No influential cases in any group — skipping.\n\n")
}

# ── H5: Individual Cook's D and extreme case profiling ───────────────────────
cat("H5: Individual Cook's D and extreme case profiling\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("Lists Cook's D for every influential case in both models.\n")
cat("Cases with mean Cook's D > 10x threshold are automatically profiled.\n\n")

cat("H5a: B-only influential cases — individual Cook's D\n\n")

if (length(only_B) > 0) {
  b_only_detail <- data.frame(
    Case_ID            = only_B,
    CooksD_B_mean      = round(cooks_B$mean[only_B], 5),
    CooksD_B_min       = round(cooks_B$min[only_B],  5),
    CooksD_B_max       = round(cooks_B$max[only_B],  5),
    CooksD_A_mean      = round(cooks_A$mean[only_B], 5),
    Ratio_to_threshold = round(cooks_B$mean[only_B] / threshold, 1)
  ) %>% arrange(desc(CooksD_B_mean))
  print_df(b_only_detail)
  cat(sprintf("Threshold (4/N): %.5f\n\n", threshold))
} else {
  cat("No B-only influential cases.\n\n")
}

cat("H5b: A-only influential cases — individual Cook's D\n\n")

if (length(only_A) > 0) {
  a_only_detail <- data.frame(
    Case_ID            = only_A,
    CooksD_A_mean      = round(cooks_A$mean[only_A], 5),
    CooksD_A_min       = round(cooks_A$min[only_A],  5),
    CooksD_A_max       = round(cooks_A$max[only_A],  5),
    CooksD_B_mean      = round(cooks_B$mean[only_A], 5),
    Ratio_to_threshold = round(cooks_A$mean[only_A] / threshold, 1)
  ) %>% arrange(desc(CooksD_A_mean))
  print_df(a_only_detail)
  cat(sprintf("Threshold (4/N): %.5f\n\n", threshold))
} else {
  cat("No A-only influential cases.\n\n")
}

cat("H5c: Extreme case profiles (mean Cook's D > 10x threshold)\n")
cat("Format: value (Nth sample percentile)\n\n")

extreme_threshold <- threshold * 10
all_inf_cases     <- unique(c(inf_idx_A, inf_idx_B))
extreme_cases     <- all_inf_cases[
  pmax(cooks_A$mean[all_inf_cases], cooks_B$mean[all_inf_cases]) > extreme_threshold
]

if (length(extreme_cases) == 0) {
  cat("No cases exceed 10x threshold — no extreme profiling required.\n\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("%d case(s) exceed 10x threshold (%.5f):\n\n",
              length(extreme_cases), extreme_threshold))

  profile_vars <- union(vars_A, vars_B)
  ref_data     <- imputed_list[[1]]

  sample_pctile <- function(val, vec) {
    if (is.na(val)) return(NA_real_)
    round(100 * mean(vec <= val, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  }

  for (case_id in sort(extreme_cases)) {
    cd_A        <- round(cooks_A$mean[case_id], 5)
    cd_B        <- round(cooks_B$mean[case_id], 5)
    outcome_val <- ref_data[[outcome_var]][case_id]

    cat(sprintf("─── Case ID: %d ───────────────────────────────────────\n", case_id))
    cat(sprintf("  Cook's D Model A: %.5f (%.1fx threshold)\n", cd_A, cd_A / threshold))
    cat(sprintf("  Cook's D Model B: %.5f (%.1fx threshold)\n", cd_B, cd_B / threshold))
    cat(sprintf("  Outcome (%s): %s\n\n", outcome_var, as.character(outcome_val)))
    cat("  Predictor values (imputation 1; percentile vs full sample):\n\n")

    for (v in profile_vars) {
      val <- ref_data[[v]][case_id]
      if (is.factor(ref_data[[v]])) {
        lev  <- as.character(val)
        prev <- round(100 * mean(ref_data[[v]] == lev, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
        cat(sprintf("  %-30s %s  (%.1f%% of sample)\n", v, lev, prev))
      } else {
        pct  <- sample_pctile(val, ref_data[[v]])
        flag <- if (!is.na(pct) && (pct >= 99 | pct <= 1)) "  *** CHECK ***" else ""
        cat(sprintf("  %-30s %8.3f  (%5.1f th pctile)%s\n", v, val, pct, flag))
      }
    }
    cat("\n")
    cat("  Interpretation guidance:\n")
    cat("  - Values at 99th+ or 1st- percentile warrant source data check\n")
    cat("  - High Cook's D with moderate percentiles = genuine clinical outlier\n")
    cat("  - High Cook's D with extreme percentiles = possible data entry error\n\n")
  }
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART I: OVERLAP-SPECIFIC DEEP-DIVE
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART I: Overlap-specific deep-dive\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("I1: Domain-generality of overlap influence\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("If overlap cases have elevated Cook's D in both models, their influence\n")
cat("is domain-general — not explained by either model's unique predictors.\n\n")

if (length(overlap) > 0) {
  d_overlap_A <- mean(cooks_A$mean[overlap], na.rm = TRUE)
  d_overlap_B <- mean(cooks_B$mean[overlap], na.rm = TRUE)
  d_onlyA_A   <- if (length(only_A) > 0) mean(cooks_A$mean[only_A], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  d_onlyB_B   <- if (length(only_B) > 0) mean(cooks_B$mean[only_B], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_

  ratio_A <- d_overlap_A / threshold
  ratio_B <- d_overlap_B / threshold

  cat(sprintf("  Overlap cases (n=%d):\n", length(overlap)))
  cat(sprintf("    Mean Cook's D in Model A: %.5f  (%.1fx threshold)\n", d_overlap_A, ratio_A))
  cat(sprintf("    Mean Cook's D in Model B: %.5f  (%.1fx threshold)\n", d_overlap_B, ratio_B))
  if (!is.na(d_onlyA_A))
    cat(sprintf("  A-only cases (n=%d): Mean Cook's D in Model A: %.5f  (%.1fx threshold)\n",
                length(only_A), d_onlyA_A, d_onlyA_A / threshold))
  if (!is.na(d_onlyB_B))
    cat(sprintf("  B-only cases (n=%d): Mean Cook's D in Model B: %.5f  (%.1fx threshold)\n",
                length(only_B), d_onlyB_B, d_onlyB_B / threshold))

  cat("\n  Interpretation:\n  ")
  cat(if (ratio_A >= 1.5 & ratio_B >= 1.5)
    sprintf("Overlap cases exceed threshold by %.1fx (Model A) and %.1fx (Model B).\n  Influence is elevated in both models — consistent with domain-general effects.",
            ratio_A, ratio_B)
  else if (ratio_A >= 1.0 & ratio_B >= 1.0)
    sprintf("Overlap cases exceed threshold in both models (%.1fx and %.1fx).\n  Review outcome rates and predictor profiles in Part H.",
            ratio_A, ratio_B)
  else
    "Influence pattern is asymmetric — review per-model Cook's D in Part H.")
  cat("\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  No overlap cases — I1 not applicable.\n\n")
}

cat("I2: What distinguishes A-only from B-only influential cases?\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

vars_A_only <- setdiff(vars_A, vars_B)
vars_B_only <- setdiff(vars_B, vars_A)

if (length(vars_A_only) == 0 && length(vars_B_only) == 0) {
  cat("  Models share all predictors — no model-specific predictors to compare.\n\n")
} else {

  if (length(only_A) > 0 && length(vars_A_only) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("A-only influential cases (n=%d) on Model A-specific predictors:\n\n",
                length(only_A)))
    cont_A_only <- vars_A_only[!sapply(vars_A_only, function(v) is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]]))]
    fac_A_only  <- vars_A_only[ sapply(vars_A_only, function(v) is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]]))]

    if (length(cont_A_only) > 0) {
      comp_df <- bind_rows(
        pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, only_A,      cont_A_only, "A-only influential"),
        pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, noninf_both, cont_A_only, "Non-influential")
      ) %>%
        mutate(Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", Mean, SD)) %>%
        select(Variable, Group, Mean_SD) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = Mean_SD)
      print_df(comp_df)
    }
    for (v in fac_A_only) {
      cat(sprintf("  Factor: %s\n", v))
      fac_df <- bind_rows(
        pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, only_A,      v, "A-only influential"),
        pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, noninf_both, v, "Non-influential")
      ) %>%
        mutate(N_Pct = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f%%)", N_level, Pct)) %>%
        select(Level, Group, N_Pct) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = N_Pct)
      print_df(fac_df)
    }
  } else if (length(only_A) == 0) {
    cat("  No A-only influential cases.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("  No predictors unique to Model A.\n\n")
  }

  if (length(only_B) > 0 && length(vars_B_only) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("B-only influential cases (n=%d) on Model B-specific predictors:\n\n",
                length(only_B)))
    cont_B_only <- vars_B_only[!sapply(vars_B_only, function(v) is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]]))]
    fac_B_only  <- vars_B_only[ sapply(vars_B_only, function(v) is.factor(imputed_list[[1]][[v]]))]

    if (length(cont_B_only) > 0) {
      comp_df <- bind_rows(
        pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, only_B,      cont_B_only, "B-only influential"),
        pooled_predictor_summary(imputed_list, noninf_both, cont_B_only, "Non-influential")
      ) %>%
        mutate(Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", Mean, SD)) %>%
        select(Variable, Group, Mean_SD) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = Mean_SD)
      print_df(comp_df)
    }
    for (v in fac_B_only) {
      cat(sprintf("  Factor: %s\n", v))
      fac_df <- bind_rows(
        pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, only_B,      v, "B-only influential"),
        pooled_factor_summary(imputed_list, noninf_both, v, "Non-influential")
      ) %>%
        mutate(N_Pct = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f%%)", N_level, Pct)) %>%
        select(Level, Group, N_Pct) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = N_Pct)
      print_df(fac_df)
    }
  } else if (length(only_B) == 0) {
    cat("  No B-only influential cases.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("  No predictors unique to Model B.\n\n")
  }
}

cat("I3: Do overlap cases drive the non-robust findings?\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

run_overlap_sensitivity <- function(model_label, formula_obj,
                                    removal_comparison, chunk12_summary,
                                    overlap_idx) {

  nonrobust_terms <- removal_comparison %>%
    filter(Robustness %in% c("Non-robust (loses significance)",
                             "Non-robust (effect collapses after removal)")) %>%
    pull(term)

  if (length(nonrobust_terms) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s: No non-robust predictors — I3 not applicable.\n\n", model_label))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  if (length(overlap_idx) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s: No overlap cases — cannot run overlap-only removal.\n\n", model_label))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  cat(sprintf("  %s — removing overlap cases only (n=%d):\n",
              model_label, length(overlap_idx)))

  overlap_noninf <- setdiff(seq_len(N), overlap_idx)

  fits_overlap_removed <- lapply(seq_len(m), function(i) {
    tryCatch(
      glm(formula_obj,
          data = imputed_list[[i]][overlap_noninf, , drop = FALSE],
          family = binomial()),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
  })

  pooled_overlap <- pool_glm_manual(fits_overlap_removed) %>%
    filter(term %in% nonrobust_terms) %>%
    select(term, OR_overlap = OR, OR_CI_overlap = OR_CI,
           p_overlap = p.value, m_converged)

  pooled_ref <- chunk12_summary %>%
    filter(term %in% nonrobust_terms) %>%
    select(term, OR_full = OR, OR_CI_full = OR_CI, p_full = p.value)

  comparison <- full_join(pooled_ref, pooled_overlap, by = "term") %>%
    mutate(
      Sig_full    = p_full    < 0.05,
      Sig_overlap = p_overlap < 0.05,
      Driven_by_overlap = case_when(
        m_converged == 0        ~ "Yes — collapses after overlap-only removal",
        Sig_full & !Sig_overlap ~ "Yes — loses significance after overlap-only removal",
        TRUE                    ~ "No — persists after overlap-only removal"))

  print_df(comparison %>%
             select(term, OR_CI_full, OR_CI_overlap, Driven_by_overlap))

  invisible(comparison)
}

# FIX: pass raw-score summaries to overlap sensitivity as well
i3_A <- run_overlap_sensitivity(
  "Model A", formula_A,
  results_A$removal_comparison, chunk12$summaries$model_A_raw, overlap)

i3_B <- run_overlap_sensitivity(
  "Model B", formula_B,
  results_B$removal_comparison, chunk12$summaries$model_B_raw, overlap)

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART J: CROSS-MODEL SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART J: Cross-model summary\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

summary_table <- data.frame(
  Model             = c("Model A (Personality)", "Model B (Full)"),
  N_Influential     = c(results_A$n_influential, results_B$n_influential),
  Pct_Influential   = sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * c(results_A$n_influential,
                                                 results_B$n_influential) / N),
  Pct_Discontinued  = sprintf("%.1f%%", c(
    results_A$outcome_summary$Pct_Discontinued[
      results_A$outcome_summary$Group == "Influential"],
    results_B$outcome_summary$Pct_Discontinued[
      results_B$outcome_summary$Group == "Influential"])),
  Scenario          = c(results_A$scenario$scenario, results_B$scenario$scenario),
  Robust_Predictors = sprintf("%d / %d",
                               c(results_A$n_robust, results_B$n_robust),
                               c(length(vars_A), length(vars_B))),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

print_df(summary_table)
cat(sprintf("Cases influential in both models: %d\n", length(overlap)))
cat(sprintf("A-only influential:               %d\n", length(only_A)))
cat(sprintf("B-only influential:               %d\n\n", length(only_B)))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART K: SAVE RESULTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART K: Saving results\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

output <- list(
  results_A = results_A, results_B = results_B,
  overlap = overlap, only_A = only_A, only_B = only_B,
  noninf_both = noninf_both, overlap_cooks = overlap_cooks,
  outcome_by_group = outcome_by_group, summary_table = summary_table,
  cooks_A = cooks_A, cooks_B = cooks_B,
  i3_A = i3_A, i3_B = i3_B,
  metadata = list(
    outcome          = outcome_var, vars_A = vars_A, vars_B = vars_B,
    N = N, m = m, threshold = threshold,
    scores_used      = "raw (original scale — consistent with Chunk 12 raw models)",
    chunk12_summary_source = "model_A_raw / model_B_raw from regression_comparison_results.rds",
    scenario_note    = paste0(
      "Continuous: quartile rate ratio >= 1.5 directional pooled. ",
      "Factor: any level prevalence ratio >= 1.5 pooled."),
    cooks_d_note     = paste0(
      "D_i = (h_ii / (p*(1-h_ii)^2)) * r_i^2. ",
      "Mean D across imputations used for flagging. Threshold = 4/N. ",
      "Min-max range indicates stability of influence across imputed values.")))

saveRDS(output,                         "influential_cases_results.rds")
write_csv(summary_table,                "influential_cases_summary.csv")
write_csv(results_A$removal_comparison, "influential_removal_modelA.csv")
write_csv(results_B$removal_comparison, "influential_removal_modelB.csv")
write_csv(outcome_by_group,             "influential_outcome_by_group.csv")
if (nrow(overlap_cooks) > 0)
  write_csv(overlap_cooks,              "influential_overlap_cooks.csv")

cat("✓ Saved: influential_cases_results.rds\n")
cat("✓ Saved: influential_cases_summary.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: influential_removal_modelA.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: influential_removal_modelB.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: influential_outcome_by_group.csv\n")
if (nrow(overlap_cooks) > 0) cat("✓ Saved: influential_overlap_cooks.csv\n")
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 14 COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

for (res in list(results_A, results_B)) {
  p_vars <- if (res$model_label == results_A$model_label) length(vars_A)
            else length(vars_B)
  cat(sprintf("%s\n", res$model_label))
  cat(sprintf("  Influential cases:   %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              res$n_influential, 100 * res$n_influential / N))
  cat(sprintf("  Scenario:           %s\n", res$scenario$scenario))
  cat(sprintf("  Flagged predictors: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              res$scenario$n_flagged, p_vars, res$scenario$pct_flagged))
  cat(sprintf("  Robust predictors:  %d / %d\n", res$n_robust, p_vars))
  if (res$n_nonrobust > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  Non-robust:         %d / %d\n", res$n_nonrobust, p_vars))
    res$removal_comparison %>%
      filter(Robustness %in% c("Non-robust (loses significance)",
                               "Non-robust (effect collapses after removal)")) %>%
      pull(term) %>% walk(~ cat(sprintf("    • %s\n", .x)))
  }
  if (res$n_gains > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  Gains significance after removal: %d / %d\n", res$n_gains, p_vars))
    res$removal_comparison %>%
      filter(Robustness == "Gains significance after removal") %>%
      pull(term) %>% walk(~ cat(sprintf("    • %s\n", .x)))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

cat(sprintf("Overlap (influential in both): %d\n", length(overlap)))
cat(sprintf("A-only influential:            %d\n", length(only_A)))
cat(sprintf("B-only influential:            %d\n\n", length(only_B)))

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. influential_cases_results.rds\n")
cat("  2. influential_cases_summary.csv\n")
cat("  3. influential_removal_modelA.csv\n")
cat("  4. influential_removal_modelB.csv\n")
cat("  5. influential_outcome_by_group.csv\n")
cat("  6. influential_overlap_cooks.csv  (if overlap cases exist)\n\n")

cat("NOTES:\n")
cat(sprintf("  - m = %d imputations throughout\n", m))
cat(sprintf("  - Cook's D threshold: 4/N = 4/%d\n", N))
cat("  - Cook's D = (h_ii / (p*(1-h_ii)^2)) * r_i^2\n")
cat("    h_ii = leverage; r_i = Pearson residual; p = n parameters\n")
cat("    Averaged across imputations; stable influence = small min-max range\n")
cat("  - Raw-score models used (consistent with Chunk 12)\n")
cat("  - Chunk 12 reference: model_A_raw / model_B_raw summaries\n")
cat("    (updated Chunk 12 stores raw and standardized separately)\n")
cat("  - Factor variables: frequency tables in Part D, skipped in\n")
cat("    Part E, prevalence-ratio classification in Part F\n")
cat("  - Scenario thresholds: >= 75% = Scenario 1, 25-74% = Scenario 2,\n")
cat("    < 25% = Scenario 3\n")
cat("  - Robust/non-robust counts exclude intercept term\n\n")

cat("REFERENCES:\n")
cat("  Cook RD (1977). Detection of influential observations. Technometrics.\n")
cat("  Cook RD, Weisberg S (1982). Residuals and Influence in Regression.\n")
cat("  Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.\n\n")

cat("✓ Ready for Chunk 15\n\n")
```






## Subgroup Check
```{r}
#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 14b: PREDICTED PROBABILITY & INFLUENCE MECHANISM DIAGNOSTIC
#            MODEL A & MODEL B — INTERPRETATION SET
#==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   Answer two related questions arising from Chunk 14:
#
#   QUESTION 1 — Influence mechanism: subgroup or outlier?
#     Both models had influential cases with very high discontinuation rates
#     (Model A: 91.3%, Model B: 86.7%), but their scenario classifications
#     differ in specificity:
#
#     Model A (Scenario 1 — coherent clinical subgroup, 5/6 predictors
#     flagged, all high-extreme): The influential cases are elevated across
#     nearly all personality predictors. The subgroup conclusion is already
#     well-supported by the scenario classification. The leverage/residual
#     decomposition and PMPP confirm the mechanism: are these cases
#     identified as high-risk from predictor values alone (high leverage /
#     low residual), or are they mispredicted despite typical predictors
#     (low leverage / high residual)?
#
#     Model B (Scenario 2 — heterogeneous/partial profile, 5/9 predictors
#     flagged): The subgroup conclusion is less certain. Flagged predictors
#     are bzra_daily_dose_eq, c_lengthofuse, n_sleep_conditions,
#     wdl_symptom_count, and DBAS_Medications (low extreme). Four predictors
#     are not flagged. The leverage/residual decomposition and PMPP
#     diagnostic are particularly important here to determine whether these
#     cases represent a coherent high-risk subgroup or a mix of statistical
#     outliers driven by a subset of unusual predictor values.
#
#   QUESTION 2 — Magnitude instability in Model B robust predictors:
#     Chunk 14 Part G identified two predictors in Model B that retained
#     significance after influential case removal but showed substantial
#     OR magnitude shifts:
#       capabilityC:  full OR=2.36 -> clean OR=4.34 (+83.6%)
#       side_effectsC: full OR=1.75 -> clean OR=2.39 (+36.5%)
#     This part investigates whether these shifts are driven by the overlap
#     cases (influential in both models), the B-only influential cases, or
#     are distributed across all influential cases. Understanding the
#     mechanism informs how to report these predictors.
#
# APPROACH:
#
#   Part B: Leverage vs residual decomposition (both models)
#     Cook's D = (leverage * residual^2) / scaling. Decomposing into its
#     two components tells us WHY cases are influential:
#       High leverage + low residual  -> unusual predictor profile, well
#                                        predicted -> coherent subgroup
#       Low leverage  + high residual -> typical predictors, unexpected
#                                        outcome -> statistical outlier
#       High leverage + high residual -> both -> most problematic
#     hat values (h_ii) and Pearson residuals extracted per imputation,
#     averaged across imputations. Wilcoxon comparison vs non-influential.
#
#   Part C: Pooled mean predicted probability (PMPP) — both models
#     PMPP is the row-wise mean predicted probability across all imputations,
#     derived from predictor values alone (independent of observed outcome).
#     For Model A: confirms whether the model identifies influential cases
#     as high-risk from personality predictors alone, supporting Scenario 1.
#     For Model B: primary disambiguation tool — high PMPP despite Scenario 2
#     supports a partial subgroup; low PMPP suggests outlier/residual-driven
#     influence.
#     Thresholds:
#       PMPP median >= 0.70 -> model identifies as high-risk -> subgroup
#       PMPP median 0.40-0.69 -> partial identification -> moderate support
#       PMPP median < 0.40  -> model did not predict outcomes -> outlier
#
#   Part D: Magnitude shift investigation — capabilityC and side_effectsC
#     The +83.6% and +36.5% OR shifts in Model B are large enough to affect
#     clinical interpretation. This part determines which influential cases
#     are driving the attenuation in the full model:
#       D1. Sequential removal: remove overlap cases only (n=12), then
#           B-only cases only (n=18), compare ORs to full and fully-
#           cleaned models. Identifies which subgroup is responsible.
#       D2. Predictor profiles: compare capabilityC and side_effectsC
#           values for overlap vs B-only influential cases vs non-influential,
#           to understand the mechanism of attenuation.
#       D3. Reporting recommendation based on D1-D2 findings.
#
# References:
#   Cook RD (1977). Detection of influential observations. Technometrics.
#   Cook RD, Weisberg S (1982). Residuals and Influence in Regression.
#   Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.
#==============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(mice)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 14b: INFLUENCE MECHANISM DIAGNOSTIC\n")
cat("Model A & Model B — interpretation set\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("PURPOSE:\n")
cat("  1. Decompose Cook's D into leverage vs residual components to\n")
cat("     determine whether influential cases are high-risk subgroup\n")
cat("     members or statistical outliers (both models).\n")
cat("  2. PMPP diagnostic: does the model identify influential cases\n")
cat("     as high-risk from predictor values alone?\n")
cat("     - Model A (Scenario 1): confirmation of coherent subgroup.\n")
cat("     - Model B (Scenario 2): primary disambiguation tool.\n")
cat("  3. Investigate the +83.6%% (capabilityC) and +36.5%%\n")
cat("     (side_effectsC) OR magnitude shifts in Model B to identify\n")
cat("     which influential cases drive the attenuation.\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART A: LOAD DATA
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART A: Loading data\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

if (!file.exists("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds not found. Run Chunk 9 first.")
if (!file.exists("influential_cases_results.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: influential_cases_results.rds not found. Run Chunk 14 first.")
if (!file.exists("regression_comparison_results.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: regression_comparison_results.rds not found. Run Chunk 12 first.")

imputed_list <- readRDS("imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds")
inf_results  <- readRDS("influential_cases_results.rds")
chunk12      <- readRDS("regression_comparison_results.rds")

m            <- length(imputed_list)
N            <- nrow(imputed_list[[1]])

# Model A
inf_idx_A    <- inf_results$results_A$inf_idx
noninf_idx_A <- setdiff(seq_len(N), inf_idx_A)
vars_A       <- inf_results$metadata$vars_A
outcome_var  <- inf_results$metadata$outcome
formula_A    <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(vars_A, collapse = " + ")))

# Model B
inf_idx_B    <- inf_results$results_B$inf_idx
noninf_idx_B <- setdiff(seq_len(N), inf_idx_B)
vars_B       <- inf_results$metadata$vars_B
formula_B    <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(vars_B, collapse = " + ")))

# Overlap cases (influential in both models)
overlap_idx  <- intersect(inf_idx_A, inf_idx_B)
B_only_idx   <- setdiff(inf_idx_B, inf_idx_A)
A_only_idx   <- setdiff(inf_idx_A, inf_idx_B)

outcome1     <- imputed_list[[1]][[outcome_var]]

cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded imputed_datasets_complete_final.rds  (m=%d, N=%d)\n", m, N))
cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded influential_cases_results.rds\n"))
cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded regression_comparison_results.rds\n\n"))

cat(sprintf("Model A: %d influential cases  |  %d non-influential\n",
            length(inf_idx_A), length(noninf_idx_A)))
cat(sprintf("  Outcome rate — Influential: %.1f%%  |  Non-influential: %.1f%%\n",
            100 * mean(outcome1[inf_idx_A]),
            100 * mean(outcome1[noninf_idx_A])))
cat(sprintf("  Scenario 1: coherent clinical subgroup (5/6 predictors flagged,\n"))
cat(sprintf("  all high-extreme): DBAS_Medications, SURPS_Hopelessness,\n"))
cat(sprintf("  CISS_Emotion, DBAS_Consequences, BFI_Neuroticism\n\n"))

cat(sprintf("Model B: %d influential cases  |  %d non-influential\n",
            length(inf_idx_B), length(noninf_idx_B)))
cat(sprintf("  Outcome rate — Influential: %.1f%%  |  Non-influential: %.1f%%\n",
            100 * mean(outcome1[inf_idx_B]),
            100 * mean(outcome1[noninf_idx_B])))
cat(sprintf("  Scenario 2: heterogeneous/partial profile (5/9 predictors\n"))
cat(sprintf("  flagged): bzra_daily_dose_eq (high), c_lengthofuse (high),\n"))
cat(sprintf("  n_sleep_conditions (high), wdl_symptom_count (high),\n"))
cat(sprintf("  DBAS_Medications (low extreme)\n\n"))

cat(sprintf("Overlap (influential in both): %d\n", length(overlap_idx)))
cat(sprintf("A-only influential:            %d\n", length(A_only_idx)))
cat(sprintf("B-only influential:            %d\n\n", length(B_only_idx)))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART B: LEVERAGE VS RESIDUAL DECOMPOSITION — BOTH MODELS
#
# Cook's D = (h_ii / (p * (1 - h_ii)^2)) * r_i^2
# Decomposing into h_ii (leverage) and |r_i| (absolute Pearson residual)
# separately reveals the mechanism of influence:
#
#   High h, low |r|  -> unusual predictor profile, well predicted
#                        -> coherent high-risk subgroup
#   Low  h, high |r| -> typical predictor profile, unexpected outcome
#                        -> statistical outlier / ceiling effect
#   High h, high |r| -> both unusual predictors AND mispredicted
#                        -> most influential, hardest to interpret
#
# Values averaged across all m imputations for stability.
#
# Model A expectation (Scenario 1): high leverage / low residual pattern
#   expected, confirming the coherent subgroup classification.
# Model B expectation (Scenario 2): pattern less certain; mix of
#   mechanisms plausible given partial predictor flagging.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART B: Leverage vs residual decomposition\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("Cook's D = (leverage * residual^2) / scaling.\n")
cat("Decomposing reveals WHY cases are influential:\n")
cat("  High leverage + low  residual  -> unusual predictors, well predicted\n")
cat("                                     -> coherent high-risk subgroup\n")
cat("  Low  leverage + high residual  -> typical predictors, unexpected outcome\n")
cat("                                     -> statistical outlier / ceiling effect\n")
cat("  High leverage + high residual  -> both -> most influential\n\n")
cat("Model A (Scenario 1): high leverage / low residual pattern expected.\n")
cat("Model B (Scenario 2): pattern uncertain — primary disambiguation.\n\n")

decompose_influence <- function(imputed_list, formula_obj, inf_idx, label) {

  m  <- length(imputed_list)
  N  <- nrow(imputed_list[[1]])

  hat_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = N, ncol = m)
  res_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = N, ncol = m)

  for (i in seq_len(m)) {
    fit_i        <- glm(formula_obj, data = imputed_list[[i]], family = binomial())
    hat_mat[, i] <- hatvalues(fit_i)
    res_mat[, i] <- abs(residuals(fit_i, type = "pearson"))
  }

  mean_hat   <- rowMeans(hat_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_res   <- rowMeans(res_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  noninf_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(N), inf_idx)

  # Wilcoxon tests
  w_hat <- wilcox.test(mean_hat[inf_idx], mean_hat[noninf_idx], exact = FALSE)
  w_res <- wilcox.test(mean_res[inf_idx], mean_res[noninf_idx], exact = FALSE)

  # Classification thresholds: 2x non-influential median
  h_thresh <- median(mean_hat[noninf_idx]) * 2
  r_thresh <- median(mean_res[noninf_idx]) * 2

  # Per-case summary
  case_df <- data.frame(
    Case_ID       = inf_idx,
    Mean_Leverage = round(mean_hat[inf_idx], 5),
    Mean_AbsResid = round(mean_res[inf_idx], 4),
    Outcome       = imputed_list[[1]][[all.vars(formula_obj)[1]]][inf_idx]
  ) %>% arrange(desc(Mean_Leverage))

  case_df$Mechanism <- dplyr::case_when(
    case_df$Mean_Leverage >= h_thresh & case_df$Mean_AbsResid < r_thresh ~
      "High leverage / low residual (subgroup)",
    case_df$Mean_Leverage <  h_thresh & case_df$Mean_AbsResid >= r_thresh ~
      "Low leverage / high residual (outlier)",
    case_df$Mean_Leverage >= h_thresh & case_df$Mean_AbsResid >= r_thresh ~
      "High leverage / high residual (both)",
    TRUE ~ "Low leverage / low residual (near-threshold)"
  )

  cat(sprintf("%s\n", label))
  cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Non-influential median leverage:       %.5f\n",
              median(mean_hat[noninf_idx])))
  cat(sprintf("  Non-influential median |residual|:     %.4f\n",
              median(mean_res[noninf_idx])))
  cat(sprintf("  Classification thresholds (2x median):\n"))
  cat(sprintf("    High leverage threshold:  >= %.5f\n", h_thresh))
  cat(sprintf("    High residual threshold:  >= %.4f\n\n", r_thresh))

  cat(sprintf("  Influential cases — mean leverage:     %.5f  (p vs non-inf: %.4f)\n",
              mean(mean_hat[inf_idx]), w_hat$p.value))
  cat(sprintf("  Influential cases — mean |residual|:   %.4f  (p vs non-inf: %.4f)\n\n",
              mean(mean_res[inf_idx]), w_res$p.value))

  mech_tab <- table(case_df$Mechanism)
  cat("  Mechanism classification:\n")
  for (mech in names(mech_tab))
    cat(sprintf("    %-45s n = %d\n", mech, mech_tab[mech]))
  cat("\n")

  cat("  Per-case leverage and residual:\n")
  print(case_df, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\n")

  # Interpretation
  n_total    <- nrow(case_df)
  n_subgroup <- sum(case_df$Mechanism == "High leverage / low residual (subgroup)")
  n_outlier  <- sum(case_df$Mechanism == "Low leverage / high residual (outlier)")
  n_both     <- sum(case_df$Mechanism == "High leverage / high residual (both)")
  pct_sg     <- 100 * n_subgroup / n_total
  pct_out    <- 100 * n_outlier  / n_total
  pct_both   <- 100 * n_both     / n_total

  cat("  INTERPRETATION:\n")
  if (pct_sg >= 50) {
    cat(sprintf("  Majority (%.0f%%) show high leverage / low residual. The model\n",
                pct_sg))
    cat("  identified their predictor profiles as unusual and predicted\n")
    cat("  their outcomes reasonably well. Influence reflects genuine signal\n")
    cat("  concentration in a high-risk predictor region. Supports coherent\n")
    cat("  subgroup interpretation.\n\n")
  } else if (pct_out >= 50) {
    cat(sprintf("  Majority (%.0f%%) show low leverage / high residual. Their\n",
                pct_out))
    cat("  predictor profiles are not unusual, but outcomes were not\n")
    cat("  predicted by the model. Consistent with ceiling effect or\n")
    cat("  statistical outlier pattern — influence is residual-driven.\n\n")
  } else if (pct_both >= 30) {
    cat(sprintf("  %.0f%% show both high leverage and high residual — unusual\n",
                pct_both))
```
