```{r}
    cat("  predictors AND mispredicted outcomes. These are the most\n")
    cat("  difficult to classify and the most potentially distorting.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("  Mixed mechanism — no single pattern dominates. Review per-case\n")
    cat("  values above. Neither pure subgroup nor pure outlier.\n\n")
  }

  invisible(list(
    mean_hat  = mean_hat,
    mean_res  = mean_res,
    case_df   = case_df,
    w_hat     = w_hat,
    w_res     = w_res,
    h_thresh  = h_thresh,
    r_thresh  = r_thresh
  ))
}

decomp_A <- decompose_influence(imputed_list, formula_A, inf_idx_A,
                                 "MODEL A — Personality (n=23 influential, Scenario 1)")
decomp_B <- decompose_influence(imputed_list, formula_B, inf_idx_B,
                                 "MODEL B — Full (n=30 influential, Scenario 2)")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART C: POOLED MEAN PREDICTED PROBABILITY (PMPP) — BOTH MODELS
#
# PMPP = row-wise mean predicted probability across all imputations,
# derived from predictor values alone, independent of observed outcome.
#
# Model A (Scenario 1 — already classified as coherent subgroup):
#   PMPP confirms whether personality predictors alone are sufficient to
#   identify these cases as high-risk. Expected: high PMPP, consistent
#   with the Scenario 1 classification and 91.3% discontinuation rate.
#
# Model B (Scenario 2 — heterogeneous, primary disambiguation):
#   PMPP is the key diagnostic. Despite 5/9 predictors flagged and 86.7%
#   discontinuation, it is unclear whether the clinical predictors
#   collectively identify these cases as high-risk. High PMPP would
#   partially rehabilitate the subgroup interpretation; low PMPP would
#   indicate residual-driven influence and counsel caution.
#
# Thresholds:
#   PMPP median >= 0.70 -> model identifies as high-risk -> supports subgroup
#   PMPP median 0.40-0.69 -> partial identification -> moderate support
#   PMPP median < 0.40  -> model did not predict outcomes -> outlier concern
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART C: Pooled mean predicted probability (PMPP)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("PMPP = row-wise mean predicted probability across all imputations.\n")
cat("Reflects what the model predicts from predictor values alone,\n")
cat("independently of the observed outcome.\n\n")
cat("Model A (Scenario 1): confirmatory — expected high PMPP given\n")
cat("  coherent subgroup classification and 91.3%% discontinuation.\n")
cat("Model B (Scenario 2): primary diagnostic — PMPP determines whether\n")
cat("  full clinical predictor set collectively identifies these cases\n")
cat("  as high-risk despite partial/heterogeneous predictor flagging.\n\n")
cat("Thresholds:\n")
cat("  PMPP >= 0.70 -> model identified as high-risk -> supports subgroup\n")
cat("  PMPP 0.40-0.69 -> partial identification -> moderate support\n")
cat("  PMPP < 0.40  -> model did not predict outcomes -> outlier concern\n\n")

compute_pmpp <- function(imputed_list, formula_obj, inf_idx, label) {

  m <- length(imputed_list)
  N <- nrow(imputed_list[[1]])

  pred_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = N, ncol = m)
  for (i in seq_len(m)) {
    fit_i         <- glm(formula_obj, data = imputed_list[[i]], family = binomial())
    pred_mat[, i] <- predict(fit_i, type = "response")
  }

  pmpp        <- rowMeans(pred_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  noninf_idx  <- setdiff(seq_len(N), inf_idx)
  pmpp_inf    <- pmpp[inf_idx]
  pmpp_noninf <- pmpp[noninf_idx]
  outcome1    <- imputed_list[[1]][[all.vars(formula_obj)[1]]]

  wtest <- wilcox.test(pmpp_inf, pmpp_noninf, exact = FALSE)

  pmpp_table <- data.frame(
    Case_ID   = inf_idx,
    Outcome   = outcome1[inf_idx],
    PMPP_mean = round(pmpp[inf_idx], 3),
    PMPP_min  = round(apply(pred_mat[inf_idx, , drop = FALSE], 1,
                             min, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    PMPP_max  = round(apply(pred_mat[inf_idx, , drop = FALSE], 1,
                             max, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  ) %>% arrange(desc(PMPP_mean))

  cat(sprintf("%s\n", label))
  cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Influential (n=%d):\n", length(inf_idx)))
  cat(sprintf("    Mean PMPP:   %.3f\n",   mean(pmpp_inf)))
  cat(sprintf("    Median PMPP: %.3f\n",   median(pmpp_inf)))
  cat(sprintf("    IQR:         %.3f – %.3f\n",
              quantile(pmpp_inf, 0.25), quantile(pmpp_inf, 0.75)))
  cat(sprintf("    Range:       %.3f – %.3f\n\n", min(pmpp_inf), max(pmpp_inf)))

  thresholds <- c(0.30, 0.50, 0.70, 0.90)
  cat("  Cases above PMPP thresholds:\n")
  for (thr in thresholds) {
    n_ab <- sum(pmpp_inf >= thr)
    cat(sprintf("    >= %.2f: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
                thr, n_ab, length(pmpp_inf), 100 * n_ab / length(pmpp_inf)))
  }
  cat("\n")

  cat(sprintf("  Non-influential (n=%d):\n", length(noninf_idx)))
  cat(sprintf("    Median PMPP: %.3f\n\n", median(pmpp_noninf)))

  cat(sprintf("  Wilcoxon rank-sum: W = %.1f, p = %.4f\n\n",
              wtest$statistic, wtest$p.value))

  cat("  Per-case PMPP:\n")
  print(pmpp_table, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("\n")

  med_pmpp <- median(pmpp_inf)
  cat("  INTERPRETATION:\n")
  if (med_pmpp >= 0.70) {
    cat(sprintf("  Median PMPP = %.3f. The model assigned high predicted\n",
                med_pmpp))
    cat("  probabilities from predictor values alone. Influential cases\n")
    cat("  are recognised as high-risk by the model. Combined with the\n")
    cat("  leverage decomposition, this supports a coherent high-risk\n")
    cat("  subgroup interpretation.\n\n")
  } else if (med_pmpp >= 0.40) {
    cat(sprintf("  Median PMPP = %.3f. The model partially identified\n",
                med_pmpp))
    cat("  influential cases as elevated risk, but their outcomes exceeded\n")
    cat("  model predictions. Subgroup interpretation is plausible but\n")
    cat("  predictor profiles alone do not fully explain their influence.\n")
    cat("  Acknowledge the partial nature of the subgroup in reporting.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  Median PMPP = %.3f. The model did not identify influential\n",
                med_pmpp))
    cat("  cases as high-risk despite their high discontinuation rate.\n")
    cat("  Influence is driven by residuals (misprediction) more than\n")
    cat("  leverage (unusual predictors). Report findings cautiously;\n")
    cat("  the subgroup interpretation is not supported.\n\n")
  }

  invisible(list(
    pmpp        = pmpp,
    pmpp_inf    = pmpp_inf,
    pmpp_noninf = pmpp_noninf,
    pmpp_table  = pmpp_table,
    wtest       = wtest,
    median_inf  = med_pmpp,
    median_noninf = median(pmpp_noninf)
  ))
}

pmpp_A <- compute_pmpp(imputed_list, formula_A, inf_idx_A,
                        "MODEL A — Personality (Scenario 1, confirmatory)")
pmpp_B <- compute_pmpp(imputed_list, formula_B, inf_idx_B,
                        "MODEL B — Full (Scenario 2, primary diagnostic)")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART D: MAGNITUDE SHIFT INVESTIGATION — capabilityC AND side_effectsC
#
# Chunk 14 Part G found that two predictors in Model B retained significance
# after removing all 30 influential cases but showed substantial OR shifts:
#   capabilityC:   full OR=2.36 [1.70, 3.29] -> clean OR=4.34 [2.60, 7.24]  (+83.6%)
#   side_effectsC: full OR=1.75 [1.21, 2.53] -> clean OR=2.39 [1.46, 3.92]  (+36.5%)
#
# These shifts mean the full-model ORs are attenuated relative to the
# effect in the non-influential population. The direction is stable (both
# positive) but the magnitude is not. Understanding which subgroup of
# influential cases drives the attenuation informs reporting.
#
# APPROACH:
#   D1. Sequential removal: compute ORs after removing:
#       (a) overlap cases only (n=12): tests whether domain-general cases
#           drive the attenuation
#       (b) B-only cases only (n=18): tests whether B-specific cases drive it
#       (c) all B influential cases (n=30): the full clean model (from Chunk 14)
#   D2. Predictor profiles for capabilityC and side_effectsC across
#       influence groups (overlap vs B-only vs non-influential), to
#       understand why these cases attenuate the ORs.
#   D3. Reporting recommendation.
#
# Helper: pool GLM coefficients across imputations using Rubin's rules.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART D: Magnitude shift investigation\n")
cat("capabilityC (+83.6%%) and side_effectsC (+36.5%%) — Model B\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("BACKGROUND:\n")
cat("  Chunk 14 Part G: capabilityC and side_effectsC remain significant\n")
cat("  after removing all 30 influential cases, but ORs shift substantially:\n")
cat("    capabilityC:   2.36 [1.70, 3.29] -> 4.34 [2.60, 7.24]  (+83.6%%)\n")
cat("    side_effectsC: 1.75 [1.21, 2.53] -> 2.39 [1.46, 3.92]  (+36.5%%)\n\n")
cat("  These shifts indicate that influential cases attenuate both\n")
cat("  associations in the full model. This part identifies which\n")
cat("  subgroup of influential cases is responsible.\n\n")

# Helper: pool GLM results from a list of fitted models
pool_glm_manual <- function(fit_list, terms_of_interest = NULL) {

  valid <- Filter(Negate(is.null), fit_list)
  m_v   <- length(valid)
  if (m_v < 2) return(NULL)

  coef_list <- lapply(valid, function(f) {
    s <- summary(f)$coefficients
    data.frame(
      term     = rownames(s),
      estimate = s[, "Estimate"],
      se       = s[, "Std. Error"],
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  all_terms <- unique(unlist(lapply(coef_list, `[[`, "term")))
  if (!is.null(terms_of_interest))
    all_terms <- intersect(all_terms, terms_of_interest)

  bind_rows(lapply(all_terms, function(trm) {

    ests <- sapply(coef_list, function(d) {
      idx <- match(trm, d$term)
      if (is.na(idx)) NA_real_ else d$estimate[idx]
    })
    ses <- sapply(coef_list, function(d) {
      idx <- match(trm, d$term)
      if (is.na(idx)) NA_real_ else d$se[idx]
    })

    vm <- !is.na(ests) & is.finite(ests) & is.finite(ses)
    if (sum(vm) < 2)
      return(data.frame(term = trm, OR = NA_real_,
                        OR_lower = NA_real_, OR_upper = NA_real_,
                        OR_CI = "NA", p.value = NA_real_,
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

    Q_bar <- mean(ests[vm])
    U_bar <- mean(ses[vm]^2)
    B     <- var(ests[vm])
    T_var <- U_bar + (1 + 1 / sum(vm)) * B
    se_r  <- sqrt(T_var)
    z     <- Q_bar / se_r
    p_val <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(z))
    or    <- exp(Q_bar)
    lo    <- exp(Q_bar - 1.96 * se_r)
    hi    <- exp(Q_bar + 1.96 * se_r)

    data.frame(
      term     = trm,
      OR       = round(or,    3),
      OR_lower = round(lo,    3),
      OR_upper = round(hi,    3),
      OR_CI    = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", or, lo, hi),
      p.value  = round(p_val, 4),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
}

focus_terms <- c("capabilityC", "side_effectsC")

# ── D1: Sequential removal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("D1: Sequential removal — which influential subgroup drives attenuation?\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

cat(sprintf("  Full model (all N=%d):             reference\n", N))
cat(sprintf("  Remove overlap only (n=%d):        N=%d remaining\n",
            length(overlap_idx), N - length(overlap_idx)))
cat(sprintf("  Remove B-only only (n=%d):         N=%d remaining\n",
            length(B_only_idx), N - length(B_only_idx)))
cat(sprintf("  Remove all B influential (n=%d):   N=%d remaining\n\n",
            length(inf_idx_B), N - length(inf_idx_B)))

# (a) Remove overlap only
idx_no_overlap <- setdiff(seq_len(N), overlap_idx)
fits_no_overlap <- lapply(seq_len(m), function(i) {
  tryCatch(
    glm(formula_B, data = imputed_list[[i]][idx_no_overlap, ],
        family = binomial()),
    error = function(e) NULL)
})
res_no_overlap <- pool_glm_manual(fits_no_overlap, focus_terms)

# (b) Remove B-only only
idx_no_Bonly <- setdiff(seq_len(N), B_only_idx)
fits_no_Bonly <- lapply(seq_len(m), function(i) {
  tryCatch(
    glm(formula_B, data = imputed_list[[i]][idx_no_Bonly, ],
        family = binomial()),
    error = function(e) NULL)
})
res_no_Bonly <- pool_glm_manual(fits_no_Bonly, focus_terms)

# (c) Remove all B influential (= clean model from Chunk 14)
fits_clean_B <- lapply(seq_len(m), function(i) {
  tryCatch(
    glm(formula_B, data = imputed_list[[i]][noninf_idx_B, ],
        family = binomial()),
    error = function(e) NULL)
})
res_clean_B <- pool_glm_manual(fits_clean_B, focus_terms)

# Full model reference (from Chunk 12 saved results)
full_ref <- chunk12$summaries$model_B %>%
  filter(term %in% focus_terms) %>%
  select(term, OR_CI_full = OR_CI)

# Assemble comparison table
seq_table <- full_ref %>%
  left_join(
    res_no_overlap %>% select(term, OR_CI_overlap = OR_CI),
    by = "term") %>%
  left_join(
    res_no_Bonly %>% select(term, OR_CI_Bonly = OR_CI),
    by = "term") %>%
  left_join(
    res_clean_B %>% select(term, OR_CI_clean = OR_CI),
    by = "term")

cat("  Sequential removal results for capabilityC and side_effectsC:\n\n")
cat(sprintf("  %-14s  %-22s  %-22s  %-22s  %-22s\n",
            "Term", "Full (N=422)", "No overlap (N=410)",
            "No B-only (N=404)", "Clean (N=392)"))
cat(sprintf("  %s\n", strrep("─", 100)))
for (i in seq_len(nrow(seq_table))) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  %-22s  %-22s  %-22s  %-22s\n",
              seq_table$term[i],
              seq_table$OR_CI_full[i],
              seq_table$OR_CI_overlap[i],
              seq_table$OR_CI_Bonly[i],
              seq_table$OR_CI_clean[i]))
}
cat("\n")

cat("  INTERPRETATION GUIDE:\n")
cat("  If OR shifts most when removing overlap cases:\n")
cat("    -> Domain-general influential cases (unusual on both predictor sets)\n")
cat("       are primarily responsible for the attenuation.\n")
cat("  If OR shifts most when removing B-only cases:\n")
cat("    -> Cases unusual on clinical predictors specifically drive it.\n")
cat("  If OR shifts only after removing all B influential:\n")
cat("    -> Both subgroups contribute roughly equally.\n\n")

# ── D2: Predictor profiles for capabilityC and side_effectsC ─────────────────
cat("D2: Predictor profiles — capabilityC and side_effectsC\n")
cat("    across influence groups (pooled across imputations)\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

cat("  Understanding WHY these cases attenuate the ORs requires examining\n")
cat("  their values on capabilityC and side_effectsC. Attenuation occurs\n")
cat("  when influential cases have extreme predictor values but outcomes\n")
cat("  that contradict the direction of the main effect (e.g., high\n")
cat("  capability but did not stop; high side effects but did not stop).\n\n")

profile_vars <- c("capabilityC", "side_effectsC")

profile_pooled <- lapply(profile_vars, function(v) {
  vals_list <- lapply(seq_len(m), function(i) {
    d <- imputed_list[[i]]
    data.frame(
      group = dplyr::case_when(
        seq_len(N) %in% overlap_idx ~ "Overlap (both)",
        seq_len(N) %in% B_only_idx  ~ "B-only influential",
        seq_len(N) %in% A_only_idx  ~ "A-only influential",
        TRUE                        ~ "Non-influential"
      ),
      value = d[[v]]
    )
  })
  all_vals <- bind_rows(vals_list) %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_val = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      sd_val   = round(sd(value,   na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(variable = v) %>%
    select(variable, group, mean_val, sd_val)
})

profile_df <- bind_rows(profile_pooled)

cat(sprintf("  %-15s  %-22s  %s\n", "Variable", "Group", "Mean (SD)"))
cat(sprintf("  %s\n", strrep("─", 60)))
for (i in seq_len(nrow(profile_df))) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-15s  %-22s  %.3f (%.3f)\n",
              profile_df$variable[i],
              profile_df$group[i],
              profile_df$mean_val[i],
              profile_df$sd_val[i]))
}
cat("\n")

cat("  KEY QUESTION: Do influential cases have lower capabilityC and/or\n")
cat("  higher side_effectsC than non-influential cases?\n")
cat("  If NO (similar or reversed values): attenuation is driven by\n")
cat("    outcome mismatch — cases with low capability/high side effects\n")
cat("    who did NOT stop, pulling the OR toward 1.0.\n")
cat("  If YES (extreme in predicted direction): attenuation is driven by\n")
cat("    leverage — cases dominating the regression with extreme but\n")
cat("    consistent predictor-outcome patterns.\n\n")

# ── D3: Reporting recommendation ─────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("D3: Reporting recommendation\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

cat("BASED ON D1-D2 FINDINGS:\n\n")
cat("  1. Report both the full-model and clean-model ORs for capabilityC\n")
cat("     and side_effectsC in the sensitivity analysis section.\n")
cat("     The direction of both effects is stable; the magnitude is not.\n\n")
cat("  2. The full-model ORs (capabilityC=2.36, side_effectsC=1.75) should\n")
cat("     be understood as conservative estimates attenuated by a small\n")
cat("     subgroup of influential cases. The clean-model ORs represent\n")
cat("     the association in the remaining 392 participants.\n\n")
cat("  3. D1 results will determine whether to attribute the attenuation\n")
cat("     primarily to overlap cases (domain-general influence) or B-only\n")
cat("     cases (clinical predictor-specific influence). Report whichever\n")
cat("     subgroup drives the step change.\n\n")
cat("SUGGESTED REPORTING LANGUAGE:\n")
cat("  'Two predictors — perceived capability and side effects — retained\n")
cat("  significance across both the full and sensitivity samples, though\n")
cat("  their effect sizes were attenuated in the full model. Removal of\n")
cat("  influential cases (n=30) increased the OR for capability from\n")
cat("  2.36 to 4.34 and for side effects from 1.75 to 2.39, suggesting\n")
cat("  that a small subgroup of influential cases moderated these\n")
cat("  associations. Full-model estimates are reported as conservative\n")
cat("  values; sensitivity estimates are reported in supplementary\n")
cat("  materials.'\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART E: SYNTHESIS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART E: Synthesis\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("MODEL A — Personality influential cases (n=23, Scenario 1):\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Outcome rate:     91.3%% (vs 23.6%% base rate)\n")
cat("  Scenario 1:       5/6 predictors flagged (all high-extreme)\n")
cat("                    DBAS_Medications, SURPS_Hopelessness, CISS_Emotion,\n")
cat("                    DBAS_Consequences, BFI_Neuroticism\n")
cat("  Leverage/PMPP:    see Part B and Part C results above\n\n")
cat("  EXPECTED PATTERN:\n")
cat("  Scenario 1 already supports a coherent clinical subgroup. Parts B\n")
cat("  and C should confirm this:\n")
cat("  - Part B: predominantly high leverage / low residual\n")
cat("  - Part C: median PMPP >= 0.60\n")
cat("  If confirmed: these are high-risk participants with elevated\n")
cat("  dysfunctional sleep beliefs, hopelessness, emotional coping style,\n")
cat("  and neuroticism who overwhelmingly discontinued. The model\n")
cat("  identified them as high-risk from personality predictors alone.\n")
cat("  Note that DBAS_Medications is the single robust predictor after\n")
cat("  their removal; the other 4 flagged predictors are non-significant\n")
cat("  in the broader sample.\n\n")
cat("  If Parts B/C show residual-driven pattern (unexpected given Scenario 1):\n")
cat("  Report that the personality model identified the subgroup by predictor\n")
cat("  profile but could not fully predict their near-ceiling outcome rate.\n\n")

cat("MODEL B — Full influential cases (n=30, Scenario 2):\n")
cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("  Outcome rate:     86.7%% (vs 22.7%% non-influential)\n")
cat("  Scenario 2:       5/9 predictors flagged — heterogeneous profile\n")
cat("                    High: bzra_daily_dose_eq, c_lengthofuse,\n")
cat("                          n_sleep_conditions, wdl_symptom_count\n")
cat("                    Low:  DBAS_Medications\n")
cat("  Robust predictors (Chunk 14): DBAS_Medications (stable),\n")
cat("                    capabilityC (magnitude shift +83.6%%),\n")
cat("                    side_effectsC (magnitude shift +36.5%%)\n")
cat("  Gains significance: c_lengthofuse\n\n")
cat("  The Scenario 2 classification means the subgroup conclusion is\n")
cat("  less certain than for Model A. Parts B and C are the key tests:\n")
cat("  - If PMPP >= 0.60 AND high leverage pattern: the clinical predictors\n")
cat("    collectively identify these as a high-burden subgroup (long-term\n")
cat("    high-dose users with multiple sleep conditions) despite not all\n")
cat("    predictors being flagged.\n")
cat("  - If PMPP < 0.40 OR residual-dominated: the 86.7%% discontinuation\n")
cat("    exceeds what the predictor profile predicts; these cases have\n")
cat("    disproportionate leverage not fully justified by their predictor\n")
cat("    values. Report subgroup characterisation with caution.\n\n")
cat("  The capabilityC and side_effectsC magnitude shifts (Part D) are\n")
cat("  a separate reporting issue. Both effects are real but attenuated\n")
cat("  in the full model. Use the clean-model estimates as the primary\n")
cat("  effect size reference with full-model estimates as conservative\n")
cat("  lower bounds.\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART F: SAVE RESULTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART F: Saving results\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

chunk14b_results <- list(

  decomp_A       = decomp_A,
  decomp_B       = decomp_B,
  pmpp_A         = pmpp_A,
  pmpp_B         = pmpp_B,

  magnitude_shift = list(
    sequential_removal = seq_table,
    predictor_profiles = profile_df,
    focus_terms        = focus_terms
  ),

  metadata = list(
    outcome     = outcome_var,
    vars_A      = vars_A,
    vars_B      = vars_B,
    inf_idx_A   = inf_idx_A,
    inf_idx_B   = inf_idx_B,
    overlap_idx = overlap_idx,
    B_only_idx  = B_only_idx,
    A_only_idx  = A_only_idx,
    N           = N,
    m           = m,
    notes = paste0(
      "Chunk 14b corrected to align with Chunk 14 actual results. ",
      "Model A: n=23 influential, Scenario 1 (5/6 flagged). ",
      "Model B: n=30 influential, Scenario 2 (5/9 flagged). ",
      "stop_wdl_exp_combined has 3 levels (no separation issue). ",
      "Part D investigates capabilityC (+83.6%) and side_effectsC (+36.5%) ",
      "magnitude shifts via sequential removal of overlap vs B-only cases.")
  )
)

saveRDS(chunk14b_results,                    "chunk14b_results.rds")
write_csv(decomp_A$case_df,                  "chunk14b_leverage_residual_modelA.csv")
write_csv(decomp_B$case_df,                  "chunk14b_leverage_residual_modelB.csv")
write_csv(pmpp_A$pmpp_table,                 "chunk14b_pmpp_modelA.csv")
write_csv(pmpp_B$pmpp_table,                 "chunk14b_pmpp_modelB.csv")
write_csv(seq_table,                         "chunk14b_sequential_removal_modelB.csv")
write_csv(profile_df,                        "chunk14b_capability_sideeffects_profiles.csv")

cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_results.rds\n")
cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_leverage_residual_modelA.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_leverage_residual_modelB.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_pmpp_modelA.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_pmpp_modelB.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_sequential_removal_modelB.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: chunk14b_capability_sideeffects_profiles.csv\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 14b COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("QUESTION 1 — Influence mechanism: subgroup or outlier?\n")
cat(sprintf("  Model A: %d influential cases, 91.3%% discontinued, Scenario 1\n",
            length(inf_idx_A)))
cat("  -> High leverage / low residual expected (coherent subgroup)\n")
cat("  -> PMPP confirmatory; expected median >= 0.60\n\n")
cat(sprintf("  Model B: %d influential cases, 86.7%% discontinued, Scenario 2\n",
            length(inf_idx_B)))
cat("  -> Mechanism uncertain; Part B and C are primary diagnostics\n")
cat("  -> PMPP median determines subgroup vs outlier interpretation\n\n")

cat("QUESTION 2 — Magnitude shifts in capabilityC and side_effectsC:\n")
cat("  capabilityC:   full=2.36 -> clean=4.34 (+83.6%%)\n")
cat("  side_effectsC: full=1.75 -> clean=2.39 (+36.5%%)\n")
cat("  -> D1 sequential removal identifies which influential subgroup\n")
cat("     (overlap vs B-only) drives the attenuation\n")
cat("  -> D2 profiles explain the mechanism\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. chunk14b_results.rds\n")
cat("  2. chunk14b_leverage_residual_modelA.csv\n")
cat("  3. chunk14b_leverage_residual_modelB.csv\n")
cat("  4. chunk14b_pmpp_modelA.csv\n")
cat("  5. chunk14b_pmpp_modelB.csv\n")
cat("  6. chunk14b_sequential_removal_modelB.csv\n")
cat("  7. chunk14b_capability_sideeffects_profiles.csv\n\n")

cat("NOTES:\n")
cat(sprintf("  - m = %d imputations throughout\n", m))
cat("  - Leverage threshold: 2x median of non-influential cases\n")
cat("  - Residual threshold: 2x median of non-influential cases\n")
cat("  - Wilcoxon rank-sum used for leverage and residual comparisons\n")
cat("  - PMPP derived from predict(..., type='response') averaged\n")
cat("    across imputations, independent of observed outcome\n")
cat("  - Sequential removal pools via Rubin's rules (manual)\n")
cat("  - No separation issue in stop_wdl_exp_combined (3 levels,\n")
cat("    all imputations converged in Chunk 14); Firth regression\n")
cat("    not required\n\n")

cat("REFERENCES:\n")
cat("  Cook RD (1977). Detection of influential observations. Technometrics.\n")
cat("  Cook RD, Weisberg S (1982). Residuals and Influence in Regression.\n")
cat("  Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.\n\n")

cat("✓ Ready for Chunk 15\n\n")
```





## Complete Case Analysis
```{r}
#==============================================================================
# CHUNK 15: COMPLETE CASE SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS
#==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   Compare MI results (Chunk 12) to complete case analysis for Model A
#   and Model B. Assesses whether imputation meaningfully changed findings.
#
# Models:
#   Model A — Personality (interpretation set)
#             5 predictors: DBAS_Medications, SURPS_Hopelessness,
#             CISS_Emotion, BFI_Neuroticism, DBAS_Consequences
#   Model B — Full (interpretation set)
#             8 predictors: capabilityC, DBAS_Medications, side_effectsC,
#             bzra_daily_diazepam_eq, stop_wdl_exp_combined,
#             wdl_symptom_count, n_sleep_conditions, c_lengthofuse
#
# Complete case definition:
#   Non-missing on ALL predictors (union of both models) + outcome.
#   Both CC models use the IDENTICAL case pool for fair comparison.
#   Any difference in estimates reflects imputation, not sample composition.
#
# Variable sourcing — ALL from SIMOA_analysis.rds raw items:
#
#   Subscales (DBAS, CISS, BFI, SURPS):
#     Reconstructed from raw items using make_subscale(), matching
#     Chunk 9 exactly (na.rm = FALSE, all items required).
#     Reverse-coded items use _r suffix applied in Chunk 6.
#
#   capabilityC and side_effectsC:
#     COM-B composite scores centred in Chunk 9. Reconstructed here
#     from their raw Likert items in SIMOA_analysis.rds:
#       capabilityC   <- mean(capability_1) centred by subtracting mean
#       side_effectsC <- mean(side_effects_1:4) centred by subtracting mean
#     Centring uses the complete-case mean (not the full-sample mean used
#     in Chunk 9). This is noted as a minor methodological deviation.
#
#   stopped_bzra (outcome):
#     Not present in SIMOA_analysis.rds — derived here from freq_dich
#     using the identical logic as Chunk 9:
#       1 = Stoppers  (freq_dich 0 or 1)
#       0 = Continuers (freq_dich 2)
#
#   All other Model B variables (bzra_daily_diazepam_eq, stop_wdl_exp_combined,
#   wdl_symptom_count, n_sleep_conditions, c_lengthofuse):
#     Taken directly from SIMOA_analysis.rds as-is.
#
# References:
#   Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.
#   White IR et al. (2011). Multiple imputation using chained equations.
#     Statistics in Medicine, 30(4), 377-399.
#==============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(pROC)
library(broom)
library(car)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 15: COMPLETE CASE SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS\n")
cat("Model A (Personality) and Model B (Full)\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART A: LOAD DATA AND MI RESULTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART A: Loading data and Chunk 12 MI results\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

if (!file.exists("SIMOA_analysis.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: SIMOA_analysis.rds not found. Run Chunk 6 first.")
if (!file.exists("regression_comparison_results.rds"))
  stop("ERROR: regression_comparison_results.rds not found. Run Chunk 12 first.")

raw_data <- readRDS("SIMOA_analysis.rds")
chunk12  <- readRDS("regression_comparison_results.rds")

outcome_var <- chunk12$metadata$outcome
vars_A      <- chunk12$variables$model_A
vars_B      <- chunk12$variables$model_B
m_imps      <- chunk12$metadata$m   # 42 — pulled directly, not hardcoded

cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded SIMOA_analysis.rds      (%d rows × %d columns)\n",
            nrow(raw_data), ncol(raw_data)))
cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded regression_comparison_results.rds\n\n"))
cat(sprintf("Outcome:          %s\n", outcome_var))
cat(sprintf("Imputations (m):  %d\n", m_imps))
cat(sprintf("Model A vars (%d): %s\n", length(vars_A), paste(vars_A, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("Model B vars (%d): %s\n", length(vars_B), paste(vars_B, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("Union pool vars:  %s\n\n",
            paste(c(outcome_var, union(vars_A, vars_B)), collapse = ", ")))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART B: REVERSE CODING VERIFICATION
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART B: Reverse coding verification\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

cat("NOTE: Reverse coding was applied in Chunk 6. Items stored as _r.\n")
cat("      Verifying _r items are present before subscale creation.\n\n")

reversed_expected <- c(
  "reserved_r", "lazy_r", "relaxed_r", "few_interests_r", "find_fault_r",
  "surps1_r", "surps4_r", "surps7_r", "surps13_r", "surps20_r", "surps23_r"
)

present <- reversed_expected[reversed_expected %in% names(raw_data)]
absent  <- reversed_expected[!reversed_expected %in% names(raw_data)]

cat(sprintf("  Reversed items present: %d / %d\n",
            length(present), length(reversed_expected)))

if (length(absent) > 0) {
  cat("  ✗ MISSING reversed items:\n")
  for (v in absent) cat(sprintf("    • %s\n", v))
  stop("Cannot proceed — reversed items missing. Check Chunk 6 output.")
} else {
  cat("  ✓ All reversed items confirmed present\n\n")
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART C: SUBSCALE CONSTRUCTION (IDENTICAL TO CHUNK 9)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART C: Subscale construction (na.rm = FALSE, matching Chunk 9)\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

make_subscale <- function(data, items, var_name) {
  available <- items[items %in% names(data)]
  missing   <- items[!items %in% names(data)]

  if (length(missing) > 0)
    cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %s: items not found: %s\n",
                var_name, paste(missing, collapse = ", ")))

  if (length(available) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  ✗  %s: no items found — skipping\n", var_name))
    return(data)
  }

  data[[var_name]] <- rowMeans(data[, available, drop = FALSE], na.rm = FALSE)
  n_complete <- sum(!is.na(data[[var_name]]))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓  %-35s %d/%d items  %d complete cases\n",
              var_name, length(available), length(items), n_complete))
  data
}

# ── DBAS-16 subscales ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("DBAS-16 subscales:\n")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  c("dbas_3","dbas_4","dbas_5","dbas_7","dbas_9","dbas_12","dbas_16"),
  "DBAS_Consequences")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  c("dbas_8","dbas_10","dbas_11","dbas_14"),
  "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  c("dbas1","dbas_2"),
  "DBAS_Expectations")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  c("dbas_6","dbas_13","dbas_15"),
  "DBAS_Medications")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  c("dbas1", paste0("dbas_", 2:16)),
  "DBAS_Total")
cat("\n")

# ── CISS subscales ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("CISS subscales:\n")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  paste0("ciss", c(2, 6, 8, 11, 13, 16, 19)), "CISS_Task")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  paste0("ciss", c(3, 5, 10, 12, 14, 17, 20)), "CISS_Emotion")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  paste0("ciss", c(1, 4, 7, 9, 15, 18, 21)),  "CISS_Avoidance")
cat("\n")

# ── BFI-10 subscales (using _r items) ────────────────────────────────────────
cat("BFI-10 subscales:\n")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data, c("reserved_r","outgoing"),        "BFI_Extraversion")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data, c("find_fault_r","trusting"),      "BFI_Agreeableness")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data, c("lazy_r","thorough"),            "BFI_Conscientiousness")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data, c("relaxed_r","nervous"),          "BFI_Neuroticism")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data, c("few_interests_r","imagination"), "BFI_Openness")
cat("\n")

# ── SURPS subscales ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("SURPS subscales:\n")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  c("surps1_r","surps4_r","surps7_r","surps13_r","surps17","surps20_r","surps23_r"),
  "SURPS_Hopelessness")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  paste0("surps", c(2, 5, 11, 15, 22)),    "SURPS_Impulsivity")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  paste0("surps", c(3, 6, 9, 12, 16, 19)), "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking")
raw_data <- make_subscale(raw_data,
  paste0("surps", c(8, 10, 14, 18, 21)),   "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity")
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART D: CONSTRUCT COM-B COMPOSITE SCORES FROM RAW ITEMS
#
# capabilityC and side_effectsC are mean Likert composites centred by
# subtracting the sample mean. Reconstructed here from raw items in
# SIMOA_analysis.rds using the same item sets as Chunk 9.
#
# Centring: subtract column mean (na.rm = TRUE) to match Chunk 9 approach.
# NOTE: centring uses the complete-case mean here, not the full-sample mean
# from Chunk 9. This is a minor methodological deviation documented below.
#
# Only capabilityC and side_effectsC are strictly required for Model B.
# The others are constructed for completeness / future use.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART D: Constructing COM-B composite scores from raw items\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

cat("NOTE: Composites centred using complete-case column mean (minor\n")
cat("      deviation from Chunk 9, which used full-sample imputed mean).\n\n")

centre_composite <- function(data, items, var_name) {
  available <- items[items %in% names(data)]
  missing   <- items[!items %in% names(data)]

  if (length(missing) > 0)
    cat(sprintf("  ⚠️  %s: items not found: %s\n",
                var_name, paste(missing, collapse = ", ")))

  if (length(available) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  ✗  %s: no items found — skipping\n", var_name))
    return(data)
  }

  raw_mean <- rowMeans(data[, available, drop = FALSE], na.rm = FALSE)
  col_mean <- mean(raw_mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  data[[var_name]] <- raw_mean - col_mean

  n_complete <- sum(!is.na(data[[var_name]]))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓  %-35s %d/%d items  %d complete  mean centred at %.3f\n",
              var_name, length(available), length(items), n_complete, col_mean))
  data
}

# Capability — single item; try capability_1 first, fall back to capability
if ("capability_1" %in% names(raw_data)) {
  raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data, "capability_1", "capabilityC")
} else if ("capability" %in% names(raw_data)) {
  raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data, "capability", "capabilityC")
  cat("  (Note: used 'capability' — 'capability_1' not found)\n")
} else {
  stop("ERROR: Neither 'capability_1' nor 'capability' found in raw_data.")
}

# Side effects — 4-item composite
raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data,
  paste0("side_effects_", 1:4), "side_effectsC")

# Additional COM-B composites (not required for models but built for completeness)
cat("\n  Additional COM-B composites (for completeness):\n")
raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data,
  c("benefit_1","benefit_4","benefit_5","benefit_6"), "benefitsC")
raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data,
  c("adls_1","adls_2"), "adlsC")
raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data,
  c("dependence_1","dependence_2","dependence_3"), "dependenceC")
raw_data <- centre_composite(raw_data,
  c("motivation_1","motivation_2","motivation_3"), "motivationC")
cat("\n")

# Verify capabilityC and side_effectsC were created
for (v in c("capabilityC", "side_effectsC")) {
  if (!v %in% names(raw_data))
    stop(sprintf("ERROR: %s could not be constructed. Check raw item names.", v))
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ %s verified: %d non-missing values\n",
              v, sum(!is.na(raw_data[[v]]))))
}
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART E: VERIFY REMAINING MODEL B VARIABLES AND DERIVE OUTCOME
#
# stopped_bzra is not present in SIMOA_analysis.rds — it was derived in
# Chunk 9 from freq_dich. Derived here using identical logic:
#   1 = Stoppers  (freq_dich 0 or 1)
#   0 = Continuers (freq_dich 2)
#   NA otherwise
#
# All other Model B variables (bzra_daily_diazepam_eq, stop_wdl_exp_combined,
# wdl_symptom_count, n_sleep_conditions, c_lengthofuse) are taken directly
# from SIMOA_analysis.rds.
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART E: Verifying remaining Model B variables and deriving outcome\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

# ── Derive outcome from freq_dich (matches Chunk 9 exactly) ──────────────────
if (!outcome_var %in% names(raw_data)) {

  if (!"freq_dich" %in% names(raw_data))
    stop("ERROR: 'freq_dich' not found in raw_data — cannot derive stopped_bzra.")

  raw_data[[outcome_var]] <- dplyr::case_when(
    raw_data$freq_dich %in% c(0, 1) ~ 1L,
    raw_data$freq_dich == 2          ~ 0L,
    TRUE                             ~ NA_integer_
  )

  cat(sprintf("  ✓ '%s' derived from freq_dich (matching Chunk 9 logic)\n",
              outcome_var))
  cat(sprintf("    Stoppers   (1): %d\n",
              sum(raw_data[[outcome_var]] == 1L, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("    Continuers (0): %d\n",
              sum(raw_data[[outcome_var]] == 0L, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("    Missing  (NA): %d\n\n",
              sum(is.na(raw_data[[outcome_var]]))))

} else {
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ '%s' already present in raw_data (%d non-missing)\n\n",
              outcome_var, sum(!is.na(raw_data[[outcome_var]]))))
}

# ── Verify direct Model B variables ──────────────────────────────────────────
direct_vars <- c("bzra_daily_diazepam_eq", "stop_wdl_exp_combined",
                 "wdl_symptom_count", "n_sleep_conditions", "c_lengthofuse")

for (v in direct_vars) {
  if (v %in% names(raw_data)) {
    cat(sprintf("  ✓  %-35s %d non-missing\n", v, sum(!is.na(raw_data[[v]]))))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  ✗  %-35s NOT FOUND in raw_data\n", v))
  }
}
cat("\n")

# Ensure stop_wdl_exp_combined is a factor with levels 0:5 matching Chunk 9/10
if ("stop_wdl_exp_combined" %in% names(raw_data)) {
  if (!is.factor(raw_data$stop_wdl_exp_combined)) {
    raw_data$stop_wdl_exp_combined <- factor(raw_data$stop_wdl_exp_combined,
                                             levels = 0:5)
    cat("  ✓ stop_wdl_exp_combined coerced to factor (levels 0:5)\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("  ✓ stop_wdl_exp_combined already a factor\n\n")
  }
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART F: FINAL VARIABLE CHECK AND DEFINE COMPLETE CASES
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART F: Final variable check and defining complete cases\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

all_model_vars <- union(vars_A, vars_B)
still_missing  <- all_model_vars[!all_model_vars %in% names(raw_data)]

if (length(still_missing) > 0) {
  cat("  ✗ Variables still missing after all construction steps:\n")
  for (v in still_missing) cat(sprintf("    • %s\n", v))
  stop("Cannot proceed. Check item names in SIMOA_analysis.rds.")
} else {
  cat("  ✓ All model variables verified present\n\n")
}

cat("NOTE: Completeness defined on UNION of Model A + Model B vars + outcome.\n")
cat("      Both CC models use the identical case pool.\n\n")

cc_vars <- c(outcome_var, all_model_vars)
cc_data <- raw_data %>%
  select(all_of(cc_vars)) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))

# Drop unused factor levels after subsetting
if ("stop_wdl_exp_combined" %in% names(cc_data))
  cc_data$stop_wdl_exp_combined <- droplevels(cc_data$stop_wdl_exp_combined)

n_total     <- nrow(raw_data)
n_cc        <- nrow(cc_data)
n_excluded  <- n_total - n_cc
n_events    <- sum(cc_data[[outcome_var]])
n_nonevents <- n_cc - n_events

cat("COMPLETE CASE SUMMARY:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat(sprintf("  Full sample:        %d\n", n_total))
cat(sprintf("  Complete cases:     %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_cc, 100 * n_cc / n_total))
cat(sprintf("  Excluded (missing): %d (%.1f%%)\n\n", n_excluded,
            100 * n_excluded / n_total))

cat("OUTCOME IN COMPLETE CASES:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Discontinued (1): %d (%.1f%%)\n", n_events,
            100 * n_events / n_cc))
cat(sprintf("  Continued    (0): %d (%.1f%%)\n\n", n_nonevents,
            100 * n_nonevents / n_cc))

cat("EPV:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Model A (%d predictors): EPV = %.1f\n",
            length(vars_A), n_events / length(vars_A)))
cat(sprintf("  Model B (%d predictors): EPV = %.1f\n\n",
            length(vars_B), n_events / length(vars_B)))

cat("MISSINGNESS PER MODEL VARIABLE (full sample):\n")
for (v in cc_vars) {
  n_miss <- sum(is.na(raw_data[[v]]))
  cat(sprintf("  %-35s %d missing (%.1f%%)\n",
              v, n_miss, 100 * n_miss / n_total))
}
cat("\n")

# Report stop_wdl_exp_combined level counts in CC sample
if ("stop_wdl_exp_combined" %in% names(cc_data)) {
  cat("stop_wdl_exp_combined level counts in CC sample:\n")
  print(table(cc_data$stop_wdl_exp_combined, useNA = "ifany"))
  cat("\n")
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART G: FIT COMPLETE CASE MODELS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART G: Fitting complete case models\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

formula_A_cc <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(vars_A, collapse = " + ")))
formula_B_cc <- as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", paste(vars_B, collapse = " + ")))

cat(sprintf("Model A CC formula: %s\n",   deparse(formula_A_cc)))
cat(sprintf("Model B CC formula: %s\n\n", deparse(formula_B_cc)))

model_A_cc <- glm(formula_A_cc, data = cc_data, family = binomial())
model_B_cc <- glm(formula_B_cc, data = cc_data, family = binomial())

cat("✓ Model A CC fitted\n")
cat("✓ Model B CC fitted\n\n")

# Check for separation warnings
if (any(is.na(coef(model_A_cc))))
  cat("⚠️  Model A CC: NA coefficients detected — possible separation\n\n")
if (any(is.na(coef(model_B_cc))))
  cat("⚠️  Model B CC: NA coefficients detected — possible separation\n\n")

# VIF — GVIF used automatically for factor predictors
cat("VIF CHECK (complete case models):\n")
for (label in c("A", "B")) {
  mod      <- if (label == "A") model_A_cc else model_B_cc
  vif_vals <- tryCatch(car::vif(mod), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(vif_vals)) {
    vif_num <- if (is.matrix(vif_vals)) vif_vals[, 1] else vif_vals
    cat(sprintf("  Model %s: max VIF/GVIF = %.2f\n", label, max(vif_num)))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  Model %s: VIF calculation failed\n", label))
  }
}
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART H: EXTRACT CC COEFFICIENTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART H: Extracting CC coefficients\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

extract_cc <- function(model, label) {
  broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      OR_CI = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", estimate, conf.low, conf.high),
      sig   = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
        p.value < 0.10  ~ ".",
        TRUE            ~ ""
      ),
      model = label
    ) %>%
    select(model, term,
           CC_OR    = estimate,
           CC_lower = conf.low,
           CC_upper = conf.high,
           CC_OR_CI = OR_CI,
           CC_p     = p.value,
           CC_sig   = sig)
}

cc_A <- extract_cc(model_A_cc, "Model A CC")
cc_B <- extract_cc(model_B_cc, "Model B CC")

cat("MODEL A CC COEFFICIENTS:\n")
print(cc_A %>% select(term, CC_OR_CI, CC_p, CC_sig), row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")

cat("MODEL B CC COEFFICIENTS:\n")
print(cc_B %>% select(term, CC_OR_CI, CC_p, CC_sig), row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART I: AUC FOR CC MODELS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("PART I: AUC (bootstrap CI, B=2000)\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

calc_auc <- function(model, data, outcome_var, boot_n = 2000, seed = 12345) {
  pred    <- predict(model, type = "response")
  roc_obj <- roc(data[[outcome_var]], pred, quiet = TRUE)
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  set.seed(seed)
  ci_vals <- ci.auc(roc_obj, method = "bootstrap", boot.n = boot_n)
  list(
    auc       = auc_val,
    lower     = ci_vals[1],
    upper     = ci_vals[3],
    formatted = sprintf("%.3f [%.3f, %.3f]", auc_val, ci_vals[1], ci_vals[3])
  )
}

cat("Computing AUC for Model A CC (B=2000)...\n")
auc_A_cc <- calc_auc(model_A_cc, cc_data, outcome_var)
cat(sprintf("  ✓ Model A CC AUC: %s\n\n", auc_A_cc$formatted))

cat("Computing AUC for Model B CC (B=2000)...\n")
auc_B_cc <- calc_auc(model_B_cc, cc_data, outcome_var)
cat(sprintf("  ✓ Model B CC AUC: %s\n\n", auc_B_cc$formatted))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPER: EXTRACT MI SUMMARIES FROM CHUNK 12
# Chunk 12 stores auc as a data frame with AUC_mean / AUC_lower /
# AUC_upper / AUC_formatted columns (confirmed from Chunk 12 source).
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

get_mi_summary <- function(chunk12, model_key) {
  chunk12$summaries[[model_key]] %>%
    mutate(term = as.character(term)) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    select(term,
           MI_OR    = OR,
           MI_lower = OR_lower,
           MI_upper = OR_upper,
           MI_OR_CI = OR_CI,
           MI_p     = p.value,
           MI_sig   = sig)
}

extract_mi_auc <- function(auc_obj) {
  if (is.data.frame(auc_obj)) {
    list(AUC       = auc_obj$AUC_mean[1],
         AUC_lower = auc_obj$AUC_lower[1],
         AUC_upper = auc_obj$AUC_upper[1],
         formatted = auc_obj$AUC_formatted[1])
  } else if (is.list(auc_obj)) {
    `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b
    list(AUC       = auc_obj$AUC_mean   %||% auc_obj$auc,
         AUC_lower = auc_obj$AUC_lower  %||% auc_obj$lower,
         AUC_upper = auc_obj$AUC_upper  %||% auc_obj$upper,
         formatted = auc_obj$AUC_formatted %||% auc_obj$formatted)
  } else {
    stop("Unrecognised AUC object format in chunk12$auc. Check Chunk 12 output.")
  }
}

mi_A     <- get_mi_summary(chunk12, "model_A")
mi_B     <- get_mi_summary(chunk12, "model_B")
mi_auc_A <- extract_mi_auc(chunk12$auc$model_A)
mi_auc_B <- extract_mi_auc(chunk12$auc$model_B)

cat(sprintf("MI AUC (Model A): %s\n",   mi_auc_A$formatted))
cat(sprintf("MI AUC (Model B): %s\n\n", mi_auc_B$formatted))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART J: CC vs MI COMPARISON — MODEL A
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART J: CC vs MI COMPARISON — MODEL A\n")
cat(sprintf("(%s)\n", paste(vars_A, collapse = ", ")))
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

build_comparison <- function(cc_df, mi_df) {
  cc_df <- cc_df %>% mutate(term = as.character(term))
  mi_df <- mi_df %>% mutate(term = as.character(term))

  full_join(mi_df, cc_df %>% select(-model), by = "term") %>%
    mutate(
      OR_change_pct = round(100 * (CC_OR - MI_OR) / MI_OR, 1),
      Direction     = case_when(
        CC_OR > MI_OR ~ "CC higher",
        CC_OR < MI_OR ~ "CC lower",
        TRUE          ~ "No change"
      ),
      Sig_consistency = case_when(
        MI_p < 0.05 & CC_p < 0.05  ~ "Both significant",
        MI_p < 0.05 & CC_p >= 0.05 ~ "Lost significance in CC",
        MI_p >= 0.05 & CC_p < 0.05 ~ "Gained significance in CC",
        TRUE                        ~ "Both non-significant"
      )
    ) %>%
    select(term, MI_OR_CI, MI_sig, CC_OR_CI, CC_sig,
           OR_change_pct, Direction, Sig_consistency)
}

comp_A <- build_comparison(cc_A, mi_A)

cat("COEFFICIENT COMPARISON:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
print(comp_A, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")

auc_diff_A <- round(auc_A_cc$auc - mi_auc_A$AUC, 3)

cat("AUC COMPARISON:\n")
cat(sprintf("  MI AUC:         %s\n",   mi_auc_A$formatted))
cat(sprintf("  CC AUC:         %s\n",   auc_A_cc$formatted))
cat(sprintf("  AUC difference: %+.3f  (CC − MI)\n\n", auc_diff_A))

mean_or_change_A <- mean(abs(comp_A$OR_change_pct), na.rm = TRUE)
n_consistent_A   <- sum(comp_A$Sig_consistency %in%
                          c("Both significant", "Both non-significant"))

cat("SUMMARY:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean |OR change|:  %.1f%%\n", mean_or_change_A))
cat(sprintf("  Consistent terms: %d / %d\n", n_consistent_A, nrow(comp_A)))
cat(sprintf("  AUC difference:   %+.3f\n\n", auc_diff_A))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART K: CC vs MI COMPARISON — MODEL B
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART K: CC vs MI COMPARISON — MODEL B\n")
cat(sprintf("(%s)\n", paste(vars_B, collapse = ", ")))
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

comp_B <- build_comparison(cc_B, mi_B)

cat("COEFFICIENT COMPARISON:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
print(comp_B, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")

auc_diff_B <- round(auc_B_cc$auc - mi_auc_B$AUC, 3)

cat("AUC COMPARISON:\n")
cat(sprintf("  MI AUC:         %s\n",   mi_auc_B$formatted))
cat(sprintf("  CC AUC:         %s\n",   auc_B_cc$formatted))
cat(sprintf("  AUC difference: %+.3f  (CC − MI)\n\n", auc_diff_B))

mean_or_change_B <- mean(abs(comp_B$OR_change_pct), na.rm = TRUE)
n_consistent_B   <- sum(comp_B$Sig_consistency %in%
                          c("Both significant", "Both non-significant"))

cat("SUMMARY:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean |OR change|:  %.1f%%\n", mean_or_change_B))
cat(sprintf("  Consistent terms: %d / %d\n", n_consistent_B, nrow(comp_B)))
cat(sprintf("  AUC difference:   %+.3f\n\n", auc_diff_B))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART L: COMBINED AUC SUMMARY TABLE
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART L: Combined AUC summary\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

auc_table <- data.frame(
  Model       = c("Model A", "Model B"),
  Description = c("Personality interpretation set", "Full interpretation set"),
  N_MI        = chunk12$metadata$N,
  N_CC        = n_cc,
  MI_AUC      = c(mi_auc_A$formatted, mi_auc_B$formatted),
  CC_AUC      = c(auc_A_cc$formatted, auc_B_cc$formatted),
  AUC_Diff    = c(auc_diff_A, auc_diff_B),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

print(auc_table, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART M: PUBLICATION-READY TABLE
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART M: Publication-ready comparison table\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

pub_A <- comp_A %>%
  mutate(Model = "Model A — Personality") %>%
  select(Model, Predictor = term,
         `MI OR [95% CI]` = MI_OR_CI, `MI p`  = MI_sig,
         `CC OR [95% CI]` = CC_OR_CI, `CC p`  = CC_sig,
         `OR Δ (%)`       = OR_change_pct,
         Consistency      = Sig_consistency)

pub_B <- comp_B %>%
  mutate(Model = "Model B — Full") %>%
  select(Model, Predictor = term,
         `MI OR [95% CI]` = MI_OR_CI, `MI p`  = MI_sig,
         `CC OR [95% CI]` = CC_OR_CI, `CC p`  = CC_sig,
         `OR Δ (%)`       = OR_change_pct,
         Consistency      = Sig_consistency)

pub_combined <- bind_rows(pub_A, pub_B)

print(pub_combined, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n")

cat("FOOTNOTE:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat(sprintf(
  "MI = Multiple imputation (N = %d, m = %d). CC = Complete cases (N = %d, %.1f%% of full sample).\n",
  n_total, m_imps, n_cc, 100 * n_cc / n_total
))
cat("Complete cases defined as non-missing on all model predictors and outcome (union of Models A and B).\n")
cat("All variables reconstructed from raw items in SIMOA_analysis.rds.\n")
cat("stopped_bzra derived from freq_dich (0/1 = stopper, 2 = continuer), matching Chunk 9.\n")
cat("capabilityC and side_effectsC centred using complete-case column mean\n")
cat("(minor deviation from Chunk 9, which centred on full imputed sample mean).\n")
cat("OR = odds ratio. CI = 95% confidence interval. OR Δ = 100 × (CC OR − MI OR) / MI OR.\n")
cat("AUC bootstrapped (B = 2000). *p<.05 **p<.01 ***p<.001.\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART N: INTERPRETATION
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART N: Interpretation\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

robust_A <- mean_or_change_A < 10 && n_consistent_A == nrow(comp_A)
robust_B <- mean_or_change_B < 10 && n_consistent_B == nrow(comp_B)

for (label in c("A", "B")) {
  comp     <- if (label == "A") comp_A     else comp_B
  auc_cc   <- if (label == "A") auc_A_cc   else auc_B_cc
  auc_mi   <- if (label == "A") mi_auc_A   else mi_auc_B
  auc_diff <- if (label == "A") auc_diff_A else auc_diff_B
  n_cons   <- if (label == "A") n_consistent_A else n_consistent_B
  mean_chg <- if (label == "A") mean_or_change_A else mean_or_change_B
  robust   <- if (label == "A") robust_A   else robust_B

  cat(sprintf("MODEL %s:\n", label))
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  cat(sprintf("  MI AUC:               %s\n", auc_mi$formatted))
  cat(sprintf("  CC AUC:               %s\n", auc_cc$formatted))
  cat(sprintf("  AUC difference:       %+.3f\n", auc_diff))
  cat(sprintf("  Mean |OR change|:     %.1f%%\n", mean_chg))
  cat(sprintf("  Consistent terms:     %d / %d\n\n", n_cons, nrow(comp)))

  if (robust) {
    cat("  CONCLUSION: MI RESULTS ROBUST\n")
    cat("  CC estimates are consistent with MI. Imputation did not\n")
    cat("  meaningfully alter findings.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("  CONCLUSION: NOTABLE DIFFERENCES BETWEEN MI AND CC\n")
    cat("  CC estimates differ from MI, which may reflect bias from\n")
    cat("  non-random missingness. MI estimates are preferred given\n")
    cat("  MAR assumption is supported.\n\n")
  }
}

cat("SUGGESTED MANUSCRIPT TEXT:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat(sprintf(
'"As a sensitivity analysis, both models were refit using complete
cases only (n = %d, %.1f%% of the full sample of %d). Complete cases
were defined as non-missing on all predictors across both models and
the outcome (union of Models A and B). All variables were reconstructed
from raw items; COM-B composites (capabilityC, side_effectsC) were
re-centred on the complete-case mean. For Model A (%d predictors),
odds ratios changed by a mean of %.1f%% relative to MI estimates
(AUC CC = %s vs. MI AUC = %s). For Model B (%d predictors),
odds ratios changed by a mean of %.1f%% (AUC CC = %s vs.
MI AUC = %s). Significance classifications were consistent across
all predictors in both models, supporting the robustness of the
primary MI results (m = %d imputations)."\n\n',
  n_cc, 100 * n_cc / n_total, n_total,
  length(vars_A), mean_or_change_A, auc_A_cc$formatted, mi_auc_A$formatted,
  length(vars_B), mean_or_change_B, auc_B_cc$formatted, mi_auc_B$formatted,
  m_imps
))

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART O: SAVE RESULTS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("PART O: Saving results\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cc_output <- list(
  n_total     = n_total,
  n_cc        = n_cc,
  n_excluded  = n_excluded,
  n_events_cc = n_events,
  model_A = list(model = model_A_cc, cc_results = cc_A,
                 auc = auc_A_cc, comparison = comp_A),
  model_B = list(model = model_B_cc, cc_results = cc_B,
                 auc = auc_B_cc, comparison = comp_B),
  auc_table = auc_table,
  pub_table = pub_combined,
  metadata  = list(
    outcome = outcome_var, vars_A = vars_A, vars_B = vars_B,
    m = m_imps, N_total = n_total, N_cc = n_cc
  )
)

saveRDS(cc_output,      "complete_case_sensitivity_results.rds")
write_csv(pub_combined, "CC_vs_MI_comparison_table.csv")
write_csv(auc_table,    "CC_vs_MI_AUC_comparison.csv")
write_csv(comp_A,       "CC_vs_MI_modelA_coefficients.csv")
write_csv(comp_B,       "CC_vs_MI_modelB_coefficients.csv")

cat("✓ Saved: complete_case_sensitivity_results.rds\n")
cat("✓ Saved: CC_vs_MI_comparison_table.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: CC_vs_MI_AUC_comparison.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: CC_vs_MI_modelA_coefficients.csv\n")
cat("✓ Saved: CC_vs_MI_modelB_coefficients.csv\n\n")

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUMMARY
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 15 COMPLETE\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat(sprintf("Complete cases: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n\n",
            n_cc, n_total, 100 * n_cc / n_total))

for (label in c("A", "B")) {
  comp     <- if (label == "A") comp_A     else comp_B
  auc_cc   <- if (label == "A") auc_A_cc   else auc_B_cc
  auc_mi   <- if (label == "A") mi_auc_A   else mi_auc_B
  auc_diff <- if (label == "A") auc_diff_A else auc_diff_B
  mean_chg <- if (label == "A") mean_or_change_A else mean_or_change_B
  robust   <- if (label == "A") robust_A   else robust_B

  cat(sprintf("Model %s:\n", label))
  cat(sprintf("  MI AUC: %s   CC AUC: %s   Diff: %+.3f\n",
              auc_mi$formatted, auc_cc$formatted, auc_diff))
  cat(sprintf("  Mean |OR change|: %.1f%%\n", mean_chg))
  cat(sprintf("  Verdict: %s\n\n",
              if (robust) "✓ ROBUST" else "⚠️  NOTABLE DIFFERENCES"))
}

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("  1. complete_case_sensitivity_results.rds\n")
cat("  2. CC_vs_MI_comparison_table.csv\n")
cat("  3. CC_vs_MI_AUC_comparison.csv\n")
cat("  4. CC_vs_MI_modelA_coefficients.csv\n")
cat("  5. CC_vs_MI_modelB_coefficients.csv\n\n")

cat("NOTES:\n")
cat(sprintf("  - m = %d imputations throughout\n", m_imps))
cat("  - Complete cases = non-missing on union of all model vars + outcome\n")
cat("  - ALL variables built from raw items in SIMOA_analysis.rds\n")
cat("  - stopped_bzra derived from freq_dich (0/1=stopper, 2=continuer)\n")
cat("    matching Chunk 9 logic exactly\n")
cat("  - capabilityC: mean(capability_1) centred on CC column mean\n")
cat("  - side_effectsC: mean(side_effects_1:4) centred on CC column mean\n")
cat("  - Minor deviation: Chunk 9 centred on full imputed sample mean\n")
cat("  - stop_wdl_exp_combined: unused levels dropped after CC subsetting\n")
cat("  - Factor structure preserved; separation checked automatically\n")
cat("  - VIF uses GVIF for factor predictors (car::vif)\n")
cat("  - AUC bootstrapped (B=2000, seed=12345)\n")
cat("  - Robustness threshold: mean |OR change| < 10% AND all terms\n")
cat("    consistent in significance direction\n\n")

cat("REFERENCES:\n")
cat("  Rubin DB (1987). Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys.\n")
cat("  White IR et al. (2011). Stat Med, 30(4), 377-399.\n\n")

cat("✓ Ready for Chunk 16\n\n")
```


## Correlation Matrix
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# CHUNK 16: COMPLETE CASE MIXED-MEASURE ASSOCIATION MATRIX
# ==============================================================================
# Purpose:
#   Produce a pairwise association matrix on the complete case sample defined
#   in Chunk 15, using the statistically appropriate measure for each variable-
#   pair type. Variables are constructed subscales/composites and selected
#   health/attitudinal single-item variables — NO raw items.
#
# Variable set:
#   1. Model vars      — vars_A + vars_B + outcome (from cc_output)
#   2. DBAS subscales  — Consequences, Worry_Helplessness, Expectations,
#                        Medications, Total
#   3. CISS subscales  — Task, Emotion, Avoidance
#   4. BFI subscales   — Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness,
#                        Neuroticism, Openness
#   5. SURPS subscales — Hopelessness, Impulsivity, Sensation_Seeking,
#                        Anxiety_Sensitivity
#   6. COM-B composites — capabilityC, side_effectsC, benefitsC, adlsC,
#                         dependenceC, motivationC
#   7. Health/attitudinal single items — gen_health, osss_3_score, phq2_score,
#      c_lengthofuse, bzra_daily_diazepam_eq, n_sleep_conditions,
#      wdl_symptom_count, stop_wdl_exp_combined, med_quant, phys_health_con,
#      ment_health_con, mobil_aid, fall, intent
#
#   All sourced from raw_data (modified in-place by Chunk 15) filtered to the
#   same complete-case rows as Chunk 15.
#
# Association measures:
#
#   Pair type                               Measure        Symbol  Range
#   ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   Continuous/ordinal × continuous/ord     Spearman ρ     ρ       −1 to +1
#   Binary nominal    × continuous/ord      Rank-biserial  r       −1 to +1
#   Binary nominal    × binary nominal      Phi            φ       −1 to +1
#   Multi-cat nominal × continuous/ord      Eta squared    η       0 to +1  *
#   Multi-cat nominal × nominal (any)       Cramér's V     V       0 to +1  *
#
#   * Unsigned: direction cannot be summarised as a single value for
#     multi-category nominal variables. Group descriptives recommended
#     as a supplement where direction is of interest.
#
# Colour scales:
#   Signed   (ρ, r, φ) — diverging  red–white–blue  (−1 to +1)
#   Unsigned (η, V)    — sequential white–purple     (0 to +1)
#
# Cell labels: value + symbol  e.g. ".45ρ"  ".08η"  ".31V"
# Axis labels: model vars highlighted in red; HTML tick labels append ★
#
# Requires:
#   Chunk 15 run in the same session:
#     cc_data, cc_output, chunk12  in environment
#     raw_data (modified in-place by Chunk 15 with all subscales)
#
# Outputs:
#   1. cc_correlation_matrix.png            — full matrix, static hi-res
#   2. cc_correlation_matrix.pdf            — vector version
#   3. cc_correlation_matrix.html           — interactive, dual colour bars
#   4. cc_correlation_matrix_model_only.png — model vars only
#
# Methodological note (for thesis):
#   Constructed composites (mean of items) and single-item variables differ in
#   reliability. Composite–composite correlations are generally less attenuated
#   by measurement error than composite–single-item or single–single pairs.
#   This should be acknowledged in a footnote to the correlation table.
#
# References:
#   Cureton EE (1956). Rank-biserial correlation. Psychometrika, 21(3), 287-290.
#   Cohen J (1988). Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioral Sciences (2nd ed.).
#   Richardson JTE (2011). Eta squared. Practical Assessment, Research & Eval, 16(6).
# ==============================================================================

# ── Packages ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pkgs <- c("dplyr", "ggplot2", "plotly", "htmlwidgets")
for (p in pkgs)
  if (!p %in% rownames(installed.packages())) install.packages(p, quiet = TRUE)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(htmlwidgets)

if (!requireNamespace("ggnewscale", quietly = TRUE))
  install.packages("ggnewscale", quiet = TRUE)
has_ggnewscale <- requireNamespace("ggnewscale", quietly = TRUE)
if (has_ggnewscale) library(ggnewscale)

cat("\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CHUNK 16: COMPLETE CASE MIXED-MEASURE ASSOCIATION MATRIX\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

# ==============================================================================
# PART A: CHECK ENVIRONMENT
# ==============================================================================

cat("PART A: Checking environment\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

for (obj in c("cc_data", "cc_output", "chunk12", "raw_data"))
  if (!exists(obj))
    stop(sprintf("ERROR: '%s' not found. Run Chunk 15 first in this session.", obj))

outcome_var <- cc_output$metadata$outcome
vars_A      <- cc_output$metadata$vars_A
vars_B      <- cc_output$metadata$vars_B
model_vars  <- unique(c(outcome_var, vars_A, vars_B))

cat(sprintf("✓ cc_data   : %d rows × %d columns\n", nrow(cc_data), ncol(cc_data)))
cat(sprintf("✓ raw_data  : %d rows × %d columns\n", nrow(raw_data), ncol(raw_data)))
cat(sprintf("✓ outcome   : %s\n", outcome_var))
cat(sprintf("✓ Model A (%d): %s\n", length(vars_A), paste(vars_A, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("✓ Model B (%d): %s\n", length(vars_B), paste(vars_B, collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("✓ Union model vars (inc. outcome): %d\n\n", length(model_vars)))

# ==============================================================================
# PART B: DEFINE VARIABLE SET — SUBSCALES, COMPOSITES, SINGLE ITEMS
# ==============================================================================

cat("PART B: Defining variable set (subscales + composites + single items)\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

# ── Constructed subscales (built in Chunk 15 Parts C–D on raw_data) ────────────
dbas_subscales <- c(
  "DBAS_Consequences", "DBAS_Worry_Helplessness", "DBAS_Expectations",
  "DBAS_Medications", "DBAS_Total"
)

ciss_subscales <- c("CISS_Task", "CISS_Emotion", "CISS_Avoidance")

bfi_subscales <- c(
  "BFI_Extraversion", "BFI_Agreeableness", "BFI_Conscientiousness",
  "BFI_Neuroticism", "BFI_Openness"
)

surps_subscales <- c(
  "SURPS_Hopelessness", "SURPS_Impulsivity",
  "SURPS_Sensation_Seeking", "SURPS_Anxiety_Sensitivity"
)

comb_composites <- c(
  "capabilityC", "side_effectsC", "benefitsC",
  "adlsC", "dependenceC", "motivationC"
)

# ── Health / attitudinal single-item variables ─────────────────────────────────
# These are meaningful as standalone items and routinely appear in similar
# clinical studies. Raw Likert items (dbas_3, surps1, etc.) are NOT included.
health_single <- c(
  "gen_health",            # self-rated general health
  "osss_3_score",          # social support (3-item scale score)
  "phq2_score",            # PHQ-2 depression screen
  "phys_health_con",       # physical health condition count/flag
  "ment_health_con",       # mental health condition count/flag
  "mobil_aid",             # mobility aid use (binary)
  "fall",                  # fall in past year (binary)
  "med_quant",             # number of medications
  "c_lengthofuse",         # length of BZRA use (continuous)
  "bzra_daily_diazepam_eq",# BZRA dose equivalence (continuous)
  "n_sleep_conditions",    # number of sleep conditions
  "wdl_symptom_count",     # withdrawal symptom count
  "stop_wdl_exp_combined", # prior stopping/withdrawal experience (factor)
  "intent",                # intent to stop (binary/ordinal)
  "phq2_score"             # already listed above but harmless duplicate
)
health_single <- unique(health_single)

# ── Full variable list for matrix ─────────────────────────────────────────────
all_matrix_vars <- unique(c(
  outcome_var,
  model_vars,           # ensures model vars present even if not in lists below
  dbas_subscales,
  ciss_subscales,
  bfi_subscales,
  surps_subscales,
  comb_composites,
  health_single
))

cat(sprintf("Target variable count: %d\n\n", length(all_matrix_vars)))
cat("Variable groups:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Model vars (inc. outcome): %d\n", length(model_vars)))
cat(sprintf("  DBAS subscales:            %d\n", length(dbas_subscales)))
cat(sprintf("  CISS subscales:            %d\n", length(ciss_subscales)))
cat(sprintf("  BFI subscales:             %d\n", length(bfi_subscales)))
cat(sprintf("  SURPS subscales:           %d\n", length(surps_subscales)))
cat(sprintf("  COM-B composites:          %d\n", length(comb_composites)))
cat(sprintf("  Health/attitudinal items:  %d\n\n", length(health_single)))

# ==============================================================================
# PART C: BUILD COMPLETE-CASE WORKING DATASET
# ==============================================================================

cat("PART C: Building complete-case working dataset\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

# Ensure outcome is in raw_data (derived from freq_dich if absent)
if (!outcome_var %in% names(raw_data)) {
  if (!"freq_dich" %in% names(raw_data))
    stop("ERROR: outcome and freq_dich both absent from raw_data. Check Chunk 15.")
  raw_data[[outcome_var]] <- dplyr::case_when(
    raw_data$freq_dich %in% c(0, 1) ~ 1L,
    raw_data$freq_dich == 2          ~ 0L,
    TRUE                             ~ NA_integer_
  )
  cat("  ✓ Derived outcome from freq_dich\n")
}

# Identify CC rows using the same union-of-model-vars rule as Chunk 15
cc_union_vars   <- c(outcome_var, unique(c(vars_A, vars_B)))
cc_check_cols   <- cc_union_vars[cc_union_vars %in% names(raw_data)]
cc_rows         <- complete.cases(raw_data[, cc_check_cols, drop = FALSE])

cat(sprintf("  ✓ CC rows (Chunk 15 definition): %d / %d\n", sum(cc_rows), nrow(raw_data)))

if (sum(cc_rows) != nrow(cc_data))
  cat(sprintf("  ⚠ Warning: CC row count (%d) differs from cc_data (%d). Check Chunk 15.\n",
              sum(cc_rows), nrow(cc_data)))

# Subset raw_data to CC rows
work_raw <- raw_data[cc_rows, , drop = FALSE]

# Check which target vars are present in work_raw
present_vars <- all_matrix_vars[all_matrix_vars %in% names(work_raw)]
absent_vars  <- all_matrix_vars[!all_matrix_vars %in% names(work_raw)]

if (length(absent_vars) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n  ⚠ Variables absent from raw_data (%d) — will be skipped:\n",
              length(absent_vars)))
  for (v in absent_vars) cat(sprintf("      • %s\n", v))
  cat("\n")
} else {
  cat("  ✓ All target variables found in raw_data\n\n")
}

work_data <- work_raw %>% select(all_of(present_vars))

cat(sprintf("  ✓ Working dataset: %d rows × %d columns\n\n",
            nrow(work_data), ncol(work_data)))

# ==============================================================================
# PART D: CLASSIFY VARIABLE TYPES
# ==============================================================================

cat("PART D: Classifying variable types\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

classify_var <- function(x) {
  if (is.character(x))                   return("multi_nominal")
  if (is.factor(x) && !is.ordered(x)) {
    if (length(levels(x)) == 2)          return("binary_nominal")
    return("multi_nominal")
  }
  # Numeric/integer/ordered: check if effectively binary 0/1
  vals <- sort(unique(na.omit(as.numeric(x))))
  if (length(vals) == 2 && all(vals == c(0, 1))) return("binary_nominal")
  return("continuous")
}

var_types <- sapply(work_data, classify_var)
n_cont    <- sum(var_types == "continuous")
n_binom   <- sum(var_types == "binary_nominal")
n_multi   <- sum(var_types == "multi_nominal")

cat(sprintf("  Continuous / ordinal:     %d\n", n_cont))
cat(sprintf("  Binary nominal:           %d\n", n_binom))
cat(sprintf("  Multi-category nominal:   %d\n\n", n_multi))

if (n_multi > 0) {
  cat("  Multi-category nominal variables (η or V will be used):\n")
  for (v in names(var_types)[var_types == "multi_nominal"]) {
    ncat <- length(unique(na.omit(work_data[[v]])))
    cat(sprintf("    • %-35s [%d categories]\n", v, ncat))
  }
  cat("\n")
}

cat("  Variable type by name:\n")
for (v in names(var_types))
  cat(sprintf("    %-35s %s\n", v, var_types[v]))
cat("\n")

# ==============================================================================
# PART E: ASSOCIATION FUNCTIONS
# ==============================================================================

cat("PART E: Defining association functions\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")

# Returns list(value, symbol, signed)

# ── Spearman ρ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
assoc_spearman <- function(x, y) {
  cc <- complete.cases(x, y)
  if (sum(cc) < 5) return(list(value = NA_real_, symbol = "ρ", signed = TRUE))
  list(value  = cor(as.numeric(x[cc]), as.numeric(y[cc]), method = "spearman"),
       symbol = "ρ", signed = TRUE)
}

# ── Rank-biserial r  (binary × continuous) ────────────────────────────────────
# Direction: positive when group coded 1 ranks higher than group coded 0.
# Formula:   r = 1 − (2W) / (n0 * n1)  where W is the Wilcoxon statistic
#            for group1 vs group0 (one-sided direction: group1 higher).
assoc_rankbiserial <- function(bin_var, cont_var) {
  cc   <- complete.cases(bin_var, cont_var)
  b    <- as.integer(bin_var[cc])
  y    <- as.numeric(cont_var[cc])
  grp0 <- y[b == 0]; grp1 <- y[b == 1]
  n0   <- length(grp0); n1 <- length(grp1)
  if (n0 < 3 || n1 < 3)
    return(list(value = NA_real_, symbol = "r", signed = TRUE))
  W <- wilcox.test(grp1, grp0, exact = FALSE)$statistic
  r <- 1 - (2 * W) / (n0 * n1)
  list(value = as.numeric(r), symbol = "r", signed = TRUE)
}

# ── Phi φ  (binary × binary) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Phi = Pearson r on two 0/1 variables. Signed: positive = co-occurrence of
# 1s exceeds chance.
assoc_phi <- function(x, y) {
  cc <- complete.cases(x, y)
  xi <- as.integer(x[cc]); yi <- as.integer(y[cc])
  if (length(xi) < 5) return(list(value = NA_real_, symbol = "φ", signed = TRUE))
  list(value  = cor(xi, yi, method = "pearson"),
       symbol = "φ", signed = TRUE)
}

# ── Eta squared η  (multi-cat nominal × continuous) ───────────────────────────
# Kruskal-Wallis based η²: (H − k + 1) / (n − k)
# Unsigned: strength only, no direction. Direction requires group descriptives.
assoc_eta2 <- function(nom_var, cont_var) {
  cc  <- complete.cases(nom_var, cont_var)
  grp <- factor(nom_var[cc])
  y   <- as.numeric(cont_var[cc])
  k   <- length(levels(grp))
  n   <- length(y)
  if (k < 2 || n < 5)
    return(list(value = NA_real_, symbol = "η", signed = FALSE))
  H    <- kruskal.test(y ~ grp)$statistic
  eta2 <- max(0, (as.numeric(H) - k + 1) / (n - k))
  list(value = eta2, symbol = "η", signed = FALSE)
}

# ── Cramér's V  (nominal × nominal) ──────────────────────────────────────────
# V = sqrt(χ² / (n × (min(r,c) − 1))). Unsigned, 0 to 1.
assoc_cramerv <- function(x, y) {
  cc <- complete.cases(x, y)
  xi <- factor(x[cc]); yi <- factor(y[cc])
  if (length(levels(xi)) < 2 || length(levels(yi)) < 2 || sum(cc) < 5)
    return(list(value = NA_real_, symbol = "V", signed = FALSE))
  tbl  <- table(xi, yi)
  chi2 <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tbl, correct = FALSE)$statistic)
  n    <- sum(tbl)
  k    <- min(nrow(tbl), ncol(tbl))
  V    <- sqrt(as.numeric(chi2) / (n * (k - 1)))
  list(value = min(V, 1), symbol = "V", signed = FALSE)  # cap at 1 for safety
}

# ── Dispatcher ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pair_assoc <- function(v1, v2, data, types) {
  t1 <- types[v1]; t2 <- types[v2]
  x  <- data[[v1]]; y  <- data[[v2]]

  # Continuous × continuous
  if (t1 == "continuous"     && t2 == "continuous")      return(assoc_spearman(x, y))
  # Binary × continuous (either order)
  if (t1 == "binary_nominal" && t2 == "continuous")      return(assoc_rankbiserial(x, y))
  if (t1 == "continuous"     && t2 == "binary_nominal")  return(assoc_rankbiserial(y, x))
  # Binary × binary
  if (t1 == "binary_nominal" && t2 == "binary_nominal")  return(assoc_phi(x, y))
  # Multi-cat × continuous (either order)
  if (t1 == "multi_nominal"  && t2 == "continuous")      return(assoc_eta2(x, y))
  if (t1 == "continuous"     && t2 == "multi_nominal")   return(assoc_eta2(y, x))
  # Multi-cat × binary (either order) → Cramér's V
  if (t1 == "multi_nominal"  && t2 == "binary_nominal")  return(assoc_cramerv(x, y))
  if (t1 == "binary_nominal" && t2 == "multi_nominal")   return(assoc_cramerv(y, x))
  # Multi-cat × multi-cat
```
